words.e: Vegetables are full of important nutrients.
words.zh: (名) 營養物質；養分

words.d: To overcome a problem is to successfully fix it.
words.e: She overcame her shyness and spoke in front of the class.
words.zh: (動) 克服；戰勝

words.d: To recall something is to remember it.
words.e: She was trying to recall what she had told her friend.
words.zh: (動) 回憶；召回

words.d: To regret something is to wish that it didn’t happen.
words.e: I regret that I was mean to my sister.
words.zh: (動) 後悔；遺憾

words.d: A soul is a person’s spirit.
words.e: Some people believe that the soul lives after the body dies.
words.zh: (名) 靈魂；精神

words.d: When something is sufficient, you have enough of it.
words.e: After eating a sufficient amount of food, I left the table.
words.zh: (形) 足夠的；充分的

words.d: Surgery is medical treatment when the doctor cuts open your body.
words.e: I needed surgery to repair my leg after the accident.
words.zh: (名) 手術；外科

words.d: If something is tough, it is difficult.
words.e: The man passed his driving test even though it was very tough.
words.zh: (形) 困難的；堅韌的

words.d: A tube is a pipe through which water or air passes.
words.e: The pile of tubes was going to be put in the ground.
words.zh: (名) 管子；顯像管；捷運

words.d: To admit something means to say that it is true.
words.e: I had to admit that I stole his idea.
words.zh: (動) 承認；坦白　(動) 允許進入；放行

words.d: A bin is a container that holds things.
words.e: He put his trash in the bin.
words.zh: (名) 垃圾桶；廢棄物桶　(動) 將...放入垃圾桶；丟棄

words.d: A bowl is a deep, round dish that holds food or liquid.
words.e: I ate a bowl of cereal for breakfast.
words.zh: (名) 碗；盤　(動) 滾球；投球

words.d: A cabin is a small house made of wood.
words.e: My grandfather owns a small cabin in the country.
words.zh: (名) 小屋；船艙

words.d: Cash is money in the form of paper or coins.
words.e: I was able to save up enough cash to buy my mother a gift.
words.zh: (名) 現金；現款　(動) 兌現；支取現金

words.d: A criminal is a person who does something against the law.
words.e: The police made sure the criminal couldn’t move his hands.
words.zh: (形) 犯罪的；刑事的　(名) 罪犯；犯人

words.d: A dozen is a group of twelve things.
words.e: Mom brought home a dozen donuts for us as a treat.
words.zh: (名) 一打（12個）；許多

words.d: An elder is a person who is older than the people around them.
words.e: Uncle Ray is my elder so I always try to be respectful.
words.zh: (名) 年長者；長輩　(形) 年長的；較年長的

words.d: If something is facial, it is related to a person’s face.
words.e: I could tell from his facial features that he was angry.
words.zh: (形) 臉部的；面部的　(名) 臉部護理；美容療程

words.d: A fence is a structure around a house or field.
words.e: The farmer put up a fence so that no one could walk on her field.
words.zh: (名) 柵欄；圍籬　(動) 用柵欄圍住；限制

words.d: To inspire means to make a person want to do something.
words.e: I was inspired to write a poem after watching the sunrise.
words.zh: (動) 激勵；鼓舞

words.d: When something is mere, it is small or not important.
words.e: We lost the game by a mere two points.
words.zh: (形) 僅僅的；只不過的　(名) 湖；池塘

words.d: If something is neat, it is very clean or organized well.
words.e: My sister always keeps her bedroom very neat.
words.zh: (形) 整潔的；乾淨的　(形) 巧妙的；靈巧的

words.d: An occasion is a time when something important happens.
words.e: Her graduation was an occasion to have a good time.
words.zh: (名) 場合；時刻　(名) 機會；原因　(動) 引起；造成

words.d: A penalty is a punishment, given when someone breaks a rule or law.
words.e: I had to pay a $100 penalty for parking in the wrong spot.
words.zh: (名) 處罰；罰金

words.d: When someone is rude, they are mean and not polite.
words.e: The children at the bus stop were very rude.
words.zh: (形) 無禮的；粗魯的

words.d: To settle a problem means to end it by finding a solution.
words.e: It took three of our best executives all day to settle the problem.
words.zh: (動) 解決；安頓

words.d: A vehicle is a thing that moves people or things to another place.
words.e: A ferry is a vehicle that takes people from one island to another.
words.zh: (名) 車輛；交通工具

words.d: A wallet is a thing that holds money and fits in a pocket.
words.e: I keep my money in a wallet.
words.zh: (名) 錢包；皮夾

words.d: To yell is to say something very loudly.
words.e: The coach yelled at his team for their poor performance.
words.zh: (動) 大喊；叫喊

words.d: To accuse someone of something is to blame them for doing it.
words.e: She accused her brother of breaking her computer.
words.zh: (動) 指控；控告　非難；譴責

words.d: To adjust something means to change it so it is better.
words.e: He adjusted the old guitar to make it sound better.
words.zh: (動) 調整；適應　(動) 調節；改變

words.d: To amuse someone means to do something that is funny or entertaining.
words.e: The singer was very good. She amused the crowd.
words.zh: (動) 逗樂；使高興　(動) 消遣；娛樂

words.d: Coral is the hard, colorful material formed by the shells of animals.
words.e: The diver admired the beautiful coral under the water.
words.zh: (名) 珊瑚　(形) 珊瑚色的；珊瑚製的

words.d: Cotton is a cloth made from the fibers of the cotton plant.
words.e: I like to wear clothes made from cotton in the summer.
words.zh: (名) 棉花；棉布　(形) 棉製的；棉質的

words.d: To crash means to hit and break something.
words.e: There was a loud noise when the car crashed into the tree.
words.zh: (動) 撞擊；碰撞　(名) 撞車；碰撞聲

words.d: A deck is a wooden floor built outside of a house or the floor of a ship.
words.e: A ship will store many supplies below its deck.
words.zh: (名) 甲板；層板　(動) 裝飾；整理

words.d: To engage in something means to do it.
words.e: Dad was engaged in sawing a piece of wood in half.
words.zh: (動) 吸引；使參與　(動) 從事；參加

words.d: When something is firm, it is solid but not too hard.
words.e: He sleeps better on a firm bed.
words.zh: (形) 堅固的；結實的　(動) 使堅固；成立

words.d: Fuel is something that creates heat or energy.
words.e: Heat is the fuel that comes from fire.
words.zh: (名) 燃料；汽油　(動) 供給燃料；激起

words.d: When something is grand, it is big and liked by people.
words.e: The grand mountain rose high into the sky.
words.zh: (形) 宏偉的；壯麗的　(形) 重要的；盛大的

words.d: A hurricane is a bad storm that happens over the ocean.
words.e: The wind from the hurricane bent the palm tree.
words.zh: (名) 颶風；強烈風暴

words.d: A loss means the act or an instance of losing something.
words.e: I suffered a big loss while I was gambling.
words.zh: (名) 損失；虧損　(名) 失去；喪失

words.d: If something is simple, it is plain and not decorated.
words.e: He bought a pair of plain white shoes over the weekend.
words.zh: (形) 樸素的；清楚的

words.d: A reef is a group of rocks or coral that rise to or near the ocean.
words.e: He walked along the reef and looked at the water below.
words.zh: (名) 礁；暗礁

words.d: To shut something means to close it tightly.
words.e: Please shut the door; the air outside is cold.
words.zh: (動) 關閉；關上

words.d: When someone is strict, they make sure others follow rules.
words.e: The teacher is strict. She does not let students talk in class.
words.zh: (形) 嚴格的；嚴厲的

words.d: To surf means to use a special board to ride on waves in the ocean.
words.e: The students went to the beach to surf during their vacation.
words.zh: (動) 衝浪；上網瀏覽

words.d: A task is a piece of work to be done that is usually difficult.
words.e: My task for the weekend was to clean the entire back yard.
words.zh: (名) 任務；工作

words.d: A zone is an area that has different qualities from the ones around it.
words.e: Firefighters often work in danger zones.
words.zh: (名) 區域；地帶

words.d: An apology is something someone says to show that they are sorry.
words.e: After arguing with her teacher, the girl wrote the teacher an apology.
words.zh: (名) 道歉；為...表示歉意　(動) 為...道歉；向...認錯

words.d: If someone is bold, they are not afraid of doing something.
words.e: The bold man climbed the high mountain.
words.zh: (形) 勇敢的；大膽的　(動) 大膽嘗試；冒險

words.d: To capture someone or something is to catch them.
words.e: James tried to capture the bubbles in his hands.
words.zh: (動) 捕捉；抓住　(動) 拍攝；記錄　(名) 俘虜；捕獲

words.d: If a rule or quality is cardinal, then it is the most important one.
words.e: Raising your hand in the classroom before you speak is a cardinal rule.
words.zh: (形) 最重要的；基本的　(名) 樞機主教；紅衣主教

words.d: A duke is a man of high social rank but below a king or queen.
words.e: The duke ruled over the land.
words.zh: (名) 公爵；貴族　(名) 拳擊中的揮拳動作

words.d: To expose is to make known something that is hidden.
words.e: He took off his shirt exposing his costume.
words.zh: (動) 揭露；曝光　(動) 使暴露；使接觸

words.d: If people feel guilty, they feel bad for what they did.
words.e: I felt guilty for taking my sister’s cookies.
words.zh: (形) 有罪的；內疚的　(形) 犯過錯的；感到懊悔的

words.d: To hire someone is to pay them money to work for you.
words.e: We hired a man to paint our house.
words.zh: (動) 雇用；聘用　(名) 租賃；出租

words.d: If someone is innocent, they are not guilty of a crime.
words.e: The judge said that the woman was innocent of the crime.
words.zh: (形) 無罪的；清白的　(形) 天真的；無知的

words.d: Jail is a place where criminals go to be punished.
words.e: The thief was caught and sent to jail for ten years.
words.zh: (名) 監獄；牢房　(動) 監禁；關進監獄

words.d: A minister is an important person in government with many duties.
words.e: The minister of education controls the country’s schools.
words.zh: (名) 部長；部會首長　(動) 提供幫助；照顧

words.d: If someone or something is ordinary, they are not special in any way.
words.e: Today was just an ordinary day. Nothing unusual happened.
words.zh: (形) 普通的；平凡的　(形) 日常的；常見的

words.d: If something is permanent, it lasts for a long time or forever.
words.e: We don’t know if Aunt Mildred’s visit will be a permanent one.
words.zh: (形) 永久的；固定的

words.d: To preserve is to protect something from harm.
words.e: Dad sprayed a chemical on the house to help preserve its looks.
words.zh: (動) 保存；保護

words.d: To pronounce is to say the sounds of letters or words.
words.e: Young children often have trouble pronouncing words right.
words.zh: (動) 發音；宣稱

words.d: To resemble someone is to look like them.
words.e: The baby resembles his father a great deal.
words.zh: (動) 類似；像

words.d: A symptom of a bad condition or illness is a sign that it is happening.
words.e: Sneezing and a high fever are symptoms of the common cold.
words.zh: (名) 症狀；徵兆

words.d: Tobacco is a plant whose leaves are smoked, such as in cigarettes.
words.e: The tobacco in cigarettes is bad for your health.
words.zh: (名) 菸草；菸草製品

words.d: Twins are two children born at the same time.
words.e: My sister and I are twins. We look exactly the same.
words.zh: (名) 雙胞胎之一　(形) 雙胞胎的；成對的

words.d: A witch is a woman with magical powers.
words.e: People think that witches fly around on broomsticks.
words.zh: (名) 女巫；巫婆

words.d: To accompany other people means to join them or go with them.
words.e: My brothers accompanied me to the movie.
words.zh: (動) 陪同；伴隨

words.d: When something is bare, it is plain. It has no covering.
words.e: He likes to walk around in his bare feet.
words.zh: (形) 赤裸的；光禿的　(動) 露出；暴露

words.d: A branch is the part of a tree with leaves.
words.e: The monkey was hanging from a branch on the tree.
words.zh: (名) 樹枝；分支　(動) 分開；分岔

words.d: A breath is the air that goes into and out of one’s lungs.
words.e: You can’t take a breath under water.
words.zh: (名) 呼吸；氣息　(動) 呼吸；吐氣

words.d: A bridge is something that is built over a river so people can cross it.
words.e: The old bridge fell into the river.
words.zh: (名) 橋；橋樑　(動) 架橋；連接

words.d: To cast something means to throw it.
words.e: The fisherman cast his line into the water.
words.zh: (動) 投擲；拋　(動) 選舉；投票　(名) 演員陣容；角色安排

words.d: To dare means to be brave enough to try something.
words.e: He dared to jump out of the airplane and skydive.
words.zh: (動) 敢；膽敢　(名) 挑戰；賭注

words.d: When something is electronic, it uses electricity to do something.
words.e: I like having electronic devices such as an MP3 player.
words.zh: (形) 電子的；電子設備的　(名) 電子產品；電子設備

words.d: An inn is a place where travelers can rest and eat.
words.e: The visitor got a room at the inn.
words.zh: (名) 旅館；小旅社　(名) 酒吧；客棧

words.d: A net is a bag made of strong thread. It is used to catch animals.
words.e: The boy caught butterflies in his net.
words.zh: (名) 網；網子　(動) 用網捕捉；獲得淨利　(形) 淨的；純的

words.d: A philosophy is a way to think about truth and life.
words.e: My philosophy is “live and let live. ”
words.zh: (名) 哲學；人生觀

words.d: A pot is a deep, round metal container used for cooking.
words.e: Don’t touch the pot on the stove. It’s hot.
words.zh: (名) 鍋子；壺

words.d: A seed is the hard part of a plant or fruit that trees grow from.
words.e: I planted the seed in the dirt hoping that it would grow into a tree.
words.zh: (名) 種子；(動) 播種

words.d: When something is sharp, it has a thin edge that cuts things easily.
words.e: That knife is very sharp. Be careful not to hurt yourself.
words.zh: (形) 銳利的；鋒利的　(副) 準時地；突然地

words.d: A sort of something is a type of it.
words.e: What sort of instrument do you want to learn to play?
words.zh: (動) 分類；整理　(名) 種類；類型

words.d: To subtract means to take something away.
words.e: We learned how to subtract numbers from each other in class.
words.zh: (動) 減去；扣除

words.d: When something is tight, it is fixed or fastened firmly in place.
words.e: The knots were too tight to untie.
words.zh: (形) 緊的；緊湊的

words.d: If something is virtual, then it is very close to being true or accurate.
words.e: Because he’s popular, Joe is the virtual leader of the group.
words.zh: (形) 虛擬的；實際上的

words.d: To weigh something means to see how heavy it is.
words.e: The little dog weighed exactly 3kgs.
words.zh: (動) 稱重；重達

words.d: To whisper means to say very quietly.
words.e: We have to whisper in the library so people can focus on reading.
words.zh: (動) 低聲說話；耳語　(名) 低語；耳語

words.d: If ideas are abstract, they are based on general ways of thinking.
words.e: The idea of beauty is abstract and changes over time.
words.zh: (形) ['æbstrækt] 抽象的；深奧的　(名) ['æbstrækt] 摘要；概要　(動) [æb'strækt] 摘錄；提取

words.d: If something is annual, it happens once a year.
words.e: The only time I see my aunts and uncles is at our annual family picnic.
words.zh: (形) 每年的；年度的　(名) 年刊；年鑑

words.d: Clay is a type of heavy, wet soil used to make pots.
words.e: She made a bowl out of the clay.
words.zh: (名) 黏土；陶土　(動) 用黏土塑造

words.d: Cloth is material used to make clothes.
words.e: His shirt is made of a very soft type of cloth.
words.zh: (名) 布；布料　(名) 抹布；布巾

words.d: A curtain is a cloth hung over a window or used to divide a room.
words.e: She opened the curtains to let light into the room.
words.zh: (名) 窗簾；布幕　(動) 用窗簾遮住；隔開

words.d: To deserve is to be worthy of something as a result of one’s actions.
words.e: The dog deserved a bone for behaving very well.
words.zh: (動) 應該得到；值得　(動) 應得；應受

words.d: Feathers are the things covering birds’ bodies.
words.e: That bird has orange feathers on its chest.
words.zh: (名) 羽毛；羽毛狀物　(動) 用羽毛裝飾；輕輕放下

words.d: If land is fertile, it is able to produce good crops and plants.
words.e: The farmer grew many vegetables in the fertile soil.
words.zh: (形) 肥沃的；適合種植的　(形) 多產的；富有成果的

words.d: A flood is an event in which water covers an area which is usually dry.
words.e: After three days of rain, there was a flood in the city.
words.zh: (名) 洪水；大量　(動) 淹沒；湧入　(形) 泛濫的；溢出的

words.d: Furniture is the things used in a house such as tables and chairs.
words.e: His living room only had a few simple pieces of furniture.
words.zh: (名) 家具；傢俱　(名) 房間或建築物內的可動物品

words.d: A grave is the place where a dead person is buried.
words.e: We visit our grandfather’s grave each year.
words.zh: (形) 嚴肅的；莊嚴的　(名) 墳墓；墓地　(動) 雕刻；銘刻

words.d: If something is ideal, it is the best that it can possibly be.
words.e: This house is an ideal place for my family. It has everything we need.
words.zh: (形) 理想的；完美的　(名) 理想；標準

words.d: Intelligence is the ability to learn and understand things.
words.e: Because of his high intelligence, he finished school early.
words.zh: (名) 智力；聰慧　(名) 情報；資訊

words.d: If something happens nowadays, it happens at the present time.
words.e: In the past people walked everywhere. Nowadays, they use cars.
words.zh: (副) 現在；如今　(名) 現代；當今

words.d: To obtain is to get something you want or need.
words.e: After I passed the test, I obtained my driver’s license.
words.zh: (動) 獲得；取得　(動) 達成；實現

words.d: When something is religious, it has to do with religion.
words.e: The holy man spoke about religious topics.
words.zh: (形) 宗教的；虔誠的

words.d: When something is romantic, it has to do with love.
words.e: The young couple went to see a romantic movie.
words.zh: (形) 浪漫的；多情的

words.d: A shell is a hard covering that protects the body of some sea creatures.
words.e: There were many pretty shells on the beach.
words.zh: (名) 殼；貝殼　(動) 剝殼；去殼

words.d: A shore is the edge of a large body of water.
words.e: All of the boats were floating near the shore.
words.zh: (名) 岸；海濱

words.d: A wheel is a round thing on a vehicle that turns when it moves.
words.e: A car has four wheels.
words.zh: (名) 輪子；方向盤

words.d: To appeal to someone is to be interesting or attractive to them.
words.e: Sleeping all day appeals to me, but I have to go to school.
words.zh: (名) 上訴；呼籲　(動) 吸引；打動；上訴

words.d: To assume something is to think that it is true, even with no proof.
words.e: I assume you are both familiar with this plan.
words.zh: (動) 假設；假定　(動) 承擔；擔任

words.d: To borrow something is to take it and then give it back later.
words.e: Can I borrow a pencil to use today? I’ll give it back to you tomorrow.
words.zh: (動) 借用；借入　(動) 向…借錢

words.d: A client is a person or business that pays another to do a service.
words.e: She has many clients who enjoy coming to her salon.
words.zh: (名) 客戶；委託人　(名) 患者；顧客

words.d: The downtown is the center of most cities.
words.e: The downtown is filled with many tall buildings.
words.zh: (名) 市中心；商業區　(形) 往市中心的；市中心的

words.d: If something is dull, it is not exciting.
words.e: The movie was very dull. I fell asleep watching it.
words.zh: (形) 遲鈍的；無聊的　(動) 使變遲鈍；使變無聊

words.d: To embarrass someone is to make them feel ashamed or foolish.
words.e: He was embarrassed when he couldn’t remember her name.
words.zh: (動) 使尷尬；使不安　(動) 阻礙；妨礙

words.d: A fare is an amount of money paid to use a bus, train, or taxi.
words.e: Since he is a senior, my grandfather pays a low fare for the bus.
words.zh: (名) 車費；票價　(名) 食物；飯菜　(動) 進展；過活

words.d: Former describes something that used to be but is not any more.
words.e: The hotel, a former castle, was built over 200 years ago.
words.zh: (形) 以前的；從前的　(名) 前任者；前者

words.d: A formula is a set mathematical way or method of solving a problem.
words.e: I learned a new formula that may help us with our problem.
words.zh: (名) 公式；方程式　(名) 配方；成分

words.d: To found a company or organization means to start it.
words.e: The pilgrims founded one of the first colonies in the United States.
words.zh: (動) 創辦；建立　(動) 發現；找到

words.d: To invest means to use money in a way that will bring a profit later.
words.e: I invested money in a new building that should bring me a profit.
words.zh: (動) 投資；花錢購買　(動) 投入時間或精力

words.d: A loan is the act of lending something, usually money.
words.e: I got a loan from the bank.
words.zh: (名) 貸款；借款　(動) 借出；貸予

words.d: If something is practical, it is useful in normal life.
words.e: Learning English is practical; you can use it in many places.
words.zh: (形) 實用的；實際的

words.d: A quarter is 25 cents.
words.e: He paid a quarter for the candy.
words.zh: (名) 四分之一；一刻鐘；一季

words.d: A salary is how much money a person makes at his or her job.
words.e: He got a new job with a better salary.
words.zh: (名) 薪水；薪資

words.d: A scholarship is money given to one so they can go to school.
words.e: I got a scholarship to help me pay for university.
words.zh: (名) 獎學金

words.d: If something is temporary, it exists for a short time.
words.e: This car is only temporary; I’ll get a new one soon.
words.zh: (形) 臨時的；暫時的

words.d: A treasure is a collection of valuable things, especially jewels or gold.
words.e: They became very rich when they found the buried treasure.
words.zh: (動) 珍惜；珍視　(名) 寶藏；珍寶

words.d: To urge someone is to try very hard to get them to do something.
words.e: He urged them to believe his story.
words.zh: (名) 強烈的慾望；衝動　(動) 驅策；力勸

words.d: To arise is to happen.
words.e: Difficulties arose with his computer because it was old.
words.zh: (動) 出現；產生　發生；起身

words.d: A benefactor is a person who gives money to help someone.
words.e: The student’s benefactor gave him money to spend on his studies.
words.zh: 贊助人是提供金錢幫助他人的人。

words.d: A blacksmith is a person who makes things out of metal.
words.e: The blacksmith pounded the piece of metal until it was flat.
words.zh: (名) 鐵匠；打鐵工人

words.d: When someone is charitable, they help people who are in need.
words.e: My sister was charitable enough to help me buy my first house.
words.zh: (形) 慈善的；樂善好施的　(形) 寬容的；仁慈的

words.d: A chimney is a tall pipe used to carry smoke out of a building.
words.e: The cat was on the roof sitting next to the chimney.
words.zh: (名) 煙囪；烟道

words.d: To compensate is to pay someone for the time they spent doing something.
words.e: Her boss compensated her for the extra work she did last week.
words.zh: (動) 補償；賠償　(動) 彌補；抵消

words.d: If you encounter something, you meet or come close to it.
words.e: I encountered a sea turtle while I was swimming.
words.zh: (動) 遇見；碰面　(名) 相遇；邂逅

words.d: To exceed is to be more than something.
words.e: Since I exceeded my limit, I decided to get rid of my credit cards.
words.zh: (動) 超過；勝過　(動) 超出限制；過量

words.d: To forge is to make or produce, especially with difficulty.
words.e: Stacy and Heather forged their friendship when they were teenagers.
words.zh: (動) 鍛造；偽造　(名) 鍛造廠；熔爐

words.d: People who are humble do not believe that they are better than other people.
words.e: Even though Bob is the smartest boy in his class, he is humble.
words.zh: (形) 謙虛的；謙遜的　(動) 使謙卑；貶低

words.d: Iron is a strong metal that is used to make many objects.
words.e: The horse had shoes made of iron.
words.zh: (名) 鐵；熨斗　(動) 熨燙；解決　(形) 堅硬的；鐵製的

words.d: A ladder is an object that is used to climb up and down things.
words.e: He used a ladder to climb to the top of his tree house.
words.zh: (名) 梯子；階梯　(名) 晉升的途徑；升遷機會

words.d: If people are modest, they do not think that they are too important.
words.e: Derek is very modest for someone who is so rich.
words.zh: (形) 謙虛的；不傲慢的　(形) 適度的；不過分的

words.d: To occupy a place is to live, work, or be there.
words.e: Kevin and Alice occupied the chairs and had a long discussion.
words.zh: (動) 佔據；佔領　(動) 使某人忙碌；使某人專注

words.d: A penny is a coin worth one cent.
words.e: U.S. President Abraham Lincoln is on the penny.
words.zh: (名) 便士；一分錢

words.d: To preach is to talk about and promote a religious idea.
words.e: Aaron often preached about living an honest life.
words.zh: (動) 宣揚；講道

words.d: To prosper is to be successful or make a lot of money.
words.e: Frank’s new business finally prospered after many years of hard work.
words.zh: (動) 繁榮；成功

words.d: A province is a small area that is controlled by a country.
words.e: Canada is divided into several different provinces.
words.zh: (名) 省份；省

words.d: Satisfaction is a feeling you get when you do or receive something good.
words.e: Brad was filled with satisfaction when he saw what was for dinner.
words.zh: (名) 滿足；滿意

words.d: To sustain something is to keep it going.
words.e: Wind power is a clean way to sustain a city with energy.
words.zh: (動) 維持；支撐

words.d: To acquire something is to gain possession of it.
words.e: Tina acquired a strange package yesterday.
words.zh: (動) 獲得；取得　(動) 學到；習得

words.d: If something is awkward, it is embarrassing and uncomfortable.
words.e: After dropping his coffee cup, Robbie felt awkward.
words.zh: (形) 尷尬的；不自在的　(形) 笨拙的；不靈巧的

words.d: A caretaker is a person who takes care of very young, old, or sick people.
words.e: My grandmother’s caretaker helps her get around the house.
words.zh: (名) 照顧者；管理員　(名) 臨時代理人；代理負責人

words.d: To deceive someone is to make them believe something that is not true.
words.e: He tried to deceive his friends as they were playing a game.
words.zh: (動) 欺騙；迷惑　(名) 欺騙；詐騙

words.d: To discourage someone is to make them feel less excited about something.
words.e: Mr. Perry discouraged the students from quitting school.
words.zh: (動) 使失去勇氣；打擊信心　(動) 阻止；勸阻

words.d: If something is fake, it is made to look real in order to trick people.
words.e: The model was wearing fake eyelashes.
words.zh: (形) 假的；偽造的　(動) 偽造；冒充

words.d: Hatred is a strong feeling of not liking someone or something.
words.e: I have a hatred for the taste of medicine.
words.zh: (名) 仇恨；厭惡　(動) 痛恨；憎恨

words.d: A hut is a house made of wood, grass, or mud that has only one or two rooms.
words.e: We all went into the hut to sleep.
words.zh: (名) 小屋；茅舍　(名) 簡陋的房子

words.d: If something is inferior, it is not as good as something else.
words.e: Cars built a hundred years ago are inferior to ones built today.
words.zh: (形) 較差的；低級的　(形) 下位的；次要的

words.d: A lodge is a house in the mountains, used by people who hunt or fish.
words.e: During our ski trip, we stayed at a lodge.
words.zh: (動) 提出；提交　(名) 小屋；山間小住所

words.d: To neglect someone or something is to not take care of it properly.
words.e: William neglected his room, so it is a complete mess.
words.zh: (動) 忽視；疏忽　(名) 疏忽；忽視

words.d: A newcomer is a person who has recently arrived at a place or a group.
words.e: The students happily welcomed the newcomer to the school.
words.zh: (名) 新來的人；新手　(名) 初入者；剛到的人

words.d: An offense is behavior that is wrong or breaks a law.
words.e: Stealing a car is a very serious offense.
words.zh: (名) 冒犯；得罪　(名) 犯罪行為；違法行為

words.d: To overlook something is to not notice it, or to not realize that it is important.
words.e: Brenda overlooked the last step and had a bad fall.
words.zh: (動) 忽略；俯視

words.d: To repay is to pay back or to reward someone or something.
words.e: She repaid her friend for all of his hard work with a small gift.
words.zh: (動) 償還；回報

words.d: If something is ridiculous, it is silly or strange.
words.e: Steve looked ridiculous with those huge blue sunglasses.
words.zh: 如果某事物荒謬，就是指它愚蠢或奇怪。

words.d: If something is satisfactory, it is good enough.
words.e: Mina often received satisfactory grades since she studied so hard.
words.zh: (形) 令人滿意的；符合要求的

words.d: A shepherd is a person who protects and cares for sheep.
words.e: The shepherd moved the sheep to another field.
words.zh: (名) 牧羊人　(動) 引導；帶領

words.d: To venture is to go to a place that may be dangerous.
words.e: Even though it was dangerous, they ventured up the mountain.
words.zh: (動) 冒險；敢於

words.d: Wheat is a plant which makes grain. Wheat grain is used to make bread.
words.e: The field of golden wheat was ready to be harvested.
words.zh: (名) 小麥

words.d: An alley is a narrow road behind houses or buildings.
words.e: The alley behind my house looks dirty.
words.zh: (名) 小巷；巷道　(名) 後街；後巷

words.d: An ax is a tool used to cut wood.
words.e: She used an ax to cut some wood for the fire.
words.zh: (名) 斧頭；斧子　(動) 用斧頭砍；解僱

words.d: A bunch is a group of the same things.
words.e: She was hungry, so she ate the entire bunch of grapes.
words.zh: (名) 束；群；一堆　(動) 聚集；束在一起

words.d: A chore is a job that you have to do but don’t like.
words.e: It’s Nikki’s chore to do the dishes every Tuesday and Wednesday night.
words.zh: (名) 家務；雜務　(動) 分配家務；派遣

words.d: When something is decent, it is OK or good enough.
words.e: Eric did a decent job painting the fence.
words.zh: (形) 令人滿意的；不錯的　(形) 得體的；適當的

words.d: Disgrace is a feeling of shame.
words.e: He felt a sense of disgrace after failing his big exam.
words.zh: (名) 恥辱；羞辱　(動) 使蒙羞；玷污　(形) 可恥的；丟臉的

words.d: The elbow is the middle part of a person’s arm that lets you bend it.
words.e: She pointed to her elbow to show me where she hurt herself.
words.zh: (名) 手肘；肘部　(動) 用肘推；擠開

words.d: When you are grateful for something, you are happy that it happened.
words.e: The girls were grateful to have a chance to spend time with their grandfather.
words.zh: (形) 感謝的；感激的　(形) 令人欣慰的；令人愉快的

words.d: To irritate means to annoy someone.
words.e: She was irritated when her brother told her that he had lost her camera.
words.zh: (動) 激怒；惹惱　(動) 使發炎；刺激

words.d: To kid is to make a joke to make someone believe something which is not true.
words.e: I am not really mad. I was kidding when I said I was angry.
words.zh: (名) 小孩；兒童　(動) 開玩笑；戲弄

words.d: When something is loose, it is not held in place well.
words.e: The bolt was loose, so I tightened it with the wrench.
words.zh: (形) 鬆的；不緊的　(動) 放鬆；解開

words.d: To offend someone means to make them angry or upset.
words.e: They were both offended by what they had said to each other.
words.zh: (動) 冒犯；得罪　惹惱；傷害

words.d: When something happens overnight, it happens during the night.
words.e: The campers stayed in the tent overnight.
words.zh: (副) 在夜間；一夜之間　(形) 夜間的；突然的

words.d: To persist means to keep doing something even when it is hard.
words.e: Even though the lesson was difficult, he persisted until he understood.
words.zh: (動) 堅持；持續存在

words.d: A pine is a type of tree that is tall and thin.
words.e: There were many beautiful pine trees in the forest.
words.zh: (動) 渴望；思念　(名) 松樹

words.d: A scar is a mark on the skin after a wound heals.
words.e: The monster had horrible scars on his cheek and forehead.
words.zh: (名) 疤痕；(動) 留下疤痕

words.d: A sensation is a feeling a person gets from their senses.
words.e: Mom got a painful sensation in her head from the loud noise.
words.zh: (名) 感覺；轟動

words.d: A sled is a small vehicle that you use on snow.
words.e: Marvin likes to ride his sled down the hill in winter.
words.zh: (名) 雪橇　(動) 乘雪橇

words.d: To tease someone means to laugh at or make fun of them.
words.e: Jake teased Charlie because he was the new student.
words.zh: (動) 取笑；戲弄

words.d: A valentine is someone you love or admire with great affection.
words.e: Harry wanted Molly to be his valentine.
words.zh: (名) 情人；情人卡

words.d: When a plant blooms, it makes flowers.
words.e: Roses look so pretty when they bloom.
words.zh: (動) 開花；盛開　(名) 花朵；花期

words.d: If something is compact, it is smaller than normal.
words.e: A laptop computer is much more compact than a desktop model.
words.zh: (形) 緊湊的；小巧的　(動) 壓緊；壓縮

words.d: A curl is a small piece of something with a round shape.
words.e: The colorful ribbons were bent into curls.
words.zh: (動) 捲；使彎曲　(名) 捲髮；彎曲物

words.d: To decay is to be destroyed naturally.
words.e: The old wooden house was slowly decaying.
words.zh: (動) 腐爛；腐蝕；衰退　(名) 腐爛；衰退

words.d: Dessert is a sweet food that you eat after a meal.
words.e: Kayla wants ice cream for dessert.
words.zh: (名) 甜點；甜食　(動) 遺棄；拋棄

words.d: To dip something is to put part of it into a liquid for a short time.
words.e: Laurel dipped her strawberry into the chocolate.
words.zh: (動) 浸；沾　(名) 醬料；下降

words.d: If something is distant, it is far away.
words.e: Michael could barely see the distant island.
words.zh: (形) 遠的；遠處的　(形) 冷淡的；疏遠的

words.d: An eclipse is an occasion when the moon moves in front of the sun.
words.e: A complete eclipse of the sun is a very rare occurrence.
words.zh: (名) 日食；月食　(動) 遮蔽；使黯然失色

words.d: A fairy is a small, magic creature with wings.
words.e: There are usually fairies in fantasy stories.
words.zh: (名) 仙女；精靈　(形) 仙女般的；神奇的

words.d: Grace is a quality of moving in a smooth, relaxed and attractive way.
words.e: The ballerina danced with grace.
words.zh: (名) 優雅；恩典　(動) 使優雅；裝飾　(形) 優雅的；得體的

words.d: Leisure is time when you do not have to do work.
words.e: Eve likes to listen to music in her leisure time.
words.zh: (名) 閒暇；休閒時間　(形) 悠閒的；不急的

words.d: Mankind is all of the world’s people.
words.e: All of mankind has to work to make this a better world.
words.zh: (名) 人類；全體人類

words.d: Passion is a very strong feeling of wanting to do something.
words.e: She had a passion for dancing.
words.zh: (名) 熱情；愛好

words.d: A pillow is something that you put your head on when you sleep.
words.e: When I travel, I usually take along my favorite pillow.
words.zh: (名) 枕頭

words.d: A pulse is the beat of the heart.
words.e: The doctor checked the patient’s pulse by feeling his wrist.
words.zh: (名) 脈搏；脈衝　(動) 搏動；跳動

words.d: To refresh someone is to make them feel less hot or tired.
words.e: The baby was refreshed after taking a cool bath.
words.zh: (動) 使恢復精神；使清新

words.d: To sneeze is to suddenly blow air out of your nose and mouth.
words.e: He sneezed after smelling the flower.
words.zh: (名) 噴嚏　(動) 打噴嚏

words.d: A spice is a flavor for food and drinks.
words.e: Two common spices found in many homes are salt and pepper.
words.zh: (名) 香料；調味品　(動) 加香料於；增添趣味

words.d: To whistle is to make a sound by putting your lips together and blowing.
words.e: Ashe was listening to music, Daryl whistled.
words.zh: (名) 哨子；哨聲　(動) 吹口哨；鳴笛

words.d: Wool is the hair that a sheep has.
words.e: Grandma wants to use the blue wool to knit me a sweater.
words.zh: (名) 羊毛

words.d: To acquaint is to get to know something or someone.
words.e: Nancy acquainted herself with the new computer.
words.zh: (動) 使認識；介紹　(動) 了解；熟悉

words.d: A cemetery is where people are buried when they die.
words.e: Some people are scared of cemeteries.
words.zh: (名) 墓地；公墓

words.d: To curse someone or something is to hope that bad things happen to them.
words.e: The witch cursed the village.
words.zh: (動) 詛咒；咒罵　(名) 詛咒；髒話

words.d: A disguise is something you wear so people cannot tell who you are.
words.e: Everyone knew that it was Dad in the Santa disguise.
words.zh: (名) 偽裝；化妝　(動) 偽裝；隱瞞

words.d: If something is fancy, it is nicer than normal.
words.e: Their table was all set for a fancy dinner.
words.zh: (形) 花俏的；精美的　(動) 想要；喜歡

words.d: A flashlight is a small electric light that you carry in your hand.
words.e: We took a flashlight when we went camping.
words.zh: (名) 手電筒；閃光燈

words.d: A hood is part of a coat that goes over your head.
words.e: She put on her hood to keep her head warm.
words.zh: (名) 兜帽；風帽　(名) 街區；社區

words.d: An inhabitant is a person who lives in a certain place.
words.e: The number of inhabitants in the countryside is increasing.
words.zh: (名) 居民；住民

words.d: To nourish something is to give it food that it needs to live.
words.e: A good mother will nourish her baby every day.
words.zh: (動) 滋養；供養　(動) 培養；促進

words.d: A pirate is a sailor who steals things from other boats.
words.e: Pirates are very scary characters.
words.zh: (名) 海盜；盜版者　(動) 盜版；剽竊

words.d: A publication is something printed, like a newspaper or book.
words.e: She’s been a subscriber to that publication for over ten years.
words.zh: (名) 出版；出版物

words.d: A riddle is a question that is difficult to answer but meant to be funny.
words.e: I could not answer Wendy’s riddle, but it made me laugh.
words.zh: (名) 謎；謎語

words.d: When something rots, it slowly gets softer and is destroyed.
words.e: The old log began to rot in the forest.
words.zh: (動) 腐爛；敗壞

words.d: To scare means to cause one to feel frightened.
words.e: I was scared by the sight of the monster.
words.zh: (動) 驚嚇；嚇走

words.d: If something will happen shortly, it will happen very soon.
words.e: My workday will end shortly.
words.zh: (副) 不久；簡短地

words.d: A skeleton is the bones of a body.
words.e: There is a skeleton in the science classroom.
words.zh: (名) 骨骼；骨架

words.d: If something spoils, it turns bad or rots.
words.e: We left the fruit out too long, and it spoiled.
words.zh: (動) 破壞；寵壞

words.d: If a person starves, they do not get enough to eat and sometimes die.
words.e: During the war, many people starved.
words.zh: (動) 餓死；挨餓

words.d: A thrill is an exciting feeling.
words.e: The boys enjoy the thrill of surfing a big wave.
words.zh: (名) 興奮；震顫　(動) 使興奮；使震顫

words.d: If something is wicked, it is very bad or evil.
words.e: My boss is a very wicked man.
words.zh: (形) 邪惡的；壞的

words.d: To alert someone is to tell or warn them about something.
words.e: The fire alarm alerted us that there was a problem.
words.zh: (形) 警覺的；機敏的　(動) 警告；提醒　(名) 警報；警告

words.d: A broadcast is a television or radio show.
words.e: We watched the broadcast of the local news on TV.
words.zh: (名) 廣播；播送　(動) 廣播；播放

words.d: A bulletin is a news report that talks about very recent and important events.
words.e: There was a live bulletin reporting on the economy of the city.
words.zh: (名) 公告；簡訊　(名) 期刊；通訊

words.d: A bump is a small raised area on a surface.
words.e: The monkey got a bump on his head because he was hit by a rock.
words.zh: (名) 碰撞；腫塊　(動) 碰撞；撞擊　(形) 崎嶇不平的

words.d: To chop something means to cut it into pieces with a tool.
words.e: Mom chopped some vegetables to put into the stew.
words.zh: (動) 砍；劈　(名) 砍刀；肉排

words.d: A closet is a small room used to store things.
words.e: Marie has many clothes inside o f her closet.
words.zh: (名) 壁櫥；衣櫃　(形) 秘密的；隱藏的

words.d: To console someone who is sad means to make them comforted.
words.e: When my dog ran away, my dad consoled me.
words.zh: (動) [kənˈsol] 安慰；撫慰　(名) [ˈkɑnˌsol] 控制台；遊戲機

words.d: A district is a small part of a city, county, state, or country.
words.e: I live in a residential district of Seattle, Washington.
words.zh: (名) 地區；地帶　(名) 區域；行政區

words.d: A drawer is a small part in furniture that is used to store things.
words.e: I put my clothes into the empty drawers.
words.zh: (名) 抽屜；抽屜形容器

words.d: To endure something tough means to do or go through it.
words.e: She had to endure her husband shouting all day long.
words.zh: (動) 忍受；承受　(動) 持續；維持

words.d: To execute someone means to kill them as a legal punishment.
words.e: Some people are executed for serious crimes.
words.zh: (動) 執行；實施　(動) 處死；被處死

words.d: To grasp something means to hold it.
words.e: He grasped the bag o f money tightly.
words.zh: (動) 抓住；握住　(動) 理解；領會　(名) 掌握；理解

words.d: When something is in the rear of something, it is in the back.
words.e: The man loaded the rear of his truck with boxes.
words.zh: (動) 養育；飼養；種植

words.d: A senator is someone who makes laws for a state.
words.e: The young senator promised to make laws that would help the people.
words.zh: (名) 參議員

words.d: A skull is the hard part of your head. Your brain is inside of it.
words.e: The brain is protected by the skull.
words.zh: (名) 頭骨；骷髏

words.d: To stir something means to mix it using something small, like a spoon.
words.e: Mom stirred the batter until it was smooth.
words.zh: (名) 騷動；攪動　(動) 攪拌；攪動；激起

words.d: To tap something is to hit it lightly.
words.e: He tapped the keys of the keyboard to write a short word.
words.zh: (動) 輕拍；輕觸　(名) 水龍頭；輕拍

words.d: When something is tremendous, it is very large.
words.e: The Earth’s oceans are filled with a tremendous amount of water.
words.zh: (形) 巨大的；驚人的

words.d: When something is underneath something else, it is below or lower than it.
words.e: The roots of a tree are located underneath the ground.
words.zh: (介) 在...下面　(副) 在下面；在底下

words.d: A worm is a small animal with a long, thin body.
words.e: Worms are often used to help catch fish.
words.zh: (名) 蟲；蠕蟲

words.d: To abandon something is to leave it forever or for a long time.
words.e: The old room had been abandoned years before.
words.zh: (動) 放棄；拋棄

words.d: If someone is ambitious, they want to be rich or successful.
words.e: Kendra had to be ambitious to get into medical school.
words.zh: (形) 有抱負的；野心勃勃的　(形) 需要付出努力的；艱難的

words.d: When a dog barks, it makes a short, loud noise.
words.e: The dog barked loudly and frighteningly.
words.zh: (名) 樹皮　(動) 吠叫；大聲喊叫

words.d: A bay is an area near the ocean where the land goes inward.
words.e: The Golden Gate Bridge crosses San Francisco Bay.
words.zh: (名) 海灣；灣　(動) 包圍；圍住

words.d: If someone is brilliant, they are very smart.
words.e: My younger sister is brilliant for someone her age.
words.zh: (形) 聰慧的；傑出的　(形) 耀眼的；光彩的

words.d: Your chin is the hard part at the bottom of your face.
words.e: Luke pointed to the hair on his chin.
words.zh: (名) 下巴；頦　(動) 用力抬起下巴

words.d: A complaint is something you say to tell people that you don’t like something.
words.e: Mom said she didn’t want to hear my brother’s complaints.
words.zh: (名) 抱怨；投訴　(動) 抱怨；申訴

words.d: When someone is deaf, they cannot hear.
words.e: Susan was born deaf.
words.zh: (形) 聾的；耳聾的　(形) 不願聽的；漠視的

words.d: To be enthusiastic about something is to be excited by or interested in it.
words.e: The man was enthusiastic about his job.
words.zh: (形) 熱情的；充滿熱忱的　(形) 熱心的；積極的

words.d: An expedition is a long trip, usually to a place very far away.
words.e: They got into their spaceship to begin their expedition.
words.zh: (名) 探險隊；遠征　(名) 探險之旅；考察活動

words.d: The horizon is where the sky looks like it meets the ground.
words.e: The sun clipped below the horizon.
words.zh: (名) 地平線；視野　(名) 前景；範圍

words.d: To be loyal to something or someone is to agree to always help them.
words.e: The three friends are very loyal to each other.
words.zh: (形) 忠誠的；真實的　(形) 忠心的；可靠的

words.d: The mayor is the person in charge of a city.
words.e: The mayor o f my hometown is quite a powerful speaker.
words.zh: (名) 市長；鎮長

words.d: If something is mutual, it is felt in the same way by two or more people.
words.e: Robert likes Sarah. The feeling is mutual.
words.zh: (形) 相互的；彼此的　(形) 共同的；雙方的

words.d: If someone is overweight, they are very fat.
words.e: Roger eats too much, so now he’s overweight.
words.zh: (形) 過重的；超重的

words.d: A refuge is a place where you go to be safe.
words.e: When it started to rain, she found refuge in the house.
words.zh: (名) 避難所；庇護

words.d: To restore something is to put it back the way it was.
words.e: Victor restored the old car.
words.zh: (動) 恢復；修復

words.d: To rub something is to push on it and move your hand back and forth.
words.e: Mom’s feet were sore, so she rubbed them.
words.zh: (動) 摩擦；擦

words.d: Your senses are how you see, taste, hear, feel and smell.
words.e: It would be hard to live without your five senses.
words.zh: 感官是你觀看、品嚐、聆聽、觸摸與嗅聞的方式。

words.d: A veterinarian is a doctor that takes care of animals.
words.e: Wanda became a veterinarian because she loves dogs.
words.zh: (名) 獸醫

words.d: An anniversary is a day that celebrates something from the past.
words.e: My parents went out to eat for their wedding anniversary.
words.zh: (名) 週年紀念；周年紀念日

words.d: Arithmetic is math.
words.e: I like to study arithmetic at school.
words.zh: (名) 算術；四則運算　(形) 算術的；數字的

words.d: To be ashamed is to feel upset because you did something wrong or bad.
words.e: He was ashamed when he found out that I knew about his past.
words.zh: (形) 羞愧的；感到慚愧的　(形) 尷尬的；害臊的

words.d: To burst is to suddenly break open or apart.
words.e: The bomb burst over the city.
words.zh: (動) 突然破裂；爆炸　(名) 突然的爆發；一陣

words.d: A carpenter is a person who builds things with wood.
words.e: We hired a carpenter to make a cupboard.
words.zh: (名) 木匠；木工

words.d: Coal is a hard black rock that you burn for heat.
words.e: Many power stations burn coal to produce energy.
words.zh: (名) 煤炭；煤　(名) 餘燼；木炭

words.d: A couch is a long, soft seat that many people can sit on.
words.e: Kim and M artin’s new couch was very expensive.
words.zh: (名) 沙發；長椅　(動) 用言語表達；蹲伏

words.d: When a liquid drips, just a little bit falls at a time.
words.e: I heard water dripping from the faucet.
words.zh: (名) 滴；點滴　(動) 滴下；點滴流出

words.d: If something is elegant, it is very fancy and pleasing.
words.e: In Japan, women wear elegant kimonos on special occasions.
words.zh: (形) 優雅的；高雅的　(形) 精緻的；簡潔的

words.d: Fabric is cloth used to make clothes, furniture, etc.
words.e: The towels were made from a soft fabric.
words.zh: (名) 布料；織物　(名) 結構；構成

words.d: The highlands are high areas of land, usually with mountains.
words.e: The man had a small home in the highlands.
words.zh: 高地是通常有山脈的高海拔地區。

words.d: Ivory is a white, hard substance that comes from elephants.
words.e: The elephant’s long ivory tusks looked very impressive.
words.zh: (名) 象牙；象牙色　(形) 象牙製的；象牙色的

words.d: A mill is a building where flour is made.
words.e: The farmer took his wheat to the mill to make it into flour.
words.zh: (名) 磨坊；工廠　(動) 磨；碾碎

words.d: A needle is a small, sharp piece of metal that you use to make or fix clothes.
words.e: I used a needle to fix the hole in my pants.
words.zh: (名) 針；針頭　(動) 用針刺；慢慢推進

words.d: To polish something is to rub it in order to make it shiny.
words.e: Mark spent all morning polishing his shoes for the wedding.
words.zh: (名) 亮光劑；擦亮　(動) 擦亮；磨光　(形) 波蘭的

words.d: To sew means to put pieces of cloth together using string.
words.e: I learned to sew when I was a little girl.
words.zh: (動) 縫；縫製

words.d: A shed is a small building where you store things like tools.
words.e: We have a small shed in the backyard for storage.
words.zh: (名) 棚子；小屋　(動) 脫落；流出

words.d: A thread is a thin piece of string.
words.e: I have many different colors of thread at home.
words.zh: (名) 線；細絲　(動) 穿線；穿過

words.d: To trim something is to cut it a little bit.
words.e: I had my hair trimmed this afternoon.
words.zh: (動) 修剪；削減

words.d: If something goes upwards, it moves vertically higher.
words.e: The kite went upwards further and further.
words.zh: (副) 向上地；上升地

words.d: To ail a person or group is to cause difficulty or pain.
words.e: My brother went to the doctor to see what was ailing him.
words.zh: 困擾某人或某群體，是指造成困難或痛苦。

words.d: An ally is someone who agrees to help or support you.
words.e: I was happy to find many allies who shared the same opinion as me.
words.zh: (名) [ˈælaɪ] 盟友；同盟國　(動) [əˈlaɪ] 與…結盟；聯合

words.d: To boast is to talk about how good you are.
words.e: We all became tired o f listening to him boast about him self all day.
words.zh: (動) 吹牛；自誇　(名) 吹牛的話；自誇

words.d: To bounce is to move something up and away from a surface after hitting it.
words.e: Owen bounced the ball on the ground.
words.zh: (動) 反彈；彈回　(名) 反彈；彈力

words.d: A bully is a person who is mean to others.
words.e: The two bullies always picked on the smaller, weaker kids.
words.zh: (動) 欺凌；霸凌　(名) 霸凌者；惡棍

words.d: Carbohydrates are substances in foods like bread that give you energy.
words.e: Carbohydrates like rice are a good source o f energy for active people.
words.zh: (名) 碳水化合物；醣類

words.d: To crawl is to move slowly on your hands and knees.
words.e: The baby crawled across the floor.
words.zh: (動) 爬行；緩慢移動　(名) 爬行；緩慢速度

words.d: To defeat someone is to beat them in a game or battle.
words.e: The champion defeated the challenger in the boxing match.
words.zh: (動) 擊敗；戰勝　(名) 失敗；敗北

words.d: A dial is the front of a clock.
words.e: I looked at the dial to see what time it was.
words.zh: (動) 撥打；轉動　(名) 錶盤；刻度盤

words.d: When someone or something is dominant, they are stronger than others.
words.e: The gorilla is one o f the dominant animals in the jungle.
words.zh: (形) 佔優勢的；主要的　(形) 支配的；統治的

words.d: If you show mercy, you choose not to punish or harm someone.
words.e: He asked his boss for mercy and to not fire him.
words.zh: (名) 憐憫；慈悲　(名) 寬恕；饒恕

words.d: To nod is to move your head up and down.
words.e: I nodded my head as I listened to the song.
words.zh: (動) 點頭；表示同意　(名) 點頭的動作

words.d: An opponent is someone who fights against you.
words.e: On the field, our opponents were too good for us.
words.zh: (名) 對手；敵手　(名) 反對者；反對派

words.d: To quarrel is to argue or fight.
words.e: Billy quarreled with his wife about buying a new house.
words.zh: (動) 爭吵；吵架

words.d: A rival is someone that is trying to keep you from getting what you want.
words.e: The three rivals were all competing for the same job.
words.zh: (名) 競爭者；對手　(動) 與…匹敵；競爭

words.d: When a part of your body is sore, it hurts.
words.e: After lifting the heavy box, Mona’s back was sore.
words.zh: (形) 疼痛的；痠痛的

words.d: To sting is to cause pain by pushing a sharp part into the skin.
words.e: The needle stung my arm and made me say, “ Ouch!”
words.zh: (動) 刺；螫　(名) 刺；刺痛

words.d: To strain is to try very hard.
words.e: Casey strained to lift the heavy box.
words.zh: (名) 壓力；負擔　(動) 拉緊；使疲勞

words.d: Torture is something that causes you physical or mental pain.
words.e: Greg thought that writing the essay was torture.
words.zh: (名) 折磨；拷問　(動) 折磨；拷打

words.d: To wrestle is to play a game where you try to push someone to the floor.
words.e: My dad loved to wrestle when he was in high school.
words.zh: (動) 奮力掙扎；摔角

words.d: Absence is the state of something being away.
words.e: There is an absence of sand in the hourglass.
words.zh: (名) 缺席；不在

words.d: If you say something aloud, you say it so that others can hear you.
words.e: My father often reads stories aloud to me and my sister.
words.zh: (副) 大聲地；出聲地　(形) 大聲的；出聲的

words.d: If someone is bald, they have no hair.
words.e: My oldest brother is bald.
words.zh: (形) 禿頭的；光禿的

words.d: A blanket is a piece of cloth that you use to keep warm or to sit upon.
words.e: I laid a blanket on the ground so that we could have a picnic.
words.zh: (名) 毛毯；毛巾　(動) 覆蓋；包裹　(形) 全面的；籠統的

words.d: To creep is to move quietly and slowly.
words.e: The cat slowly crept down the tree.
words.zh: (動) 爬行；緩慢移動　(名) 令人不快的人

words.d: Divorce is an event in which a marriage is ended.
words.e: Divorce rates have increased in the past twenty years.
words.zh: (名) 離婚；婚姻解除　(動) 與...離婚；分離

words.d: To imitate someone is to do exactly what they do.
words.e: He imitated his favorite superhero by putting on a costume.
words.zh: (動) 模仿；仿造　(動) 假裝；模仿他人

words.d: An infant is a baby.
words.e: The infant cried all night.
words.zh: (名) 嬰兒；幼兒　(形) 嬰幼的；初期的

words.d: To kidnap someone is to take them illegally.
words.e: She was terrified to find out her son was kidnapped.
words.zh: (動) 綁架；誘拐　(名) 綁架案；誘拐事件

words.d: A nap is a short sleep, usually during the day.
words.e: I took a short nap because I stayed up late last night.
words.zh: (名) 小睡；打盹　(動) 打盹；小睡

words.d: You use nowhere to say that a place or thing does not exist.
words.e: Unfortunately, water was nowhere to be found.
words.zh: (副) 任何地方都沒有；無處　(名) 偏遠的地方；荒涼之地

words.d: To pat something is to hit it softly with your hand.
words.e: I patted some lotion onto my face.
words.zh: (名) 輕拍　(動) 輕拍；拍打

words.d: Relief is a feeling you get when something bad or challenging ends.
words.e: I felt a sense of relief when I heard the good news.
words.zh: (名) 減輕；解脫

words.d: To reproduce is to make something exactly how someone else did it.
words.e: The children tried to reproduce their house using toy blocks.
words.zh: (動) 複製；繁殖

words.d: To rhyme is to have the same sounds at the end of a word.
words.e: Humpty Dumpty is an old rhyme that children learn in school.
words.zh: (動) 押韻　(名) 韻；押韻詞

words.d: To suck is to put something in your mouth and try to get flavor out of it.
words.e: The baby sucked milk from her bottle.
words.zh: (動) 吸；吮　(形) 糟糕的；令人不快的

words.d: If something is urgent, it is important and needs to be done now.
words.e: He had to leave now; it was urgent.
words.zh: (形) 緊急的；迫切的

words.d: To vanish is to go away suddenly.
words.e: All the passengers vanished from the train station.
words.zh: (動) 消失；消散

words.d: A wagon is a cart you use to carry heavy things.
words.e: He used his wagon to carry some of his gifts.
words.zh: (名) 馬車；貨車

words.d: A wrinkle is a line on a person’s face that happens as they get old.
words.e: My grandfather has some wrinkles on his face.
words.zh: (動) 起皺；使起皺　(名) 皺紋

words.d: If something is abnormal, it is strange or not normal.
words.e: Her idea of art is a bit too abnormal for me.
words.zh: (形) 異常的；不正常的

words.d: Bamboo is a hard plant with thin branches and leaves.
words.e: Outside his home, there is a lot of bamboo.
words.zh: (名) 竹子；竹　(名) 竹製品

words.d: A blossom is a flower or group of flowers.
words.e: There were colorful blossoms in the yard.
words.zh: (名) 花；花朵　(動) 開花；繁榮

words.d: A compass is a device used to tell what direction one is going.
words.e: A compass is an important tool to have when you are traveling.
words.zh: (名) 指南針；羅盤　(名) 圓規；範圍

words.d: A dialect is a regional variety of language showing where you are from.
words.e: People from the U.K. have a different dialect of English from those in the U.K.
words.zh: (名) 方言；地方語言　(名) 某一群體的語言變體

words.d: If someone is dishonest, they do not tell the truth.
words.e: She was being dishonest when she copied the answers from his test.
words.zh: (形) 不誠實的；欺騙的　(名) 不誠實的人；騙子

words.d: A dwarf is a creature from stories that often looks like a short, hairy man.
words.e: The dwarf had a pleasant look on his face.
words.zh: (名) 侏儒；矮子　(動) 使顯得矮小；阻礙成長　(形) 矮小的；發育不全的

words.d: An ecosystem is a group of plants and animals in an area.
words.e: The river’s ecosystem not only includes fish but other insects and plants too.
words.zh: (名) 生態系統；生態系

words.d: If something is fatal, it causes death.
words.e: They passed away in a fatal car crash.
words.zh: (形) 致命的；導致死亡的　(形) 致命性的；毀滅性的

words.d: If someone is impatient, they are not able to wait for things.
words.e: Bob was so impatient that he could barely wait for the light to turn green.
words.zh: (形) 急躁的；沒耐心的　(形) 渴望的；急切的

words.d: A leaf is the flat green thing on trees or bushes.
words.e: In the fall, the leaves on the trees change colors.
words.zh: (名) 葉子；樹葉　(動) 翻頁；離開

words.d: A manuscript is an old book or paper written by hand.
words.e: The manuscript was written over 150 years ago.
words.zh: (名) 手稿；原稿　(名) 手寫文件；手寫本

words.d: A marsh is a type of wet land covered with grasses and short plants.
words.e: The marsh looked so lovely against the mountains.
words.zh: (名) 沼澤；濕地　(動) 行進；邁進

words.d: Patience is the ability to wait for something without becoming upset.
words.e: Sue’s patience was rewarded by catching her very first fish.
words.zh: (名) 耐心；忍耐

words.d: Perfume is a good-smelling liquid that girls wear.
words.e: When she wore her perfume, everyone said she smelled great.
words.zh: (名) 香水

words.d: A pond is a very small area of water.
words.e: The ducks swam in the pond.
words.zh: (名) 池塘

words.d: A proverb is a short saying that tells you something important.
words.e: The proverb “egg on your face” means you’ve done something embarrassing.
words.zh: (名) 諺語；格言

words.d: A pursuit is a chase.
words.e: The dinosaur was in pursuit of the caveman.
words.zh: (名) 追求；追尋

words.d: To recite something means to repeat or say aloud in front of a group.
words.e: Katie recited the Pledge of Allegiance in class.
words.zh: (動) 背誦；朗誦

words.d: A wilderness is an area where no people live.
words.e: If you are not careful, you can get lost in the wilderness.
words.zh: (名) 荒野；荒蕪之地

words.d: To anticipate something is to think that it will happen.
words.e: Carrie anticipated the arrival of her baby.
words.zh: (動) 預期；期待　(動) 先於...之前做；預先準備

words.d: A barrel is a round thing that you can keep liquids in.
words.e: There was an empty barrel outside the house.
words.zh: (名) 桶；木桶　(名) 槍管；砲管

words.d: A beam is a heavy bar.
words.e: Modern skyscrapers are made with several beams.
words.zh: (名) 光線；光束　(動) 發光；照射　(名) 梁；橫樑

words.d: If something is casual, it is relaxed or simple.
words.e: You can wear casual clothes to the party like jeans.
words.zh: (形) 隨意的；不正式的　(形) 偶然的；無計畫的

words.d: Caution is care and attention in order to avoid danger.
words.e: Please use the power saw with caution. It is very dangerous.
words.zh: (名) 小心；謹慎　(動) 警告；勸阻

words.d: If something is contrary to something else, it is the opposite.
words.e: It isn’t warm outside at all. On the contrary, it is quite cold.
words.zh: (形) 相反的；相對的　(副) 相反地；恰恰相反

words.d: If you are deliberate, you do something on purpose.
words.e: Bernie made a deliberate attempt to injure Andy.
words.zh: (動) 仔細考慮；深思熟慮　(形) 故意的；蓄意的；深思熟慮的

words.d: To dissolve something is to mix it into a liquid and disappear.
words.e: I dissolved the pill in a glass o f water.
words.zh: (動) 溶解；分解　(動) 結束；解散

words.d: When something explodes, it blows up.
words.e: My new radio exploded when I plugged it in.
words.zh: (動) 爆炸；爆發　(名) 爆炸

words.d: To fasten something is to close it or put it in the correct place.
words.e: Elizabeth fastened her seat belt.
words.zh: (動) 扣牢；繫緊　(動) 固定；栓住

words.d: A germ is something that makes you sick.
words.e: Germs are on everything that you touch.
words.zh: (名) 細菌；病菌　(名) 萌芽；種子

words.d: A kit is a set of all the things needed to do something.
words.e: Is there a first aid kit in your office?
words.zh: (名) 工具包；套裝　(名) 裝備；全套用品

words.d: A puff is a little bit of smoke or steam.
words.e: A puff of smoke came from the burnt match.
words.zh: (動) 吹氣；噴出　(名) 一陣（風、煙等）

words.d: A rag is a small towel.
words.e: Please use a rag to clean the dust o ff the table.
words.zh: (名) 破布；抹布

words.d: To scatter something is to make it go in many places.
words.e: I accidentally scattered all of my pills.
words.zh: (動) 使分散；散開

words.d: A scent is a smell.
words.e: Julie enjoyed the scent of the flowers.
words.zh: (名) 氣味；香氣　(動) 嗅；聞到

words.d: Steel is a shiny gray metal.
words.e: The new apartment building was made with steel.
words.zh: (名) 鋼鐵；鋼

words.d: If something is swift, it is fast.
words.e: The swift horse easily jumped over the hurdle.
words.zh: (形) 迅速的；敏捷的

words.d: If you toss something, you throw it softly.
words.e: He tossed a coin into the air.
words.zh: (動) 扔；拋；擲

words.d: Triumph is what you feel when you win or finish something.
words.e: He raised the award in triumph at the end of his speech.
words.zh: (名) 勝利；成功　(動) 獲勝；成功

words.d: When someone is aboard a ship or plane, they are on or in it.
words.e: They climbed aboard the kayak and paddled through the river.
words.zh: (副) 在船（車、飛機）上　(介) 上（船、車、飛機）

words.d: When a person is bitter, they are upset with someone or a situation.
words.e: He was extremely bitter when his computer crashed.
words.zh: (形) 苦的；痛苦的　(副) 極其；非常

words.d: A bullet is a small metal object that is shot out of guns.
words.e: Bullets come in different sizes for different guns.
words.zh: (名) 子彈；槍彈　(名) 要點；項目符號

words.d: The devil is a powerful evil spirit in some religions.
words.e: The church promised protection from the devil.
words.zh: (名) 魔鬼；惡魔　(名) 壞人；調皮鬼

words.d: To drift means to be moved slowly by wind or water.
words.e: The large chunk of ice drifted in the water.
words.zh: (動) 漂流；漂移　(名) 漂流；偏移

words.d: To enforce means to make a person follow a rule.
words.e: Police enforce traffic laws to keep everyone safe.
words.zh: (動) 執行；實施　(動) 強制；強迫

words.d: A fountain is a source of water made by people.
words.e: There was a beautiful fountain in the middle of the park.
words.zh: (名) 噴泉；水池

words.d: A harbor is an area of water along a shore where boats land.
words.e: There were a few small boats in the harbor.
words.zh: (名) 港灣；海港　(動) 懷有；庇護

words.d: To inhabit means to live in a certain place.
words.e: No one inhabits the ancient city.
words.zh: (動) 居住；棲息　(動) 占據；存在於

words.d: To march means to walk at a steady pace together with others.
words.e: The soldiers marched in straight rows.
words.zh: (名) 三月；行進　(動) 行走；遊行

words.d: A millionaire is a person who has at least a million dollars.
words.e: He became a millionaire because he was smart with his money.
words.zh: (名) 百萬富翁；百萬富豪

words.d: A port is a place where ships stop to load and unload things.
words.e: The ship was being loaded with materials at the port.
words.zh: (名) 港口

words.d: A sheriff is a police officer who is in charge of a large area.
words.e: It was the sheriff’s job to make the city safe.
words.zh: (名) 警長；郡治安官

words.d: To startle means to scare someone suddenly.
words.e: The loud crash startled the sleeping woman.
words.zh: (動) 使驚嚇；使嚇一跳

words.d: To sweat means to lose liquid from the body through the skin.
words.e: Whenever I workout, I sweat quite a bit.
words.zh: (動) 流汗；(名) 汗水

words.d: A trigger is the part of the gun that a person pulls to make it fire.
words.e: The man had his finger on the trigger of the gun.
words.zh: (動) 觸發；引發　(名) 扳機；觸發因素

words.d: To unify means to bring people or things together.
words.e: With their good deeds, the children tried to unify the world.
words.zh: (動) 統一；統合

words.d: A vessel is a large ship or boat.
words.e: We toured the area aboard a luxury vessel.
words.zh: (名) 船；容器

words.d: A voyage is a long journey made on a boat or an aircraft.
words.e: The astronauts took off on a long voyage to the moon.
words.zh: (名) 航行；旅行

words.d: To worship means to like and honor a person, thing, or religious figure.
words.e: Many people around the world worship in a church.
words.zh: (動) 崇拜；敬仰　(名) 崇拜；禮拜

words.d: An apprentice is a person who learns how to do a job from a skilled person.
words.e: Mark is an apprentice chef at the restaurant.
words.zh: (名) 學徒；實習生　(動) 使當學徒；訓練

words.d: To assure someone is to tell them something is true to make them less worried.
words.e: He assured the boss that the building would be done on time.
words.zh: (動) 保證；確保　(動) 使確信；讓放心

words.d: A bandage is a piece of cloth used to stop bleeding.
words.e: If you cut yourself, please get a bandage from the first-aid kit.
words.zh: (名) 繃帶；藥布　(動) 用繃帶包紮；包紮傷口

words.d: To bleed is to lose blood.
words.e: If you are not careful, you will cut your finger and bleed.
words.zh: (動) 流血；出血　(動) 滲出；洩漏

words.d: To bond with someone is to become friends with them.
words.e: The women bonded after several hours of conversation.
words.zh: (名) 紐帶；連結　(動) 粘合；結合

words.d: A chef is a person who cooks in a restaurant.
words.e: Tom is a chef at the restaurant near my house.
words.zh: (名) 主廚；廚師

words.d: A crown is the hat worn by a king or queen.
words.e: The crown is made of gold.
words.zh: (名) 皇冠；王冠　(動) 為…加冕；居首

words.d: A departure is the act of leaving a place.
words.e: They were excited about their departure back home.
words.zh: (名) 出發；離開　(名) 偏離；出發時間

words.d: If someone is diligent, they work hard and are careful.
words.e: Craig has always been a very diligent person at work.
words.zh: (形) 勤勉的；認真的　(形) 盡心盡力的；努力的

words.d: An emperor is the leader of a group of countries.
words.e: The emperor Julius Caesar was in control o f ancient Rome.
words.zh: (名) 皇帝；帝王

words.d: Fiber is a thread of a substance used to make clothes or rope.
words.e: The mat was made from tiny fibers.
words.zh: (名) 纖維；纖維素　(名) 光纖

words.d: If something is horrible, it is very bad.
words.e: The assignment was horrible. I hated it.
words.zh: (形) 可怕的；討厭的　(形) 極壞的；糟糕的

words.d: If someone is impolite, they are rude.
words.e: Roger is im polite to everyone he meets.
words.zh: (形) 無禮的；不禮貌的

words.d: To kneel is to put one or both knees on the ground.
words.e: Daryl got down on one knee and asked Nina to marry him.
words.zh: (動) 跪下；跪著　(名) 跪的動作

words.d: A luxury is an expensive thing that you do not need.
words.e: We stayed at a luxury resort for our honeymoon.
words.zh: (名) 奢侈；奢華　(形) 豪華的；奢侈的

words.d: If something is massive, it is very big.
words.e: The wheels on his truck were massive.
words.zh: (形) 巨大的；龐大的　(形) 大規模的；廣泛的

words.d: To panic is to feel so nervous or afraid that you cannot think clearly.
words.e: Everyone panicked when the house caught on fire.
words.zh: (名) 恐慌；驚慌　(動) 使恐慌；驚慌失措

words.d: A priority is something that is more important than other things.
words.e: My priority is to get good grades in school.
words.zh: (名) 優先；優先事項

words.d: A robe is a long, loose piece of clothing.
words.e: Many people wear robes in the morning.
words.zh: (名) 長袍；浴袍

words.d: To scold means to criticize one angrily because they have done wrong.
words.e: Jesse was scolded by the teacher for not paying attention.
words.zh: (動) 責罵；訓斥

words.d: An affair is an event or a thing that happened.
words.e: My wife and I attended a formal affair over the weekend.
words.zh: (名) 事情；事務　(名) 婚外情；風流韻事

words.d: An assembly is a group that is together for the same reason.
words.e: The students had an assembly to talk about their interests.
words.zh: (名) 集會；大會　(動) 組裝；聚集

words.d: To bless is to ask God for protection or help.
words.e: The angel blessed the newborn baby to keep it safe.
words.zh: (動) 祝福；祝願　(動) 讚美；頌揚

words.d: Cereal is a food that you mix with milk and eat for breakfast.
words.e: Cereal is a fast and common breakfast food enjoyed in the U.S.
words.zh: (名) 穀類食品；麥片　(名) 穀物；谷類

words.d: If someone is cheerful, they are happy or feel good.
words.e: The children were cheerful because they didn’t have to go to school.
words.zh: (形) 快樂的；高興的　(形) 樂觀的；積極的

words.d: The diameter of a round thing is the length across its center.
words.e: The diameter o f the tree was about 22 centimeters.
words.zh: (名) 直徑；直經

words.d: To exploit something is to use it for greedy reasons rather than good reason.
words.e: The company exploits their workers and makes them stay 12 hours a day.
words.zh: (動) [ɪkˈsplɔɪt] 剝削；利用　(名) [ˈɛksplɔɪt] 功績；壯舉

words.d: A famine is a long time with little or no food.
words.e: The farmers couldn’t grow any food on the dry soil, so there was a famime.
words.zh: (名) 飢荒；飢餓　(形) 饑饉的；極度缺乏的

words.d: A harvest is the act of collecting food from farming.
words.e: They had a lot of wheat from the last harvest.
words.zh: (名) 收穫；豐收　(動) 收穫；採收

words.d: If someone is merry, they are very happy.
words.e: They felt merry because the weather was great.
words.zh: (形) 快樂的；充滿歡樂的　(形) 愉快的；高興的

words.d: A nut is a hard seed or fruit that comes from some trees and bushes.
words.e: To eat a nut, first you have to crack its shell.
words.zh: (名) 堅果；核果　(名) 螺帽；螺栓

words.d: To pardon is a way to ask someone to repeat what was said before.
words.e: Pardon me teacher, but could you repeat what you just said?
words.zh: (名) 原諒；寬恕　(動) 原諒；赦免

words.d: A pharaoh was a king in ancient Egypt.
words.e: The pharaohs ruled Egypt for thousands of years.
words.zh: 法老是古埃及的國王。

words.d: When a fruit is ripe, it is ready to be eaten.
words.e: The cherries were nice and ripe.
words.zh: (形) 成熟的

words.d: To roast something is to cook it in an oven or over a fire.
words.e: Mom roasted a turkey for the holiday dinner.
words.zh: (動) 烤；烘烤　(名) 烤肉

words.d: A routine is a way of doing things that is the same every time.
words.e: My father’s daily routine includes shaving right before breakfast.
words.zh: (名) 例行公事；常規　(形) 例行的；常規的

words.d: A scheme is a plan or design.
words.e: Mickey and Minnie came up with a scheme to solve the problem.
words.zh: (名) 方案；計畫　(動) 策劃；圖謀

words.d: If something or someone is slim, they are thin.
words.e: Look at my new cell phone. It’s very slim.
words.zh: (形) 苗條的；薄的

words.d: A stove is a device used to cook food.
words.e: Our new stove helps us to cook food much faster than before.
words.zh: (名) 爐子；火爐

words.d: A theft is a criminal act that involves someone stealing something.
words.e: The theft of his TV took place when he was at work.
words.zh: (名) 偷竊；盜竊

words.d: An adolescent is a young person or a teenager.
words.e: The adolescent was excited about getting a skateboard for his birthday.
words.zh: (名) 青少年；十幾歲的人　(形) 青春期的；少年的

words.d: Aptitude is a natural ability or skill.
words.e: He has a natural aptitude for water skiing.
words.zh: (名) 天賦；才能　(名) 傾向；素質

words.d: To compliment is to say a nice thing about someone or something.
words.e: Her co-worker complimented her for doing a good job.
words.zh: (名) 讚美；稱讚　(動) 稱讚；恭維

words.d: To hinder is to keep someone or something from doing something.
words.e: All the traffic hindered me from getting to work on time.
words.zh: (動) 妨礙；阻礙　(動) 阻止；延遲

words.d: Journalism is the work of collecting the news to put in newspapers or on TV.
words.e: Before becoming a teacher, she worked in journalism.
words.zh: (名) 新聞工作；新聞業

words.d: A jury is a group of people that listen to a trial and say if someone is guilty.
words.e: The jury listened closely to the attorney before they made their decision.
words.zh: (名) 陪審團；評委會

words.d: Justice is fairness in the way that you treat other people.
words.e: People turn to the court system when they are seeking justice.
words.zh: (名) 正義；公正　(名) 司法；審判

words.d: Liberty is freedom to do what you want.
words.e: To many people, the Statue of Liberty is a symbol of freedom.
words.zh: (名) 自由；自主　(名) 許可；權利

words.d: If someone or something is literary, it is involved with literature in some way.
words.e: He worked hard to create a successful literary career.
words.zh: (形) 文學的；文學性的　(形) 逐字的；字面上的

words.d: A pharmacy is a place where medicine is sold.
words.e: My mother sells medicine to people at the pharmacy.
words.zh: (名) 藥局；藥房

words.d: A pill is a small object that has medicine inside.
words.e: She took a pill for her headache.
words.zh: (名) 藥丸；藥片

words.d: To presume is to believe something is true without being certain.
words.e: Since he raised his hand, the teacher presumed he knew the answer.
words.zh: (動) 假定；推測

words.d: To have privacy is to be away from other people.
words.e: Please hang the sign on the door so we can have some privacy.
words.zh: (名) 隱私；私密

words.d: A punishment is something that one must endure for any wrong doing.
words.e: He was given a punishment for being rude to the teacher.
words.zh: (名) 懲罰；處罰

words.d: If someone is sensible, they make good decisions.
words.e: It was sensible for her to save some money each month.
words.zh: (形) 明智的；合理的

words.d: A slice is a piece from something larger, such as a cake.
words.e: The girl enjoyed a slice of cake at her birthday party.
words.zh: (動) 切；切片　(名) 片；薄片

words.d: Sorrow is a very sad feeling.
words.e: The girl felt sorrow after her best friend moved away.
words.zh: (名) 悲傷；哀痛

words.d: A straw is a thin tube that is used to suck liquid into the mouth.
words.e: I drank the orange juice through a straw.
words.zh: (名) 吸管；稻草

words.d: To swell is to become larger and rounder.
words.e: My sister’s stomach began to swell after she got pregnant.
words.zh: (動) 膨脹；上漲　(名) 膨脹；隆起

words.d: When something is tidy, it is clean and in order.
words.e: Leon has always been a very tidy boy.
words.zh: (動) 整理；收拾　(形) 整潔的

words.d: Affection is a feeling of liking someone or something.
words.e: Amanda has a lot of affection for her little sister Sarah.
words.zh: (名) 喜愛；疼愛　(名) 感情；情感

words.d: An agency is a business or service set up to act for others.
words.e: I went to a travel agency to help me arrange a flight home.
words.zh: (名) 代理機構；仲介公司　(名) 行動；作用；力量

words.d: Ash is the grey or black powder created when something is burned.
words.e: The end of his cigar was full of ashes.
words.zh: (名) 灰燼；灰　(名) 骨灰　(形) 灰色的；蒼白的

words.d: To confine something is to keep it in one place.
words.e: The elephant is confined to a cage in the zoo.
words.zh: (動) 限制；侷限　(動) 監禁；關押

words.d: To dismiss something is to say it is not important.
words.e: He quickly dismissed my idea about a new project.
words.zh: (動) 解僱；開除　(動) 駁回；不理會

words.d: To erupt is for a volcano or something to shoot a hot substance.
words.e: The volcano erupted for the first time in ten years.
words.zh: (動) 爆發；噴發　(動) 突然發生；突然出現

words.d: Fate is a power that causes some things to happen.
words.e: Some people believe that a person’s hand can tell their fate.
words.zh: (名) 命運；宿命　(名) 結局；下場

words.d: Lava is the hot substance made of melted rock that shoots from volcanoes.
words.e: The red hot lava poured from the volcano.
words.zh: (名) 熔岩；火山岩漿

words.d: If someone is miserable, they are very unhappy.
words.e: He was miserable after his dog died.
words.zh: (形) 悲慘的；痛苦的　(形) 令人厭惡的；糟糕的

words.d: To navigate something is to control the way it moves or goes.
words.e: She navigated the ship across the ocean.
words.zh: (動) 航行；導航　航行；駕駛

words.d: To originate somewhere is to start there.
words.e: The idea of democracy originated in Ancient Greece.
words.zh: (動) 起源；發源

words.d: The remainder of something is what is left.
words.e: He took a bite of the apple, then gave me the remainder of it.
words.zh: (名) 剩餘部分；其餘的人

words.d: To retrieve something is to find it and get it back.
words.e: She retrieved her mail from the mail box.
words.zh: (動) 找回；取回

words.d: If something is shallow, it is not deep.
words.e: The kids were playing in the shallow water.
words.zh: (形) 淺的；淺薄的

words.d: A slope is ground that is not flat.
words.e: The slope to the top of the mountain was very steep.
words.zh: (名) 斜坡；坡度　(動) 傾斜；使傾斜

words.d: To span a length of time is to last that long.
words.e: His work began in 1999. It has spanned many years since then.
words.zh: (動) 跨越；涵蓋　(名) 跨度；一段時間

words.d: A superstition is something magical that people believe is real.
words.e: It is a superstition that Friday the 13<sup>th</sup> is an unlucky day.
words.zh: (名) 迷信

words.d: Sympathy is a feeling of being sad for another person.
words.e: I felt sympathy for my sister so I got her a balloon to cheer her up.
words.zh: (名) 同情；同情心

words.d: To vibrate is to shake very hard.
words.e: The machine made his whole body vibrate as he broke up the ground.
words.zh: (動) 震動；顫動

words.d: To wander is to walk without going to a certain place.
words.e: The boys like to wander in the woods and look at birds.
words.zh: (動) 漫步；閒逛

words.d: Armor is metal worn by soldiers to protect the body.
words.e: The soldier wore armor to protect his body.
words.zh: (名) 盔甲；防具　(名) 裝甲；盔甲

words.d: To blaze means to burn brightly or powerfully.
words.e: The small fire soon blazed into a large dangerous one.
words.zh: (動) 燃燒；熊熊燃燒　(名) 火焰；烈火

words.d: To boom means to make a loud, deep sound.
words.e: The firecrackers made a loud boom when they exploded.
words.zh: (名) 繁榮；景氣　(動) 發出低沉聲音；急速增長

words.d: A cliff is a high and often flat wall of rock.
words.e: The wolf stood at the cliff and howled.
words.zh: (名) 懸崖；峭壁

words.d: A flame is part of fire.
words.e: The torch was filled with yellow and orange flames.
words.zh: (名) 火焰；火苗　(動) 燃燒；發火　(形) 火紅的；激烈的

words.d: Independence is the state of being free from the control of others.
words.e: After leaving home, Sophia had a great feeling of independence.
words.zh: (名) 獨立；自主　(名) 自由；自治

words.d: An invasion is an attack by a group from another country.
words.e: In Korea, walls were built around cities to protect them from invasions.
words.zh: (名) 入侵；侵略　(名) 大量湧入；入侵

words.d: A knight is a soldier of high rank and skill who usually serves a king.
words.e: He was the best soldier, so the king made him a knight.
words.zh: (名) 騎士；爵士　(名) 國際象棋中的馬

words.d: Lightning is the bright light seen during a storm.
words.e: The lightning flashed above the water.
words.zh: (名) 閃電；電光　(形) 閃電般的；迅速的

words.d: A rebel is a person who fights the government in order to change it.
words.e: The rebel had enough of the government’s unfair polices.
words.zh: (動) 反抗；叛逆

words.d: To retreat means to run away because you have been beaten in a fight.
words.e: The army retreated because they were losing the battle.
words.zh: (動) 撤退；退卻　(名) 撤退；隱居

words.d: A revolution is a change to the political system by a group of people.
words.e: The revolution in Russia led to the creation of the Soviet Union.
words.zh: (名) 革命；變革

words.d: A spear is a long stick with a blade on one end that is used as a weapon.
words.e: The soldier was holding a spear in his hand.
words.zh: (名) 矛；魚叉　(動) 刺；叉

words.d: If something is steep, then its slope or angle rises or falls sharply.
words.e: He rode his bike up the steep hill to reach the top.
words.zh: (形) 陡峭的；急遽的

words.d: A summit is the highest part of a hill or mountain.
words.e: Snow covered the summit of the mountain even during the summer.
words.zh: (名) 山頂；最高點　(動) 參加高峰會；登上頂峰

words.d: Thunder is the loud noise heard during a storm.
words.e: The sound of the thunder startled me.
words.zh: (名) 雷；雷聲　(動) 打雷；轟隆作響

words.d: Troops are soldiers that fight in groups in a battle.
words.e: The troops were all prepared to go into battle.
words.zh: (名) 軍隊；部隊

words.d: A warrior is a brave soldier or fighter.
words.e: The samurai were some of the most skilled warriors in the ancient world.
words.zh: (名) 戰士；勇士

words.d: To withdraw means to leave a place, usually during war.
words.e: After losing the battle, the enemy withdrew back to its own country.
words.zh: (動) 退出；撤回

words.d: To yield something means to give up control of it or to give it away.
words.e: He had to yield his turn because he was in checkmate.
words.zh: (動) 讓路；屈服　(名) 產量；收益

words.d: A bench is a long seat for two or more people.
words.e: Most parks have benches for citizens to relax upon.
words.zh: (名) 長凳；板凳　(名) 運動隊的替補席

words.d: To confront a hard situation or person is to deal with it.
words.e: The couple has to confront each other about their problems.
words.zh: (動) 面對；對抗　勇敢面對（困難、問題等）

words.d: A daisy is a small flower with white petals and a yellow center.
words.e: There were a few daisies growing in the field.
words.zh: 雛菊是一種白色花瓣、黃色中心的小花。

words.d: A dispute is an argument or disagreement that people have.
words.e: Karen and Brian often have disputes about silly things.
words.zh: (名) 爭議；爭執　(動) 爭論；質疑

words.d: Horror is a feeling of being very afraid or shocked.
words.e: The audience screamed in horror when the ghost appeared in the movie.
words.zh: (名) 恐怖；驚駭　(形) 令人恐怖的；可怕的

words.d: An incident is an event that is usually not pleasant.
words.e: Mr. Wilson had an incident where he became sick and had to leave.
words.zh: (名) 事件；事故　(名) 衝突；糾紛

words.d: Mist is water that you can see in the air or on a surface.
words.e: The forest was covered with mist.
words.zh: (名) 霧；薄霧　(動) 使模糊；噴霧

words.d: An object is an inanimate thing that you can see or touch.
words.e: The shopping cart was filled with objects.
words.zh: (名) [ˈɑbdʒɪkt] 物體；對象　(動) [əbˈdʒɛkt] 反對；提出異議

words.d: An orphan is a child who does not have parents.
words.e: The orphan frequently cried during the night.
words.zh: (名) 孤兒　(動) 使成孤兒

words.d: To plot is to make a secret plan to do something that is wrong or mean.
words.e: The group was plotting to ruin the company’s financial reports.
words.zh: (名) 情節；陰謀　(動) 策劃；繪製

words.d: When a female is pregnant, she is going to have a baby.
words.e: The pregnant woman was shopping for baby clothes.
words.zh: (形) 懷孕的

words.d: Rage is a very angry feeling.
words.e: The chef was filled with rage when his helpers ruined the meal.
words.zh: (名) 憤怒；暴怒　(動) 發怒；肆虐

words.d: Revenge is what you do to hurt or punish someone who hurts you.
words.e: He broke his sister’s doll as revenge after she lost his favorite book.
words.zh: (名) 報復；復仇　(動) 報復；為…報仇

words.d: Shame is a bad feeling about things you have done wrong.
words.e: The boy felt shame about misplacing his clothes.
words.zh: (名) 羞愧；恥辱

words.d: To sigh is to breathe out loudly and show that you are tired or sad.
words.e: Molly sighed when she looked at all the information she had to research.
words.zh: (名) 嘆息；嘆氣　(動) 嘆息；嘆氣

words.d: To sneak is to move quietly so that no one hears or sees you.
words.e: The thief snuck out of the house without anyone noticing him.
words.zh: (動) 偷偷地走；偷帶

words.d: To spare something is to give it because you have more than you need.
words.e: I wanted to help him but I couldn’t spare a tire.
words.zh: (動) 抽出（時間）；讓出；節省　(形) 多餘的；備用的

words.d: The stem of a plant is the stick that grows leaves or flowers.
words.e: The rose had a long thin stem.
words.zh: (動) 阻止；遏制　(名) 莖；柄

words.d: Supper is a meal that is eaten in the evening.
words.e: We usually have supper around 6 o ’clock at my house.
words.zh: (名) 晚餐；宵夜

words.d: When something is tender, it is soft and easy to chew.
words.e: The meat was so tender they didn’t need knives to cut it with.
words.zh: (形) 嫩的；溫柔的

words.d: If something is beneath something else, it is under it.
words.e: The largest part of an iceberg lies beneath the waterline.
words.zh: (介) 在…下面；低於　(動) 在…之下

words.d: A cub is a baby animal, such as a bear or lion.
words.e: The lion cub was crying for its mother.
words.zh: (名) 幼獸；小熊　(名) 年輕缺乏經驗的人

words.d: Dawn is the time of day when the sun rises.
words.e: At dawn, the sun gently rose over the farm.
words.zh: (名) 黎明；清晨　(動) 開始；破曉

words.d: If you are dissatisfied, you are not happy with something.
words.e: I was dissatisfied with their decision to work on Sunday.
words.zh: (形) 不滿意的；不滿足的

words.d: When something is done with ease, it is not hard to do.
words.e: The monkey climbed the tree with ease.
words.zh: (動) 減輕；舒緩　(名) 輕鬆；舒適

words.d: When something is evident, it is easy to see or understand.
words.e: It was evident from the look on his face that he was unhappy.
words.zh: (形) 明顯的；清楚的　(形) 顯而易見的；無庸置疑的

words.d: Hail is ice that falls from the sky when rain freezes.
words.e: The hail from the storm was the size of golf balls.
words.zh: (名) 冰雹　(動) 稱讚；歡迎　(動) 招呼；打招呼

words.d: To howl means to make a long, loud sound like a wolf or a dog.
words.e: The wolf howled at the moon.
words.zh: (動) 嚎叫；號叫　(名) 嚎叫聲；哭喊聲

words.d: To leap means to jump a long distance.
