words.e: He had to leap over the gap to reach the other side of the hill.
words.zh: (動) 跳躍；跳過　(名) 跳躍；飛躍

words.d: When something is magnificent, it is beautiful and grand.
words.e: The man gave his wife a pair of magnificent diamond earrings.
words.zh: (形) 壯麗的；宏偉的　(形) 傑出的；卓越的

words.d: A necessity is something that is needed.
words.e: Fresh water is a necessity for life.
words.zh: (名) 必需；必要性　(名) 必需品；生活必需物

words.d: An outcome is the end of an action or event.
words.e: The outcome of his latest business plan was a complete failure.
words.zh: (名) 結果；成果

words.d: A pile is a large group of things on top of one another.
words.e: The pile of cups was beginning to tilt.
words.zh: (名) 堆；大量　(動) 堆積；累積

words.d: When something is profound, it is very intelligent.
words.e: For a little boy, Jeremy has some profound thoughts.
words.zh: (形) 深刻的；深遠的

words.d: To seize something means to grab it quickly or strongly.
words.e: The man seized as much money as he could before anyone could see him.
words.zh: (動) 抓住；奪取

words.d: To squeeze something means to press it together and hold it tightly.
words.e: When Clara saw her cat, she gave it a big squeeze.
words.zh: (動) 擠；壓榨

words.d: When something is supreme, it is the highest or best.
words.e: The supreme officer was in charge of keeping the citizens calm.
words.zh: (形) 最高的；至高的

words.d: When something is terrific, it is very good.
words.e: My youngest daughter is a terrific painter.
words.zh: (形) 極好的；絕妙的

words.d: A trait is part of someone’s personality.
words.e: One trait of Salvador’s personality is his cheerfulness.
words.zh: (名) 特質；特徵

words.d: When something is vital, it is necessary for life.
words.e: The heart is a vital organ.
words.zh: (形) 至關重要的；生死攸關的

words.d: When you become accustomed to something, you are in the habit of it.
words.e: Grandfather is accustomed to reading the newspaper every morning.
words.zh: (形) 習慣的；適應的　(動) 使習慣；使適應

words.d: To affirm is to say that something is true.
words.e: Using a graph, Malcolm affirmed the success o f the company.
words.zh: (動) 肯定；確認　(動) 聲明；宣稱

words.d: If someone is astonished, they are very surprised or shocked.
words.e: I was astonished when he pulled the live rabbit out of his hat.
words.zh: (形) 驚訝的；吃驚的　(動) 使驚訝；使吃驚

words.d: To bang is to hit something to make a noise.
words.e: The drummer banged on his drum as he marched in the parade.
words.zh: (名) 爆炸聲；砰聲　(動) 砰地關閉；猛烈撞擊　(副) 砰地一聲；正好

words.d: A clan is a group of relatives or friends.
words.e: The Lee clan meets every year to celebrate the New Year.
words.zh: (名) 氏族；家族　(名) 幫派；團體

words.d: When something is dim, it does not give out much light.
words.e: Working in a dim room is bad for your eyes.
words.zh: (形) 昏暗的；模糊的　(動) 使變暗；調暗

words.d: Emphasis is special attention or importance.
words.e: The students put special emphasis on chapter 4 because it will be on the test.
words.zh: (名) 強調；重點　(動) 強調；著重

words.d: A fable is a short story that teaches a lesson.
words.e: In the fable about the tortoise and the hare, the lesson is consistency.
words.zh: (名) 寓言；寓言故事　(名) 謊言；虛構的故事

words.d: A feast is a large meal for many people.
words.e: At Thanksgiving, I enjoy a wonderful feast with my family.
words.zh: (名) 盛宴；豐盛的餐飲　(動) 大吃；享受豐富的食物

words.d: To glow is to make a soft light.
words.e: The small flame glowed softly.
words.zh: (動) 發光；照亮　(名) 光輝；光芒

words.d: When something is hollow, it has an empty space inside.
words.e: Straws are hollow, so liquid can flow through them.
words.zh: (形) 空心的；中空的　(動) 挖空；使凹陷

words.d: Instinct is the natural way that people behave without thinking about it.
words.e: Cats hunt mice because of instinct.
words.zh: (名) 本能；直覺　(動) 憑直覺驅使

words.d: A joint is a place of the body where the bones meet, such as the knee.
words.e: Two important bones in your leg meet at a join t in your knee.
words.zh: (名) 關節；接合處　(動) 連接；結合　(形) 共同的；聯合的

words.d: To leak is to let a liquid or gas pass through a flaw.
words.e: The pipe leaks from many places.
words.zh: (名) 洩漏；漏水　(動) 洩露；漏出

words.d: A physician is a doctor.
words.e: The physician said I would feel better if I took my medicine.
words.zh: (名) 醫生；內科醫師

words.d: To sacrifice something valuable is to give it up to get something else.
words.e: Her parents sacrificed a lot of money in order for her to go to college.
words.zh: (名) ['sækrəˌfaɪs] 犧牲；奉獻　(動) [ˈsækrəˌfaɪs] 犧牲；獻出

words.d: When something is stiff, it is hard to move.
words.e: The bird was standing on the tree’s s tiff branch.
words.zh: (形) 硬的；僵硬的；(態度) 嚴格的

words.d: To stroke is to move a hand over something or someone.
words.e: She stroked her cheek to see if there was something on it.
words.zh: (名) 划水；中風　(動) 撫摸；划水

words.d: When something is tragic, it is connected with death and suffering.
words.e: The airplane crashed in a tragic accident.
words.zh: (形) 悲慘的；悲劇的

words.d: A tune is a song.
words.e: The students played a familiar tune for the audience.
words.zh: (動) 調音；調整

words.d: To accommodate is to have enough room.
words.e: The meeting room can accommodate nine people.
words.zh: (動) 容納；提供住宿

words.d: A circus is a traveling show with animals and people.
words.e: I like to go to the circus to see the animals do tricks.
words.zh: (名) 馬戲團；馬戲表演

words.d: If two things coincide, they happen at the same time.
words.e: My birthday coincides with Christmas.
words.zh: (動) 巧合；一致　(動) 同時發生；相符

words.d: To commission someone to do something is to pay them to do it.
words.e: The artist was commissioned to create a picture.
words.zh: (名) 佣金；手續費　(動) 委託；授權　(名) 委員會；機構

words.d: A dose is a certain amount of medicine that you take at one time.
words.e: My mother gave me a dose of medicine before I went to bed.
words.zh: (名) 劑量；一份　(動) 給予劑量；給藥

words.d: To dye something is to make it a certain color by using a special chemical.
words.e: Valery got her hair dyed at the salon yesterday.
words.zh: (動) 染色；將某物浸入顏料　(名) 染料；顏料

words.d: The extent of something is how large, important, or serious it is.
words.e: He ate to such an extent that he became overweight.
words.zh: (名) 程度；範圍　(名) 長度；面積

words.d: Gender is a category that describes being either a boy or a girl.
words.e: Do you know the gender of her new baby?
words.zh: (名) 性別；性　(形) 性別的；關於性別的

words.d: A headline is the title of a newspaper story.
words.e: The headline on the front page was about the economy.
words.zh: (名) 標題；頭條新聞　(動) 擔任主要角色；做為頭條刊登

words.d: When something is informal, it is not official.
words.e: They had an informal meeting to talk about their experiences.
words.zh: (形) 非正式的；不正規的　(形) 隨便的；輕鬆的

words.d: To inquire about something is to ask about it.
words.e: Dad called to inquire about the price of tickets for the show.
words.zh: (動) 詢問；打聽　(動) 調查；探究

words.d: A messenger is one who carries information from one place to another.
words.e: The messenger delivered an important document to the office.
words.zh: (名) 信使；使者　(名) 通訊軟體

words.d: To peer at something is to watch it carefully.
words.e: She peered at people through the window.
words.zh: (名) 同儕；同輩

words.d: A portrait is a painting or photograph of someone.
words.e: I saw many religious portraits when I went to the museum.
words.zh: (名) 肖像；畫像

words.d: To pose is to stay in one place without moving.
words.e: The kids and their dog posed for a picture.
words.zh: (動) 擺姿勢；造成；提出

words.d: A ranch is a large farm where animals are kept.
words.e: My uncle has many horses on his ranch.
words.zh: (名) 牧場；大農場

words.d: To steer something is to control where it goes.
words.e: He steered the go-cart around the track.
words.zh: (動) 駕駛；引導

words.d: A stripe is a thick line.
words.e: The flag of the United States has red and white stripes.
words.zh: (名) 條紋；斑紋　(動) 加條紋

words.d: When an animal is tame, it is not afraid to be near people.
words.e: The tame bird rested on his hand.
words.zh: (形) 馴服的；溫順的　(動) 馴服；馴化

words.d: To tempt people is to offer them something they want but shouldn’t have.
words.e: I wasn’t hungry, but she tempted me with a piece of my favorite cake.
words.zh: (動) 誘惑；引誘

words.d: An Aborigine is a native of Australia before Europeans lived there.
words.e: The Aborigines created beautiful artwork.
words.zh: (名) 原住民；土著

words.d: To ban something is to not let people do it.
words.e: Smoking is banned in this building.
words.zh: (動) 禁止；禁用　(名) 禁令；禁止

words.d: If you are cautious, you are careful in a dangerous situation.
words.e: Be very cautious as you stack those boxes.
words.zh: (形) 謹慎的；小心的　(形) 留意的；警覺的

words.d: To confess something is to say that you did it.
words.e: He confessed that he was responsible for choosing the winning story.
words.zh: (動) 承認；坦白　(動) 向神父認罪

words.d: A cottage is a small, old house in the countryside.
words.e: My aunt lives in a pretty cottage in the mountains.
words.zh: (名) 小屋；鄉村別墅　(名) 農舍；度假小屋

words.d: Daytime is the time of the day when the sky is light.
words.e: I only allowed my kids to play outside in the daytime.
words.zh: 白天是一天中天空明亮的時段。

words.d: If you are desperate, you will try anything to do or change something.
words.e: I’m desperate to find a new job.
words.zh: (形) 絕望的；孤注一擲的　(動) 使絕望

words.d: If something fades, it gets quieter or darker.
words.e: The piece of cloth I found was old and faded.
words.zh: (動) 褪色；消退　(動) 逐漸消失；減弱

words.d: If a person or animal is fierce, they are angry or violent.
words.e: Wolves are fierce animals. Do not disturb them.
words.zh: (形) 兇猛的；激烈的　(形) 強烈的；熾熱的

words.d: To gamble means to play a game that involves winning or losing money.
words.e: Many people like to go to casinos to gamble.
words.zh: (動) 賭博；冒險　(名) 賭博；冒險行為

words.d: A lawn is an area covered in grass.
words.e: My dad keeps the lawn in front of our house very neat.
words.zh: (名) 草坪；草地

words.d: To mow grass is to cut it to make it very short.
words.e: I mow our lawn every weekend.
words.zh: (動) 剪草；割草

words.d: An outlaw is a criminal who hides from the police.
words.e: The police passed out posters of the outlaw to all the people.
words.zh: (動) [aʊt'lɔ] 宣布...為非法；禁止　(名) ['aʊt'lɔ] 罪犯；亡命之徒

words.d: A prospect is a possibility that something will happen.
words.e: He wakes up every morning with the prospect of having a good day.
words.zh: (名) 前景；可能性　(動) 探勘；尋找

words.d: A purse is a bag where women keep money, makeup and keys.
words.e: My sister likes to buy designer purses.
words.zh: (名) 錢包；手提包

words.d: A rod is a thin stick made of wood or metal.
words.e: I bought a new fishing rod to use while on vacation.
words.zh: (名) 棒；桿

words.d: If something seldom happens, it doesn’t happen very often.
words.e: It seldom rains in southern Arizona.
words.zh: (副) 很少；不常

words.d: To shave means to cut the hairs on your face with a sharp tool.
words.e: My father shaves his face every day because he doesn’t want a beard.
words.zh: (動) 刮鬍子；剃毛

words.d: If you are terrified, you are extremely scared.
words.e: When I saw the ghost, I was absolutely terrified!
words.zh: 如果你感到驚恐，表示你極度害怕。

words.d: A wizard is a man who can do magic.
words.e: The wizard made gold fall from the sky.
words.zh: (名) 巫師；術士

words.d: Baggage is the set of bags that people take with them when they travel.
words.e: I’m taking several pieces of baggage with me on vacation.
words.zh: (名) 行李；包袱　(名) 累贅；包袱

words.d: A bulb is the glass part of an electric light.
words.e: I had to change the light bulb in my bedroom.
words.zh: (名) 燈泡；球莖

words.d: A bundle is a number of things that are tied together.
words.e: I was shocked when he showed me a bundle of money.
words.zh: (名) 束；包裹　(動) 捆紮；匆匆推動

words.d: Cattle are cows and bulls, especially on a farm.
words.e: The rancher’s cattle were eating the grass in the field.
words.zh: (名) 牛；牲畜　(名) 牲口；家畜

words.d: To flee means to run away from trouble or danger.
words.e: The crowd of men tried to flee from the danger.
words.zh: (動) 逃跑；逃離　(動) 倉皇離去

words.d: To graze means to eat grass.
words.e: The cows grazed in the field.
words.zh: (動) 放牧；吃草　(動) 輕擦；擦傷

words.d: Greed is a desire to have more than the things that you need.
words.e: She ate all the cookies out of her greed.
words.zh: (名) 貪心；貪欲　(形) 貪心的；貪婪的

words.d: A herd is a large group of the same type of animals that live together.
words.e: The herd of cows moved slowly across the ranch.
words.zh: (名) 牛群；獸群　(動) 聚集；驅趕

words.d: To initiate something means to start it.
words.e: You have to turn the switch on to initiate the computer system.
words.zh: (動) 開始；發起　(動) 啟動；著手

words.d: A lane is a small road.
words.e: The lane passes directly in front of our house.
words.zh: (名) 車道；巷弄　(名) 泳道；跑道

words.d: Nerve is bravery or the belief that you can do something.
words.e: He has the nerve to think that he can actually wrestle with a lion.
words.zh: (名) 神經；勇氣　(動) 鼓起勇氣；使膽怯

words.d: An optimist is somebody who sees the good parts of a situation.
words.e: Even though he has physical problems, my brother is an optimist.
words.zh: 樂觀者是看到情況中好的一面的人。

words.d: A parade is a celebration when groups of people walk in the same direction.
words.e: There were many marching bands in the spring parade.
words.zh: (名) 遊行；檢閱　(動) 遊行；列隊行進

words.d: To pave the ground is to lay material on it to make it easier to walk or drive on.
words.e: The path was paved with yellow bricks.
words.zh: (動) 鋪設；鋪路

words.d: A phantom is a ghost or spirit.
words.e: A scary phantom appeared from out of the darkness.
words.zh: 幻影是鬼魂或幽靈。

words.d: Something that is portable is able to be moved or carried easily.
words.e: Since computers are portable, people can use them anywhere.
words.zh: (形) 可攜帶的；輕便的

words.d: A poster is a written announcement that is used to advertise something.
words.e: I saw a poster about a free concert in the park.
words.zh: (名) 海報；廣告

words.d: To scratch is to make small cuts with a claw or fingernail.
words.e: I used a stick to scratch my back.
words.zh: (動) 抓；刮；擦　(名) 抓痕；刮痕

words.d: A symphony is a long piece of music performed by many musicians.
words.e: Alex, a violin player, has always dreamt of playing a symphony.
words.zh: (名) 交響樂；交響曲

words.d: A widow is a woman whose husband has died.
words.e: The widow had no children and was very lonely.
words.zh: (名) 寡婦

words.d: To circulate something is to spread it quickly.
words.e: The fan helped to circulate cool air through the room.
words.zh: (動) 流通；循環　傳播；散布

words.d: Consequent means happening because of a different situation.
words.e: Her consequent rash came after she touched the poison ivy.
words.zh: (形) 隨之而來的；由此產生的　(名) 結果；後果

words.d: To derive something from another source means to get it from that thing.
words.e: Red’s nickname was derived from the color of her hair.
words.zh: (動) 推導；得出　獲得；來自

words.d: To drown is to die from not being able to breathe underwater.
words.e: He would have drowned if the sailors would not have rescued him.
words.zh: (動) 溺水；淹沒　(動) 淹沒；掩蓋

words.d: A dynasty is a series of rulers who are all from the same family.
words.e: The ancient Egyptians had a dynasty that lasted for many years.
words.zh: (名) 朝代；王朝　(名) 家族統治；世家

words.d: A fraction is a small part of something.
words.e: Only a fraction of the cake was gone.
words.zh: (名) 分數；片段　(名) 小部分；微小的量

words.d: Frost is a white layer of ice that forms during very cold weather.
words.e: In the morning, the trees were all covered with frost.
words.zh: (名) 霜；寒霜　(動) 結霜；冷凍　(形) 結霜的；冷的

words.d: An illusion is something that looks real, but doesn’t actually exist.
words.e: Some pictures create an illusion for the eyes.
words.zh: (名) 幻覺；幻想　(動) 迷惑；欺騙

words.d: To invade is to take over a place by force.
words.e: The enemy forces tried to invade our country through the air.
words.zh: (動) 入侵；侵略　(動) 闖入；打擾

words.d: A lieutenant is a rank in the military or police, or a person with that rank.
words.e: The lieutenant was a good leader, and his soldiers respected him.
words.zh: (名) 中尉；副官

words.d: The word marine describes something related to the sea.
words.e: A healthy ocean is full of marine animals.
words.zh: (形) 海洋的；海生的　(名) 海軍；海員

words.d: The merit of something or someone is their good qualities.
words.e: The actor received an award for his merits in the movie.
words.zh: (名) 優點；長處　(動) 值得；應得

words.d: A navy is the part of a country’s military that fights at sea.
words.e: My country is known for our strong navy.
words.zh: (名) 海軍；艦隊　(形) 深藍色的；海軍的

words.d: Polar relates to the cold places on Earth’s north and south ends.
words.e: Only a few people live in the Earth’s northern polar region.
words.zh: (形) 極地的；極端的；相反的

words.d: A ray is a line of light that comes from a bright object.
words.e: The sun’s warm rays covered the beach.
words.zh: (名) 光線；射線

words.d: To resign means to quit a job.
words.e: After I officially resigned from work, I said goodbye to my boss.
words.zh: (動) 辭職；放棄

words.d: Suicide is the act of killing oneself.
words.e: Some people feel so sad that they think suicide is the only answer.
words.zh: (名) 自殺

words.d: To tremble is to shake as a result of cold weather.
words.e: Harry was not used to the cold, so he trembled most of the day.
words.zh: (動) 顫抖；發抖

words.d: When something is underlying, it is a hidden cause of something else.
words.e: Her underlying fear of flying reduced her traveling options.
words.zh: (形) 根本的；潛在的

words.d: To travel via something means to travel through or using something.
words.e: We arrived in the city from the airport via the train.
words.zh: (介) 經由；透過

words.d: To alter something means to make a small change to it.
words.e: I altered the color of my nails to match my hair.
words.zh: (動) 改變；修改　(動) 改小；改大

words.d: If someone stands aside, they are on or to one side.
words.e: The man stood aside and opened the door for me.
words.zh: (副) 在旁邊；另外　(介) 除了...之外　(名) 旁白；題外話

words.d: Autumn is the season of the year between summer and winter.
words.e: I love when the leaves fall in autumn because I can play in them.
words.zh: (名) 秋季；秋天　(形) 秋天的；秋季的

words.d: To blend is to mix two or more things together so that they become one thing.
words.e: My wife blended together all of the ingredients to make a delicious stew.
words.zh: (動) 混合；融合　(名) 混合物；混合品

words.d: To collapse is to fall down suddenly.
words.e: The tree collapsed right in front of our house.
words.zh: (動) 倒塌；崩潰　(名) 倒塌；崩潰

words.d: To crush something is to press it together so its shape is destroyed.
words.e: Selena’s new car was crushed when something fell on top of it.
words.zh: (動) 壓碎；粉碎　(名) 迷戀；暗戀的人

words.d: To curve is to move in a line that bends and does not go straight.
words.e: The road curves to the left and to the right.
words.zh: (名) 曲線；彎曲　(動) 彎曲；轉彎

words.d: If something is disgusting, it is very unpleasant.
words.e: After running all day, Greg’s feet had a disgusting odor.
words.zh: (形) 令人厭惡的；令人討厭的

words.d: A drain is a pipe that carries away water from a building, such as in a kitchen.
words.e: The water in the sink goes down the drain as you wash your hands.
words.zh: (動) 排水；消耗　(名) 排水管；流失

words.d: To embrace is to hug.
words.e: When they saw each other again, the happy couple embraced.
words.zh: (動) 擁抱；接受　(名) 擁抱；懷抱

words.d: To envy someone is to wish that you had something that they have.
words.e: Sally envied the happy couple.
words.zh: (動) 妒忌；羨慕　(名) 妒忌；嫉妒

words.d: Fireworks are objects that create colored lights when they are lit.
words.e: The display of fireworks was so beautiful.
words.zh: 煙火是點燃後會產生彩色光芒的物品。

words.d: Flour is a powder made from plants that is used to make foods like bread.
words.e: I wanted to bake a pie, but I needed flour.
words.zh: (名) 麵粉；粉末

words.d: A fuse is a string that you light on fireworks to make them explode.
words.e: The boy litthe fuse on the rocket and waited for it to burst in the sky.
words.zh: (動) 熔合；融合　(名) 保險絲；導火線

words.d: Ginger is a spice from the root of a plant. It tastes spicy and sweet.
words.e: Ginger is a common ingredient in many dishes from India.
words.zh: (名) 薑；生薑　(形) 薑色的；活潑的

words.d: If you are jealous, you think someone might take something from you.
words.e: Miriam was jealous because Sue was paying too much attention to Jim .
words.zh: (形) 嫉妒的；忌妒的　(形) 警惕的；保護的

words.d: A paste is a thick and smooth substance.
words.e: My son needed some paste for a school project.
words.zh: (動) 黏貼；張貼　(名) 麵團；糊狀物

words.d: A receipt is a paper that proves that something was received or bought.
words.e: After looking at my receipt, I realized that I had spent too much money.
words.zh: (名) 收據；發票　(動) 收到；簽收

words.d: To wipe something is to slide a piece of cloth over it to clean it.
words.e: She wiped the dust from the windows.
words.zh: (動) 擦；拭去

words.d: A wire is a thin string made out of metal.
words.e: The wires were connected to towers that brought electricity to the city.
words.zh: (名) 金屬線；電線　(動) 用金屬線連接；安裝電線

words.d: If you acknowledge something, you accept that it is true or that it exists.
words.e: The teacher acknowledged that the young student was hungry.
words.zh: (動) 承認；認可　(動) 表示感謝；致謝

words.d: An ambassador is a government worker who works in another country.
words.e: The ambassador from Korea was in charge ofthe conference.
words.zh: (名) 大使；外交官　(名) 代表；使者

words.d: If someone is a blonde, they have light-colored hair.
words.e: My cousin is different from me. She is a blonde with blue eyes.
words.zh: (形) 金色的；淺色的　(名) 金髮人；淺色

words.d: To conquer a country means to attack and take control of it.
words.e: The soldiers were trying to conquer the world.
words.zh: (動) 征服；攻佔　(動) 克服；戰勝

words.d: To drag something means to pull it across the ground.
words.e: The dog was dragging his owner down the street.
words.zh: (動) 拖；拉　(動) 緩慢移動；拖曳　(名) 阻力；妨礙

words.d: To exaggerate is to say that something is bigger or better than it really is.
words.e: Jimmy wasn’t exaggerating about the seriousness of his injury.
words.zh: (動) 誇大；誇張　(名) 誇大；夸張的說法

words.d: Heritage is the collection of features of a society, such as language and religion.
words.e: Teepees are part ofthe heritage of the American Indians of the plains.
words.zh: (名) 遺產；文化遺產　(名) 傳統；遺留

words.d: To insult someone is to say things that will hurt their feelings.
words.e: The girls insulted each other all afternoon.
words.zh: (動) [In`s^lt] 侮辱；辱罵　(名) [`Ins^lt] 侮辱；無禮

words.d: Meanwhile means until something happens or while something is happening.
words.e: He wants to be a doctor in the future, but meanwhile, he works a regular job.
words.zh: (副) 同時；其間　(連) 當...的時候；同時

words.d: A necklace is a piece of jewelry that people wear around their necks.
words.e: Joyce received a lovely pearl necklace for her wedding anniversary.
words.zh: (名) 項鍊；頸飾

words.d: A noble is a rich and powerful person.
words.e: The Queen invited a noble from a nearby country to dinner.
words.zh: (形) 高尚的；崇高的　(形) 貴族的；出身高貴的

words.d: When something is precious, it is valuable and important.
words.e: In a desert, water can be more precious than money.
words.zh: (形) 珍貴的；寶貴的

words.d: A prejudice is an unfair opinion about someone before you get to know them.
words.e: The company’s rules against gender prejudice must be enforced.
words.zh: (名) [ˈprɛdʒədɪs] 偏見；歧視　(動) [prɪˈdʒɛdɪs] 使有偏見；損害

words.d: A rumor is a story that may not be true.
words.e: Carla was spreading rumors around the office.
words.zh: (名) 謠言；傳聞

words.d: A sin is something that is wrong for religious reasons.
words.e: Taking something that doesn’t belong to you is a sin.
words.zh: (名) 罪；罪惡

words.d: A spectacle is an amazing sight.
words.e: Niagara Falls is quite a spectacle.
words.zh: (名) 景象；奇觀；眼鏡

words.d: A stack is a pile of different things.
words.e: There was a stack of paperwork on his desk to complete.
words.zh: (動) 堆疊；堆積　(名) 堆；疊

words.d: If someone is suspicious of someone else, they do not trust that person.
words.e: Dad was suspicious ofthe caller on the line.
words.zh: (形) 可疑的；懷疑的

words.d: Tin is a cheap white metal.
words.e: Soup is a common food that is often sold in tin cans.
words.zh: (名) 錫；罐頭；馬口鐵

words.d: A vase is an attractive container where people keep flowers.
words.e: The vase was filled with such lovely flowers.
words.zh: (名) 花瓶

words.d: If something aches, it hurts.
words.e: Mindy’s head ached because she bumped it against the wall.
words.zh: (名) 疼痛；酸痛　(動) 疼痛；酸痛

words.d: If something is arctic, it is from far north near the North Pole.
words.e: It is cold when the arctic winds blow.
words.zh: (形) 北極的；寒冷的

words.d: A canal is a path for water to travel through.
words.e: The city of Venice, Italy, has many canals.
words.zh: (名) 運河；水道　(動) 引導；疏導

words.d: A chemist is a scientist who works with chemicals.
words.e: My mother is a successful chemist who helps invent new products.
words.zh: (名) 藥劑師；化學家

words.d: A chill is a feeling of cold.
words.e: The chill from the cold wind made Bill shiver.
words.zh: (動) 冷卻；放鬆　(形) 涼爽的；酷的

words.d: Congress is a group of leaders in a government.
words.e: Congress makes laws for people to follow.
words.zh: (名) 國會；議會　(名) 會議；集會

words.d: If something is dairy, it is made from milk.
words.e: Things like milk and cheese are dairy foods.
words.zh: (名) 乳製品；牛奶製品　(形) 含乳製品的；與乳製品有關的

words.d: To descend is to go downward.
words.e: I descended the stairs to get out of the building.
words.zh: (動) 下降；下來　(動) 祖先是；源自

words.d: A grocer is a person who sells food.
words.e: Our grocer, Mr. Smith, is a very kind man.
words.zh: (名) 雜貨商；食品店老闆

words.d: To hesitate is to wait for a short time before doing something.
words.e: He hesitated for a moment before he decided which choice to make.
words.zh: (動) 猶豫；遲疑　(名) 猶豫；遲疑

words.d: An institution is an organization that works to help a city or group of people.
words.e: Banks are vital institutions that businesses and people use every day.
words.zh: (名) 機構；制度　(名) 慈善機關；學校

words.d: To jog is to run slowly.
words.e: He jogs every day so he can be healthier.
words.zh: (動) 慢跑；輕推　(名) 慢跑；輕推

words.d: A merchant is a person who sells things.
words.e: The merchant was selling used cars.
words.zh: (名) 商人；貿易商　(形) 商業的；貿易的

words.d: To poke something is to push it with your finger.
words.e: I poked my finger on a thumb tack.
words.zh: (動) 戳；捅　(名) 戳；捅

words.d: To postpone something is to make it happen later than planned.
words.e: They postponed the meeting until Elena arrived.
words.zh: (動) 延緩；延期

words.d: When a liquid splashes, it bursts and hits something.
words.e: Some green paint splashed upon the floor.
words.zh: (名) 水花；濺潑聲　(動) 濺；潑

words.d: If people are stubborn, they don’t change their minds easily.
words.e: Both of my parents are stubborn. They never give in to each other.
words.zh: (形) 固執的；頑固的

words.d: A suburb is a small part of a large city.
words.e: I grew up in the suburbs only a few minutes outside of the city.
words.zh: (名) 郊區；近郊

words.d: The tide is the level of the water in the sea.
words.e: The tide continued to crawl upon the beach little by little.
words.zh: (名) 潮汐；潮流　(動) 順勢而為；渡過

words.d: A tragedy is a very sad event.
words.e: It was a tragedy when his house was struck by lightning and burned.
words.zh: (名) 悲劇；災難

words.d: A bomb is an object that explodes and destroys large areas.
words.e: The bomb will destroy anything that is near.
words.zh: (名) 炸彈；爆彈　(動) 轟炸；投擲炸彈　(動) 砸；失敗

words.d: A certificate is a document that says that something is true or happened.
words.e: I was given a certificate after completing the computer course.
words.zh: (名) 證書；憑證　(動) 證明；認可

words.d: A circumstance is an event that makes a situation what it is.
words.e: There were many circumstances behind their success like hard work.
words.zh: (名) 環境；情況　(名) 條件；因素

words.d: A coffin is a box used to bury dead people.
words.e: When a person passes away, they are usually buried inside of a coffin.
words.zh: (名) 棺材；靈柩

words.d: To cope with a difficult or stressful situation means to deal with it.
words.e: He copes with work stress by exercising three or four times a week.
words.zh: (動) 應對；處理　(動) 克服；忍受

words.d: Criticism is the act of saying that you don’t like or approve of something.
words.e: She had a lot of criticism about their new plan.
words.zh: (名) 批評；評論　(名) 批評意見；挑剔

words.d: To devastate something means to completely destroy it.
words.e: The entire wall was devastated.
words.zh: (動) 嚴重破壞；摧毀　(動) 使極度傷心；打擊

words.d: To frown is to make an unhappy look with your face.
words.e: Melissa frowned when she found out that the party had been cancelled.
words.zh: (動) 皺眉；不悅　(名) 皺眉；不滿的表情

words.d: To gaze at something means to look at it for a long time.
words.e: We used a telescope to gaze at the stars for over an hour.
words.zh: (動) 凝視；注視　(名) 凝視；目光

words.d: To glance at something means to look at it quickly.
words.e: She glanced behind her to see if he was looking at her.
words.zh: (動) 匆匆一瞥；掃一眼　(名) 一瞥；眼神

words.d: Grief is the feeling of deep sadness, usually when a person dies.
words.e: The grief caused by losing her parents was very difficult for her.
words.zh: (名) 悲傷；哀痛　(動) 為…感到悲傷

words.d: A groom is a man who is going to be married.
words.e: The groom looked happy as he walked with his new wife.
words.zh: (名) 新郎；馬夫　(動) 梳洗打扮；培養

words.d: A license is an official document that gives one permission to do something.
words.e: In the United States you need a driver’s license to drive legally.
words.zh: (名) 執照；許可證　(動) 頒發執照；核准

words.d: A microscope is a device that makes small objects look bigger.
words.e: Germs cannot be seen without a microscope.
words.zh: (名) 顯微鏡；放大鏡

words.d: When something is nuclear, it relates to the division or joining of atoms.
words.e: Nuclear power plants provide inexpensive energy to cities.
words.zh: (形) 核的；核能的　(形) 中心的；核心的

words.d: To portray something means to describe it or show it in a picture.
words.e: Her picture portrayed the house she grew up in when she was little.
words.zh: (動) 描繪；扮演

words.d: To rotate something means to turn it around in a circle.
words.e: An airplane’s propellers rotate quickly to help it fly.
words.zh: (動) 旋轉；輪流

words.d: A souvenir is something you buy to remind you of a place or event.
words.e: I bought a Russian doll as a souvenir from my trip to Moscow.
words.zh: (名) 紀念品

words.d: A submarine is a boat that can go underwater for long periods of time.
words.e: The submarine dove under the sea so the enemy couldn’t see it.
words.zh: (名) 潛水艇；海底生物

words.d: To trace something means to follow over it with the eyes or a finger.
words.e: He traced over the graph with his finger.
words.zh: (名) 痕跡；微量　(動) 追蹤；描繪

words.d: An appliance is a piece of equipment used for jobs in the home.
words.e: Many homes have appliances like ovens, toasters and refrigerators.
words.zh: (名) 電器用品；家電　(名) 裝置；器具

words.d: A basin is large bowl for washing things. A sink is sometimes called a basin.
words.e: She filled the basin with water and washed her face.
words.zh: (名) 盆；盆地　(名) 河流的流域；集水區

words.d: A broom is a brush with a long handle used for cleaning floors.
words.e: My father usually uses a broom to sweep away dust in the basement.
words.zh: (名) 掃帚；掃把　(動) 掃；清掃

words.d: A caterpillar is a small insect that looks like a worm and eats plants.
words.e: After eating a lot of leaves, caterpillars change into butterflies.
words.zh: (名) 毛毛蟲；蝴蝶幼蟲

words.d: A cupboard is a piece of furniture that is used to store food or household items.
words.e: We put all of our dishes and food in the cupboards.
words.zh: (名) 碗櫃；食物櫃

words.d: If something or someone is delicate, they are easy to break or harm.
words.e: You should hold the baby carefully because she’s very delicate.
words.zh: (形) 細緻的；精緻的　(形) 虛弱的；脆弱的

words.d: To emerge from something means to come out of it.
words.e: The hand suddenly emerged from the grave.
words.zh: (動) 出現；浮現　(動) 從…中出來；浮出

words.d: A handicap is a condition that limits someone’s mental or physical abilities.
words.e: Joe has a slight handicap, so he uses a walker to get around.
words.zh: (名) 障礙；不利條件　(動) 妨礙；使處於不利地位　(形) 身心障礙的

words.d: A hook is a sharp curved piece of metal used for catching or holding things.
words.e: The fish went after the sharp hook.
words.zh: (名) 鉤子；魚鉤　(動) 用鉤子鉤住；吸引　(片) 打右勾拳

words.d: To hop means to jump a short distance.
words.e: The kangaroo quickly hopped away from danger.
words.zh: (動) 跳躍；單足跳　(名) 跳躍；啤酒花

words.d: Laundry is clothes that have been or need to be washed.
words.e: He folded the clean laundry and put the dirty laundry in a basket.
words.zh: (名) 洗衣服；洗衣店　(名) 待洗衣物；已洗衣物

words.d: To pursue someone or something is to chase or follow them.
words.e: The mother pursued her young child down the hill.
words.zh: (動) 追求；追趕

words.d: If someone is reluctant, they do not want to do something.
words.e: She was reluctant to eat the meager breakfast.
words.zh: (形) 不情願的；勉強的

words.d: Sleeves are the part of a shirt where your arms go.
words.e: Ryan bought a new shirt with long sleeves to keep his arms warm.
words.zh: (名) 袖子

words.d: The spine is the bone that runs up and down the middle of the back.
words.e: Our spine helps us to stand up nice and straight.
words.zh: (名) 脊柱；書脊；刺

words.d: A stain is a dirty mark that is difficult to clean.
words.e: He had a red stain on the collar of his shirt.
words.zh: (名) 污漬；斑點　(動) 玷污；染色

words.d: A strip is a long, narrow piece of material or land.
words.e: He had long strips of film that held images of his trip abroad.
words.zh: (動) 剝去；脫掉　(名) 條；帶

words.d: To swear means to promise to do something.
words.e: I will put my hand on the Bible and swear to do my best for the country.
words.zh: (動) 咒罵；發誓

words.d: To swing something means to move it back and forth or from side to side.
words.e: He can swing a golf club very powerfully.
words.zh: (動) 搖擺；盪鞦韆　(名) 鞦韆；擺動

words.d: To utilize something means to use it for a specific purpose.
words.e: They utilized a pair of scissors to cut the ribbon.
words.zh: 利用某物意指將其用於特定目的。

words.d: An aroma is a scent or smell.
words.e: I love the aroma of coffee in the morning.
words.zh: (名) 香氣；芳香　(形) 有香味的；芬芳的

words.d: A beverage is a drink.
words.e: The waiter brought our beverages first. Then he brought our food.
words.zh: (名) 飲料；飲品

words.d: A cluster of things is a small group of them placed close together.
words.e: She held a large cluster of grapes in her hand.
words.zh: (名) 群；簇；一群　(動) 聚集；群集

words.d: To combine is to join together to make a single thing or group.
words.e: Mina combined peanut butter and jelly to make a sandwich.
words.zh: (動) [kəmˋbaɪn] 結合；合併　(名) [ˋkɑmbaɪn] 聯合收割機

words.d: When a liquid is condensed, it is made thicker.
words.e: One way to make a dessert thick and sweet is to use condensed milk.
words.zh: 液體經濃縮後，會變得更加濃稠。

words.d: When something is contemporary, it is related to the present time.
words.e: Contemporary scientists have learned quite a bit about DNA.
words.zh: (形) 當代的；現代的　(名) 同時代的人；當代人物

words.d: To cultivate plants is to care for them and help them grow.
words.e: A research company is cultivating new kinds of rice to aid poor countries.
words.zh: (動) 培養；栽培　(動) 種植；耕種

words.d: When something is divine, it is related to gods.
words.e: Legends say that music was given to men as a divine gift from the gods.
words.zh: (形) 神聖的；神的　(動) 預測；猜測

words.d: When it is humid, there is a lot of water in the air.
words.e: It is very humid inside of a sauna.
words.zh: (形) 潮濕的；濕度高的　(形) 悶熱的；濕悶的

words.d: An odor is a very distinct smell.
words.e: He knew there was a leak when he noticed the strong odor of natural gas.
words.zh: (名) 氣味；臭味　(名) 香氣；芳香

words.d: The palate is the top part of the mouth.
words.e: You can touch your palate with your tongue.
words.zh: (名) 味覺；口味

words.d: Paradise is the place or condition of happiness where things are perfect.
words.e: My vacation in Hawaii was like being in paradise.
words.zh: (名) 天堂；樂園

words.d: A plantation is a big farm that only grows certain kinds of crops.
words.e: In the 1800s, there were many cotton plantations in the southern US.
words.zh: (名) 種植園；殖民地；大農場

words.d: When something is rapid, it moves or changes very quickly.
words.e: His mother was surprised by her son’s rapid growth.
words.zh: (形) 快速的；迅速的

words.d: A rate is the speed at which something happens.
words.e: Grass tends to grow at a very slow rate.
words.zh: (名) 速率；比率　(動) 評價；評定等級

words.d: When something is soothing, it makes you calm or relaxed.
words.e: The soothing music helped the baby fall asleep.
words.zh: 當某事物令人舒緩時，它能使你平靜或放鬆。

words.d: When something is subtle, it is not easy to see or notice.
words.e: The handsome man has a subtle smile.
words.zh: (形) 微妙的；細微的

words.d: The texture of something is the way its surface looks and feels.
words.e: The texture of a rock found in the water is typically very smooth.
words.zh: (名) 質地；口感

words.d: When something is toxic, it is poisonous. Toxic things are very dangerous.
words.e: Please check the label to see if the product is toxic.
words.zh: (形) 有毒的；中毒的

words.d: To vary means to be different from another thing in size or amount.
words.e: The heights of the people in my class vary by a large amount.
words.zh: (動) 變化；不同

words.d: An accident is an unexpected undesirable event.
words.e: She had to go to the hospital after she was in a serious car accident.
words.zh: (名) 意外；事故

words.d: An admiral is someone who controls many military ships.
words.e: They won the sea battle because of the admiral’s great leadership.
words.zh: (名) 海軍上將；旗艦司令官

words.d: An arc is a curved shape.
words.e: A rainbow has the shape of an arc.
words.zh: (名) 弧；弧線　(動) 形成弧形；彎曲

words.d: Your character is your personality.
words.e: My sister’s character is fun and very outgoing.
words.zh: (名) 性格；個性　(名) 人物；角色　(名) 品德；道德

words.d: Your conscience is your inner sense of what is right and wrong.
words.e: I recycle everything I can, so my conscience is clear!
words.zh: (名) 良心；道德感　(名) 道德意識；自我約束

words.d: If something is fiery, it is burning strongly.
words.e: The fiery blaze burned all night long.
words.zh: (形) 熾熱的；炎熱的　(形) 激烈的；激情的

words.d: Flesh is the skin, muscle and fat on your body.
words.e: The zebra’s flesh has black and white stripes.
words.zh: (名) 肉；肉體　(動) 使豐滿；充實　(形) 肉色的；淡紅色的

words.d: A grapefruit is a fruit similar to an orange, but bigger and not as sweet.
words.e: Would you like a grapefruit with your breakfast?
words.zh: (名) 葡萄柚；柚子

words.d: Hay is dry grass used to feed animals or used as a covering.
words.e: I need to buy some more hay for the horse to sleep on.
words.zh: (名) 乾草；牧草

words.d: If you are horrified, you are very shocked and feel upset.
words.e: I was horrified when I read about the old lady who was attacked.
words.zh: (形) 非常害怕的；驚恐的　(動過) 使...感到恐懼；嚇壞

words.d: Kerosene is a type of oil. It is used in some lamps and stoves.
words.e: Many people in poor countries cook on kerosene stoves.
words.zh: 煤油是一種油，用於某些燈具和爐具。

words.d: A loop is a line made into the shape of a circle.
words.e: He made a loop with the rope and placed it over the post.
words.zh: (名) 迴圈；環形　(動) 環繞；循環

words.d: A paddle is a piece of wood or plastic that moves a boat across water.
words.e: We need a paddle to help us move across the water.
words.zh: (名) 槳；槳板　(動) 用槳划船；涉水

words.d: A raft is a floating platform made from pieces of wood tied together.
words.e: The man made a raft out of bamboo and floated out to sea.
words.zh: (名) 木筏；橡皮艇

words.d: When something is sour, it has a sharp and unpleasant taste.
words.e: I don’t like lemons because I think they are too sour.
words.zh: (形) 酸的；酸味的

words.d: A stake is a small, sharp piece of wood or metal that is put into the ground.
words.e: We marked our property by placing stakes into the ground.
words.zh: (動) 賭注；以...打賭　(名) 樁；股份

words.d: A steward is a person like a waiter who serves food on planes and ships.
words.e: The steward is bringing some tea.
words.zh: (名) 乘務員；服務員

words.d: String is a thin piece of fabric or rope.
words.e: I found a large ball of string.
words.zh: (名) 繩子；線　(動) 串起；繫

words.d: A thorn is a sharp part of a plant.
words.e: Be careful of the thorns when you pick the roses!
words.zh: (名) 刺；荊棘

words.d: To wreck something means to destroy or ruin it.
words.e: The teenagers wrecked the house for no reason at all.
words.zh: (動) 毀壞；使失事

words.d: To admonish someone is to tell them you disapprove of their behavior.
words.e: The teacher admonished Mark because he was chewing gum in class.
words.zh: 告誡某人是指告訴對方你不贊同其行為。

words.d: If something is audible, then it is able to be heard.
words.e: The sound of the drums was audible from miles away.
words.zh: (形) 能聽到的；可聽見的

words.d: If something or someone is awesome, they are impressive or frightening.
words.e: The huge military plane was an awesome sight.
words.zh: (形) 令人驚歎的；極好的　(形) 令人敬畏的；了不起的

words.d: To beware means to be careful of something or someone that is dangerous.
words.e: You should beware of driving fast on wet roads.
words.zh: (動) 當心；提防　(片) 小心；留意

words.d: To brag means to talk of one’s abilities or achievements in a proud way.
words.e: He had strong muscles and bragged about it to the entire class.
words.zh: (動) 吹牛；自誇　(名) 吹牛；誇耀

words.d: If someone is conscious of something, then they are aware of it.
words.e: The new student was conscious of the other students staring at her.
words.zh: (形) 有意識的；清醒的　(形) 意識到的；察覺到的

words.d: To disagree with someone means to have a different opinion from them.
words.e: The lawyers disagreed about the best way to settle the case.
words.zh: (動) 不同意；反對　(動) 意見不合；爭執

words.d: To echo means that a sound repeats itself because it bounced off an object.
words.e: The child yelled over the canyon, and the wall echoed the sound.
words.zh: (名) 回聲；迴音　(動) 發出回聲；重複

words.d: If something is eventual, it will happen at the end of a series of events.
words.e: The constant training and planning led the team to an eventual victory.
words.zh: (副) 最終地；終於　(形) 最後的；終極的

words.d: A hint is information that suggests something will happen or is true.
words.e: I quietly passed on a hint to my sister about the test.
words.zh: (名) 提示；暗示　(動) 暗示；提示

words.d: An idiot is a person who is not smart or who has done something silly.
words.e: Because he got lost in the forest, the man felt like an idiot.
words.zh: (名) 白痴；傻瓜　(形) 愚蠢的；傻的

words.d: If something is immense, it is very large.
words.e: An immense amount of money was needed to buy such a large boat.
words.zh: (形) 極大的；廣闊的　(形) 龐大的；無邊無際的

words.d: If something is indirect, then it is not the easiest or straightest way.
words.e: He chose to take the most indirect route to the coast.
words.zh: (形) 間接的；不直接的　(副) 間接地；迂迴地

words.d: An option is a choice between two or more things.
words.e: The children were given the option of three houses to pick from.
words.zh: (名) 選擇；選項　(名) 可能性；機會

words.d: A pastime is an activity done for fun that you do often.
words.e: In the US, baseball is considered the national pastime.
words.zh: (名) 消遣；娛樂

words.d: If something is perfect, then it is without any mistakes.
words.e: She got all the questions right, so her score was perfect.
words.zh: (形) [ˈpɝfɪkt] 完美的；完全的　(動) [pɚˈfɛkt] 使完美

words.d: To pinpoint something means to locate it exactly.
words.e: The navigation system in my car is able to pinpoint my exact location.
words.zh: (動) 精確定位；準確找出

words.d: To switch means to change something to something else.
words.e: Mom switched the TV station from the news to her favorite show.
words.zh: (動) 轉變；交換　(名) 開關；改變

words.d: If something or someone is thorough, then they are complete in every way.
words.e: Tina did a thorough job of cleaning the stains out of the carpet.
words.zh: (形) 徹底的；詳盡的

words.d: To torment someone means to cause them to suffer on purpose.
words.e: She tormented her little brother by taking his favorite toy.
words.zh: (動) [tɔr'mɛnt] 折磨；使痛苦　(名) ['tɔrmɛnt] 折磨；痛苦

words.d: A beak is the hard curved part of a bird’s mouth.
words.e: The bird used her beak to dig a small hole in the ground.
words.zh: (名) 鳥嘴；喙

words.d: If something is damp, it is somewhat wet.
words.e: My hair is still damp from my shower.
words.zh: (形) 潮濕的；微溼的　(動) 使潮濕；抑制

words.d: To disapprove of something is to think that it is wrong.
words.e: Alex, disapproved of his son’s behavior.
words.zh: (動) 不贊成；反對　不同意；不滿意

words.d: You use except to talk about the only thing that a statement does not apply to.
words.e: Everyone had fun on the trip except Jim, who felt ill the whole time.
words.zh: (動) 除外；排除　(介) 除了；除…外

words.d: A flight is an act of flying, often in an airplane.
words.e: The airplane made a flight around the world.
words.zh: (名) 飛行；航班　(名) 逃跑；逃離　(動) 飛；飛行

words.d: When someone is fond of something, they like it.
words.e: I am very fond of chocolate cake.
words.zh: (形) 喜愛的；喜歡的　(動) 喜歡；愛好

words.d: When something or someone is immoral, they are evil or do bad things.
words.e: Stealing money from one’s mother is an immoral act.
words.zh: (形) 不道德的；缺乏品德的　(形) 邪惡的；不正當的

words.d: Ivy is a plant with long vines that grows upward typically on walls.
words.e: The walls of the castle are covered with ivy.
words.zh: (名) 常春藤；葉藤　(形) 覆滿常春藤的；被葉藤包裹的

words.d: To moan is to make a low sound when feeling pain or sadness.
words.e: The sick man moaned from his bed.
words.zh: (動) 呻吟；抱怨　(名) 呻吟聲；哭訴

words.d: If someone is oblivious of something, they are unaware of it.
words.e: I was late to school because I was oblivious of the time.
words.zh: (形) 不注意的；沒有察覺的　(形) 遺忘的；健忘的

words.d: To perish means to die, usually because of bad conditions or bad events.
words.e: Many people perish in wars.
words.zh: (動) 消亡；毀滅

words.d: A pit is a big hole in the ground.
words.e: Michael fell into the pit and needed some help to get out.
words.zh: (名) 坑；洞　(動) 使…有坑；與…較量

words.d: The rim is the outside edge of a round thing, like a cup or bottle.
words.e: I put my lips to the rim of the bottle and took a drink.
words.zh: (名) 邊緣；輪緣

words.d: A roost is a place where birds can land and rest or sleep.
words.e: The family of doves made a roost at the top of the tree.
words.zh: 棲木是鳥類可以降落、休息或睡覺的地方。

words.d: If something is slippery, it is wet or smooth and causes people to slip.
words.e: The repairman fell down on the floor because it was too slippery.
words.zh: (形) 滑的；狡猾的

words.d: To soar means to fly high in the air.
words.e: As the sun rose, two eagles soared through the sky.
words.zh: (動) 翱翔；急升

words.d: If something is trivial, it is not important or meaningful.
words.e: Television is trivial, but learning to read is very important.
words.zh: (形) 瑣碎的；不重要的

words.d: If something is typical, it is normal for a certain type of thing.
words.e: Barking is typical behavior for dogs.
words.zh: (形) 典型的；平常的

words.d: If something is utterly a way, it is completely that way.
words.e: When the sun was blocked by the moon, it was utterly dark outside.
words.zh: 若某事物完全如此，便是徹底如此。

words.d: To weep means to cry.
words.e: My sister told me the dramatic movie would make me weep.
words.zh: (動) 哭泣；流淚

words.d: If you do something awhile, you do it for a short time.
words.e: Wait here awhile, and I’ll bring some tea.
words.zh: (副) 一陣子；暫時　(名) 一段時間；一會兒

words.d: Cyberspace is the imaginary place of the Internet where activities occur.
words.e: I didn’t receive your email. It probably got lost in cyberspace.
words.zh: 網路空間是網際網路中發生活動的虛擬場所。

words.d: To edit means to correct a piece of writing so that it is suitable to be published.
words.e: She asked her brother to edit her paper before she submitted it to her teacher.
words.zh: (動) 編輯；修改　(名) 編輯版本；版本

words.d: An essay is a short piece of writing on a certain subject.
words.e: She had to write a two-page essay for her English class.
words.zh: (名) [ˈɛse] 短文；論文　(動) [ɛˈse] 嘗試；試驗

words.d: To evaluate something means to study it, so a decision can be made.
words.e: Joe and Ken evaluated the plan and agreed that it would work.
words.zh: (動) 評估；評價　(動) 計算；求值

words.d: To faint means to go unconscious and fall down.
words.e: Carol fainted because she hadn’t eaten in over a day.
words.zh: (形) 微弱的；昏迷的　(動) 昏倒；暈厥

words.d: If something is global, it happens all around the world.
words.e: Pollution is a global problem.
words.zh: (形) 全球的；全世界的　(形) 整體的；全面的

words.d: A gymnasium is a building with equipment that you can use to get exercise.
words.e: When I go to the gymnasium, I use the treadmill for 20 minutes.
words.zh: 體育館是備有器材、可供運動的建築。

words.d: To highlight something means to mark it with a color so that it is easy to see.
words.e: I read my vocabulary list and highlighted the most difficult words.
words.zh: (動) 強調；突出　(名) 最精彩的部分；亮點

words.d: If someone is ignorant about something, they have no knowledge about it.
words.e: I’m a bit ignorant about his theories. Can you explain them to me?
words.zh: (形) 無知的；缺乏知識的　(形) 不了解的；沒有意識到的

words.d: An index is a list of words at the end of a book that gives information.
words.e: If you look in the index, you’ll find the right page number.
words.zh: (名) 索引；指數　(動) 編製索引；標明

words.d: A lecture is a long, educational speech.
words.e: His lecture on world hunger was very informative.
words.zh: (名) 講座；演講　(動) 給...上課；講演

words.d: A moral is a message at the end of a story that teaches you something.
words.e: At the end of the story, Mother explained the moral to the children.
words.zh: (形) 道德的；倫理的　(名) 品格；道德教訓

words.d: To operate means to work or function.
words.e: I’m sorry, but the trains are not operating today.
words.zh: (動) 操作；運作　(動) 經營；管理

words.d: If something is private, it is only used by one person or group.
words.e: Don’t look at my diary! It’s private!
words.zh: (形) 私人的；私有的

words.d: When something is recent, it happened a short time ago.
words.e: I did not know what caused his recent behavior.
words.zh: (形) 最近的；近期的

words.d: A resolution is a personal decision.
words.e: At New Year, I made a list of resolutions to help me have a better year.
words.zh: (名) 決心；解決

words.d: A semester is a portion of a school year.
words.e: Jack is in his second semester of college.
words.zh: (名) 學期

words.d: If something is typewritten, it is written on a computer or typewriter.
words.e: This is an important, formal project and must be typewritten.
words.zh: 若某物是打字而成的，則表示以電腦或打字機書寫。

words.d: When something is weird, it is very strange.
words.e: My best friend’s dad is a bit weird.
words.zh: (形) 奇怪的；古怪的

words.d: If something is absolute, it is total or complete.
words.e: My presentation was an absolute disaster! It was terrible!
words.zh: (形) 絕對的；完全的　(副) 絕對地；完全地

words.d: Alas is a word that people say when something bad happens.
words.e: I looked everywhere for my purse, but alas, I couldn’t find it.
words.zh: 「唉」是人們在發生壞事時會說出的詞。

words.d: If you are attentive, you are careful and you pay attention.
words.e: He is a good student because he is attentive to every detail on his work.
words.zh: (形) 專注的；細心的　(形) 留意的；留神的

words.d: A cape is a large cloth that covers one’s back and is tied around the neck.
words.e: The superhero wore a long red cape.
words.zh: (名) 海角；岬角　(名) 斗篷；披肩

words.d: To envision something means to imagine that it may happen.
words.e: I can envision my mom’s face when I give her this present!
words.zh: (動) 想像；設想　(動) 展望；預見

words.d: When something happens evenly, it happens at the same rate or level.
words.e: The food was handed out evenly among the hungry people.
words.zh: (副) 均勻地；平均地　(副) 平靜地；冷靜地

words.d: Folk are common or average people.
words.e: I’ve invited all the folks from our street to a gathering at my place.
words.zh: (名) 民俗；民間文化　(形) 民間的；民俗的

words.d: To melt means to turn from a solid to a liquid.
words.e: The snowman that we made yesterday melted in the sun.
words.zh: (動) 融化；溶化　(動) 消散；逐漸消失

words.d: A patch is a part of a surface that is different in appearance from the rest.
words.e: There was one small patch of grass in the sand-covered desert.
words.zh: (名) 補丁；一小片　(動) 修補；連接

words.d: Pleasure is a feeling of happiness.
words.e: The student smiled with pleasure when she received the prize.
words.zh: (名) 樂趣；愉快

words.d: A pop is a short, loud sound.
words.e: I heard a loud pop; then my computer screen shattered.
words.zh: (名) 流行音樂；砰的一聲　(動) 突然出現；發出砰聲

words.d: A pudding is a sweet dessert.
words.e: Would you like some chocolate pudding and coffee for dessert?
words.zh: (名) 布丁

words.d: A rail is a horizontal bar made of metal or wood.
words.e: The cat was sitting on the rail of the fence.
words.zh: (名) 鐵軌；欄杆

words.d: A recipe is a set of instructions to make food.
words.e: The apple cake that you made is really tasty. Can I have the recipe, please?
words.zh: (名) 食譜；處方

words.d: A role is the normal purpose or function of a person or thing.
words.e: My role at work is to check the quality of the products.
words.zh: (名) 角色；作用

words.d: To shrink means to get smaller.
words.e: If you stick to a healthy diet, your stomach will shrink!
words.zh: (動) 縮小；縮水

words.d: To soak something means to make it very wet.
words.e: You need to soak these beans overnight before you cook them.
words.zh: (動) 浸泡；浸濕

words.d: A spark is a small, quick flash of fire.
words.e: As he welded the two pieces of metal together, sparks flew everywhere.
words.zh: (名) 火花；火星　(動) 引發；激起

words.d: A spirit is someone’s feelings and personality, but not a physical body part.
words.e: He has a very outgoing spirit. He can make friends with anyone.
words.zh: (名) 精神；心靈　(動) 鼓舞；鼓勵

words.d: A suit is a set of clothes. It usually includes a jacket with pants or a skirt.
words.e: I wore my new suit on my date with Melissa.
words.zh: (名) 套裝；西裝　(動) 適合；合身

words.d: An account with a bank is an arrangement to keep one’s money there.
words.e: After I paid for the new car, my bank account was nearly empty.
words.zh: (名) 帳戶；帳號　(動) 說明；解釋

words.d: An architect is a person who designs buildings.
words.e: The plans for building the new home were drawn by a famous architect.
words.zh: (名) 建築師；設計者　(動) 設計；規劃

words.d: To conceal something means to hide it.
words.e: You should conceal your money so no one can take it from you.
words.zh: (動) 隱藏；隱瞞　(動) 遮掩；掩蓋

words.d: A crime is something bad that a person does that can be punished by law.
words.e: Police quickly arrived at the scene of the crime.
words.zh: (名) 犯罪；罪行　(名) 犯罪行為；嚴重違法

words.d: A deed is a certificate that proves that someone owns something.
words.e: When she bought the car, she was given a deed to show the car was hers.
words.zh: (名) 行為；事蹟　(動) 做；行動

words.d: Gratitude is a feeling of being thankful.
words.e: The kids showed Aunt Tess much gratitude for visiting them.
words.zh: (名) 感謝；感激　(形) 感謝的；感激的

words.d: A habitat is the natural home of animals or plants.
words.e: Frogs are often found in a wet habitat, such as near a lake, river, or pond.
words.zh: (名) 棲息地；生活環境

words.d: To intervene means to help stop a problem between two people or groups.
words.e: The students argued until the teacher intervened.
words.zh: (動) 干預；介入　(動) 調停；仲裁

words.d: A landmark is an object that helps people find or remember a location.
words.e: The tall tree was used as a landmark for people to find the road to the inn.
words.zh: (名) 地標；landmark　(名) 里程碑；重要事件

words.d: If something is legal, it is related to the law or allowed by the law.
words.e: It was not legal for him to drive until he was eighteen years old.
words.zh: (形) 合法的；法律的　(形) 正當的；有效的

words.d: If something is memorable, then it is remembered for a special reason.
words.e: The party was memorable; people were still talking about it years later.
words.zh: (形) 難忘的；值得記住的　(形) 令人印象深刻的；顯著的

words.d: To oblige someone means to require them to do something.
words.e: If I wanted to have playtime, I was obliged to clean my room once a week.
words.zh: (動) 幫忙；協助　(動) 迫使；強制

words.d: An offense is an action that breaks the law and requires punishment.
words.e: He was put in jail for two days for the offense.
words.zh: (名) 冒犯；得罪　(名) 犯罪行為；違法行為

words.d: To proclaim something means to say it in public.
words.e: The army general proclaimed that the war was won.
words.zh: (動) 宣布；聲明

words.d: A rally is a large public meeting in order to support something.
words.e: The school had a rally in the gym to support the basketball team.
words.zh: (名) 集會；恢復　(動) 召集；恢復

words.d: To resolve something means to find a solution.
words.e: She resolved the problem with her children by giving them both a toy.
words.zh: (動) 決心；解決

words.d: Resources are a person or country’s money and materials that they can use.
words.e: The poor man didn’t have the resources to feed himself.
words.zh: (名) 資源；財力

words.d: A sentence is punishment given to someone who didn’t follow the law.
words.e: The sentence for stealing a car is much worse than for stealing candy.
words.zh: (名) 句子；判決　(動) 判刑

words.d: To volunteer means to offer to do something for free.
words.e: Many people volunteered to help the adults learn to read.
words.zh: (動) 自願做；義務服務　(名) 志願者；義工

words.d: A witness is someone who sees or hears a crime or accident happen.
words.e: The woman was the only witness of the horrible crime.
words.zh: (動) 見證；目擊　(名) 證人；目擊者

words.d: Access is the right to enter or use something.
words.e: The manager was the only person with access to the password.
words.zh: (名) 入口；使用權　(動) 存取；進入

words.d: Conduct is the way that someone acts.
words.e: She was punished for her bad conduct.
words.zh: (名) ['kɑndʌkt] 行為；舉止　(動) [kən'dʌkt] 進行；指揮

words.d: When an event or action is constant, it happens a lot or all the time.
words.e: The television at home is in constant use.
words.zh: (形) 持續的；不變的　(名) 常數；常量

words.d: A crack is a narrow space between the parts of something broken.
words.e: The old window was covered with cracks.
words.zh: (動) 裂開；破裂　(名) 裂縫；裂痕

words.d: A device is an object or a machine.
words.e: A thermometer is a device that tells temperature.
words.zh: (名) 裝置；設備　(名) 方法；手段

words.d: To enclose something is to contain it.
words.e: The cows in the field were enclosed by a fence.
words.zh: (動) 圍住；封閉　(動) 附上；隨信附寄

words.d: To grip something is to hold it very tightly.
words.e: I was scared, so I gripped my older sister’s hand.
words.zh: (名) 抓握；握力　(動) 緊握；吸引　(形) 引人入勝的；緊握的

words.d: To halt is to stop moving.
words.e: The criminal halted when he saw the police coming.
words.zh: (動) 停止；中止　(名) 停止；暫停

words.d: If something is impending, it is going to happen soon.
words.e: The student was nervous about his impending test.
words.zh: 如果某事即將發生，表示它很快就會發生。

words.d: To influence someone or something is to have an effect over them.
words.e: My friend influenced my decision to attend Terrance University.
words.zh: (名) 影響；影響力　(動) 影響；左右

words.d: A law is a rule made by the legislative body.
words.e: The students learned about different laws during social studies class.
words.zh: (名) 法律；法令　(動) 控告；起訴

words.d: A mode is a setting or condition on a machine.
words.e: Mother turned the TV to quiet mode while she talked on the phone.
words.zh: (名) 方式；模式　(名) 時尚；款式　(動) 塑造；模擬

words.d: To perspire means to sweat.
words.e: I usually perspire a lot when I am at practice.
words.zh: (動) 流汗；出汗

words.d: To replace something is to put it in the place of something else.
words.e: I replaced the tire on my car because it was flat.
words.zh: (動) 取代；替換

words.d: To snap something means to break it suddenly, which causes a loud noise.
words.e: I took the stick and snapped it with my hands.
words.zh: (動) 折斷；發出折斷聲　(名) 啪嗒聲；快照

words.d: If a person or animal is sly, they are sneaky or good at tricking people.
words.e: The sly fox stole the eggs from the nest.
words.zh: (形) 狡猾的；狡詐的

words.d: To tend to do something is to be likely to do it or to do it often.
words.e: My mom tends to buy me the perfect gift each Christmas.
words.zh: (動) 傾向於；照顧

words.d: When something is valid, it is correct or based on good reasoning.
words.e: The expert’s opinion on the subject was more valid than others.
words.zh: (形) 有效的；合理的

words.d: A version is an account of something that differs slightly from the original.
words.e: She read the students British version of the Chinese fairy tale.
words.zh: (名) 版本；說法

words.d: You use whatsoever after a noun to emphasize that there is nothing of that thing.
words.e: The boy had no idea whatsoever how to solve the story problem.
words.zh: (副) 絲毫；任何（用於否定句或疑問句，加強語氣）　(代) 無論什麼

words.d: If something is alongside another thing, then it is next to it.
words.e: We work alongside each other in the office.
words.zh: (介) 沿著；在...旁邊　(副) 一起；並肩

words.d: Your appetite is your hunger for food.
words.e: Tom has a big appetite. He eats all the time.
words.zh: (名) 食慾；胃口　(名) 慾望；渴望

words.d: To assist someone is to help them.
words.e: Andrew assisted me with my homework.
words.zh: (動) 幫助；協助　(名) 助手；輔助

words.d: A breeze is a soft wind.
words.e: The breeze caused the leaves to fall off the tree.
words.zh: (名) 微風；輕風　(動) 輕輕吹過；輕鬆行動

words.d: To defy someone is to work against them or refuse to do what they say.
words.e: The students got into trouble for defying their teacher’s rules.
words.zh: (動) 違抗；反抗　(動) 挑戰；不畏懼

words.d: To display something is to show it, especially by putting it in a certain place.
words.e: The museum displayed many wonderful paintings.
words.zh: (動) 展示；陳列　(名) 展覽；表現

words.d: If something or someone is efficient, they do not waste energy.
words.e: My car is very efficient. I rarely have to buy gas.
words.zh: (形) 有效率的；高效的　(形) 節省的；省力的

words.d: If someone is feeble, they are small or weak.
words.e: The boy was too feeble to carry the object very far.
words.zh: (形) 虛弱的；無力的　(形) 微弱的；淡薄的

words.d: To forgive someone is to stop being angry with them.
words.e: Sandra forgave Peter after he said he was sorry.
words.zh: (動) 原諒；寬恕　(動) 免除（債務、罪責）

words.d: If someone is lively, they have a lot of energy.
words.e: Jennifer is very lively: she’s always running and playing.
words.zh: (形) 活潑的；充滿生氣的　(形) 熱鬧的；生動的

words.d: If something is majestic, it is large and beautiful.
words.e: The rich people lived in a big, majestic house.
words.zh: (形) 雄偉的；莊嚴的　(形) 堂皇的；壯麗的

words.d: You use nor to connect two negative ideas.
words.e: I eat neither apples nor oranges. I don’t like either one.
words.zh: (連) 也不；既不　(連) 也沒有

words.d: If someone is outraged, they are very angry.
words.e: My father was outraged when he saw that I had crashed his car.
words.zh: (形) 憤怒的；憤慨的

words.d: If someone is pessimistic, they believe that the worst will happen.
words.e: John is pessimistic. He always thinks something bad will happen.
words.zh: (形) 悲觀的

words.d: A rumor is something people talk about even though it may not be true.
words.e: Kevin was spreading rumors about Marcia to everyone.
words.zh: (名) 謠言；傳聞

words.d: To slap someone means to hit them with the palm of the hand.
words.e: Out of anger, Helen slapped Eunice on the face.
words.zh: (動) 打耳光；拍打　(名) 一巴掌；拍打聲

words.d: To smash something is to break it into many small pieces.
words.e: Jacob smashed the window with a rock.
words.zh: (動) 粉碎；猛擊　(名) 扣殺；破碎聲

words.d: A subject is the topic that is being discussed or taught.
words.e: The subject of Marco’s speech was the economy.
words.zh: (名) [ˋsʌbdʒɪkt] 主題；科目　(動) [səbˋdʒɛkt] 使服從；使遭受

words.d: A wage is the money that a person gets for doing a job.
words.e: The wages I receive from my job are really great!
words.zh: (名) 工資；薪資

words.d: You use whereas to show how two things are different.
words.e: My sister loves horror movies, whereas I prefer comedies.
words.zh: (連) 然而；鑑於

words.d: When something is animate, it has life.
words.e: Rocks and machines are not animate things.
words.zh: (動) [ˋænəmet] 使有生氣；使活躍　(形) [ˋænəmɪt] 生動的；活潑的

words.d: To classify things is to put them into groups based on their type.
words.e: The biologist classified the plant as a completely new species.
words.zh: (動) 分類；歸類　(動) 列為機密的

words.d: To concede is to admit that something is true against your wish.
words.e: The student conceded that he had cheated on the test.
words.zh: (動) 承認；讓步　(動) 認輸；退縮

words.d: A concept is an idea about something.
words.e: I learned some concepts of molecules before working in the science lab.
words.zh: (名) 概念；想法　(名) 概括；觀念

words.d: To construct something means to make or build it.
words.e: The men used wood and metal to construct a house.
words.zh: (名) ['kɑnstrʌkt] 概念；構想　(動) [kən'strʌkt] 建造；構造

words.d: A decade is a period often years.
words.e: She celebrated her three decades of work with the company.
words.zh: (名) 十年；十個

words.d: A diagram is a simple drawing that explains what something is or how it works.
words.e: By following the diagram, I was able to put the desk together.
words.zh: (名) 圖表；圖解　(動) 用圖表表示；繪製圖表

words.d: A ferry is a boat that carries passengers over short distances.
words.e: The ferry took the people across the lake.
words.zh: (名) 渡輪；擺渡船　(動) 用渡輪運送；擺渡

words.d: If something is handy, it is useful.
words.e: An eraser is handy if you make a lot of mistakes.
words.zh: (形) 方便的；易用的　(形) 靈巧的；有用的

words.d: To isolate is to separate one person or thing from a group.
words.e: The teacher isolated the bad child from the class before talking with her.
words.zh: (動) 隔離；孤立　(動) 分離；區分

words.d: A longing is a strong feeling of wanting.
words.e: Since he skipped breakfast, he had a longing for food all morning.
words.zh: 渴望是一種強烈的想要感覺。

words.d: If something is numerous, there are many of those things.
words.e: It was hard to drive fast since there were numerous holes in the road.
words.zh: (形) 眾多的；許多的　(形) 數量龐大的；無數的

words.d: A particle is a very small piece of something.
words.e: The bottles of wine were covered in a layer of dust particles.
words.zh: (名) 粒子；微粒

words.d: A plea is a request that is urgent or emotional.
words.e: The poor, hungry man made a plea for food.
words.zh: (名) 懇求；請求

words.d: To refrain from something is to avoid doing it.
words.e: The doctor asked Mary to refrain from eating fast food as part of her diet.
words.zh: (動) 克制；避免

words.d: A review of something is a formal inspection of it by people in authority.
words.e: The government ordered a careful review of the economic situation.
words.zh: (動) 複習；檢討　(名) 複習；評論

words.d: If someone is sophisticated, they know many things about the world.
words.e: Jake is one of the most sophisticated persons I’ve ever met.
words.zh: (形) 複雜精密的；高雅的

words.d: To surrender something is to give it up.
words.e: The thief surrendered the money to the police when he was caught.
words.zh: (動) 投降；放棄

words.d: If something is upright, it is standing up straight.
words.e: Meerkats can’t walk like humans, but they can stand upright.
words.zh: (形) 正直的；垂直的　(副) 垂直地；挺直地

words.d: If something is worthwhile, it is important or useful.
words.e: On his visit to Canada, he realized studying English was worthwhile.
words.zh: (形) 值得的；有價值的

words.d: An alliance is a group of people who work together.
words.e: The junior executives formed an alliance with each other.
words.zh: (名) 聯盟；同盟　(動) 結成聯盟；聯合

words.d: Applause is the noise made when people clap hands to show approval.
words.e: At the end of his speech, everyone gave applause.
words.zh: (名) 掌聲；喝彩　(動) 為…鼓掌；喝彩

words.d: If you are armed, you are carrying a weapon.
words.e: The robber was armed with a gun.
words.zh: (形) 武裝的；帶武器的　(動) 裝備；武裝

words.d: If something is authoritative, it uses the best information available.
words.e: This book is an authoritative source on Ancient Egypt’s Queen Nefertiti.
words.zh: (形) 權威的；有權勢的　(形) 確定的；有信服力的

words.d: A ceremony is an event that happens on special occasions.
words.e: Tom and Amy’s marriage ceremony is in June.
words.zh: (名) 儀式；典禮　(名) 正式程序；禮儀

words.d: Culture is the music, art, and writings of a certain place or group of people.
words.e: Ancient Mexican culture is renowned for its architecture.
words.zh: (名) 文化；文明　(名) 修養；素質

words.d: A defense is something that is done to protect another thing.
words.e: The airforce works in defense of its country.
words.zh: (名) 防守；防衛　(名) 辯護；答辯

words.d: A detail is a small piece of information.
words.e: Jane told Ben all the details of her trip to China.
words.zh: (名) 細節；詳情　(動) 詳述；說明細節

words.d: When a group of things is diverse, it is made up of a wide variety of things.
words.e: Big cities have diverse populations with people of different races and ages.
words.zh: (形) 多樣的；各種各樣的　(形) 差異大的；不同的

words.d: To enchant someone is to make them feel very interested or happy.
words.e: The beautiful woman enchanted everybody in the room.
words.zh: (動) 迷住；使著迷　(動) 施魔法；下咒語

words.d: To equip someone is to give them the things needed to do something.
words.e: Steve was equipped with the tools needed to do the job.
words.zh: (動) 裝備；配備　(動) 使具有能力；準備

words.d: An exception is someone or something not conforming to a rule.
words.e: Most students thought the test was hard, but Tim was the exception.
words.zh: (名) 例外；特殊情況　(名) 異議；反對

words.d: A genre is a type of literature, art, or music characterized by its style.
words.e: She is considered a master in the comedy genre.
words.zh: (名) 文類；類型　(名) 風格；流派

words.d: An impact is the effect someone or something has on another.
words.e: My grandmother had a great impact on my life.
words.zh: (名) 影響；衝擊　(動) 影響；衝撞

words.d: To lure someone is to convince them to do something, by using a trick.
words.e: The store lures people in with big signs that say “Sale!”
words.zh: (動) 吸引；誘惑　(名) 誘餌；吸引力

words.d: An obstacle is an object or a problem that stops you from doing something.
words.e: Climbing over the giant rock was the biggest obstacle for the hikers.
words.zh: (名) 障礙；阻礙　(名) 困難；難題

words.d: A shelter is a place to go that is safe from danger or bad weather.
words.e: The frog took shelter from the storm in a nearby cave.
words.zh: (名) 避難所；遮蔽物　(動) 庇護；遮蔽

words.d: To sort means to separate things into different groups or classes.
words.e: He sorted his clothes by colors and sizes.
words.zh: (動) 分類；整理　(名) 種類；類型

words.d: To supply something means to give people what they need or want.
words.e: The water heater supplied the house with warm water.
words.zh: (名) 供應；補給品　(動) 供應；提供

words.d: If people are vain, they are only concerned with how they look.
words.e: Rebecca is so vain that she looks at herself in every mirror.
words.zh: (形) 徒勞的；虛榮的

words.d: An alternative is something that you can choose instead of your first choice.
words.e: Her first plan to make extra money seemed weak, so she chose an alternative.
words.zh: (形) 替代的；另外的　(名) 替代品；另一選擇

words.d: An avenue is a road, often a large one with buildings on each side.
words.e: Drive down this avenue and then turn left to go to the park.
words.zh: (名) 大街；林蔭道　(名) 途徑；方法

words.d: The belly is the stomach of a person or animal.
words.e: His belly was full because he ate a lot of food.
words.zh: (名) 腹部；肚子　(形) 腹部的；前面的

words.d: A bid to do something is an attempt to do it.
words.e: He made a bid to become the university’s next president.
words.zh: (名) 出價；投標　(動) 出價；投標

words.d: To blow means to move air or move something through the air, as in the wind.
words.e: The wind is blowing very hard today. We should stay inside.
words.zh: (動) 吹；吹動　(動) 打；打擊　(名) 一擊；打擊

words.d: A conflict is a fight between different people or groups.
words.e: The two nations had a conflict over which one could use the water in the river.
words.zh: (名) [ˈkɑnflɪkt] 衝突；爭執　(動) [kənˈflɪkt] 衝突；不一致

words.d: A continent is one of the seven large areas of land on the Earth.
words.e: Asia is the largest continent.
words.zh: (名) 大陸；洲

words.d: A current is a steady and constant flow of air or water in a river or ocean.
words.e: The ocean currents took the ship far off into the sea.
words.zh: (形) 現在的；當前的　(名) 電流；水流；趨勢

words.d: Disrespect is rudeness or a behavior that shows a lack of respect.
words.e: He showed disrespect by arguing with his boss during a meeting.
words.zh: 不尊重是指無禮或表現出缺乏尊重的行為。

words.d: Enthusiasm is a very strong good feeling about something.
words.e: The crowd showed their enthusiasm for the soccer team by cheering loudly.
words.zh: (名) 熱情；熱忱　(名) 熱心；積極的態度

words.d: When something is harsh, it is very unpleasant.
words.e: The desert can be a very harsh environment.
words.zh: (形) 刺耳的；粗糙的　(形) 嚴厲的；無情的

words.d: To lean is to bend the body in a particular direction.
words.e: The woman leaned against the counter because she was tired.
words.zh: (形) 瘦的；精幹的　(動) 傾斜；靠著　(副) 朝向；傾向

words.d: The meantime is the time between two events.
words.e: Ted began setting the table. In the meantime, I began preparing the food.
words.zh: (名) 同時；其間　(副) 在此期間；與此同時

words.d: Mischief is behavior that is meant to trick or cause trouble for people.
words.e: Ben was up to mischief when he persuaded Ken to paint his face.
words.zh: (名) 惡作劇；調皮　(動) 製造麻煩；搗亂

words.d: Muscle is a mass of tissue attached to bone that helps you move.
words.e: She went to the gym in order to make her muscles stronger.
words.zh: (名) 肌肉；肌肉組織　(名) 力量；體力

words.d: To rescue someone means to remove them from danger.
words.e: The firefighter rescued the man from the burning building.
words.zh: (名) 救援；營救　(動) 拯救；解救

words.d: A succession is a number of things that follow one after the other.
words.e: The student said the letters of the alphabet in succession.
words.zh: (名) 連續；繼承

words.d: The terrain is the land and all of its physical features or parts.
words.e: The terrain below was rocky and full of hills.
words.zh: (名) 地形；地勢

words.d: If someone is timid, they are afraid, shy, or nervous.
words.e: The timid child hides behind her mother whenever she sees a stranger.
words.zh: (形) 膽小的；羞怯的

words.d: Violence is forceful action that is meant to injure or kill people.
words.e: The boy hit his brother in an act of violence.
words.zh: (名) 暴力；猛烈

words.d: To affect someone or something is to have an influence over them.
words.e: The student’s poor attitude affected the other students in the class.
words.zh: (動) 影響；影響到　(名) 情感；感受

words.d: An autograph is the written name of a famous person.
words.e: Everybody wanted the movie star’s autograph.
words.zh: (名) 親筆簽名；手簽　(動) 簽名；在...上簽名

words.d: A bead is a drop of liquid.
words.e: Beads of water collected outside the glass.
words.zh: (名) 珠子；珠粒　(動) 形成珠粒；用珠子裝飾

words.d: To brew coffee or tea means to pour hot water over it.
words.e: Please brew a fresh pot of coffee.
words.zh: (動) 釀造；沖泡　(名) 釀造物；一杯飲料

words.d: To charm someone is to please them with your personality.
words.e: Gail charmed everyone with her humorous stories.
words.zh: (名) 魅力；吸引力　(動) 迷住；吸引　(形) 迷人的；有魅力的

words.d: A destiny is all the things that happen or will happen to a person in their life.
words.e: It was his destiny to become a great singer.
words.zh: (名) 命運；宿命　(名) 前途；發展方向

words.d: A horn is a device that makes a loud noise.
words.e: The boy honked his horn while he rode his bicycle past the house.
words.zh: (名) 喇叭；號角　(名) 角；獸角　(動) 用角頂；插嘴

words.d: When someone is irritable, they become annoyed or angry very easily.
words.e: She is irritable when she doesn’t get enough sleep.
words.zh: (形) 易怒的；容易惹惱的　(形) 易受刺激的；敏感的

words.d: To lag behind is to move slowly behind other moving objects.
words.e: The girl on rollerblades lagged behind the little girl on the bicycle.
words.zh: (動) 落後；滯後　(名) 延遲；時間差

words.d: To maximize something means to make it the biggest in size or amount.
words.e: Businesses try to maximize their profits.
words.zh: (動) 最大化；使達到最大　(動) 充分利用；發揮到最大

words.d: A nightmare is a bad or scary dream.
words.e: The girl was scared to go back to sleep because she had a nightmare.
words.zh: (名) 噩夢；惡夢　(名) 令人極度困擾的事物

words.d: When something is nutritious, it helps the body stay healthy.
words.e: Mangoes are one of the most nutritious fruits in the world.
words.zh: (形) 營養豐富的；有營養的

words.d: Protein is a substance that is necessary for the body to grow and be strong.
words.e: Fish and beef are good sources of protein.
words.zh: (名) 蛋白質

words.d: A signature is your name that you have written in your own way.
words.e: I put my signature at the end of the letter.
words.zh: (名) 簽名；署名

words.d: Stuff is a word used to refer to things without mentioning the things by name.
words.e: We gave a lot of stuff to the charity.
words.zh: (名) 東西；物品　(動) 填塞；裝滿

words.d: When something is subconscious, it is done without thinking about it.
words.e: Breathing is one of the subconscious things that the body does.
words.zh: (形) 潛意識的；下意識的

words.d: A van is a vehicle that is used for carrying things but is smaller than a truck.
words.e: The delivery company uses large vans to deliver packages.
words.zh: (名) 廂型車；貨車

words.d: To warn someone is to make them know of possible danger in the future.
words.e: The lifeguard warned people to stay away from the rough ocean.
words.zh: (動) 警告；告誡

words.d: A workout is an exercise routine that helps improve health.
words.e: She doesn’t do her workout on the weekends.
words.zh: (名) 鍛鍊；運動

words.d: To zoom is to move quickly.
words.e: The cars zoomed along the road.
words.zh: (名) 變焦；縮放　(動) 快速移動；變焦

words.d: A brick is a block of hard clay that is used for building things, such as walls.
words.e: There were several bricks scattered on the ground.
words.zh: (名) 磚；磚塊　(名) 磚紅色

words.d: To crumble means to break or fall apart into small pieces.
words.e: The old house’s walls crumbled into a pile of rock and wood.
words.zh: (動) 碎裂；崩潰　(名) 碎片；崩潰

words.d: Dough is a mixture of flour and water that becomes bread when baked.
words.e: I made heart-shaped cookies from the dough.
words.zh: (名) 麵糰；金錢　(動) 搓揉；製作麵糰

words.d: To express a feeling or idea means to show others how one thinks or feels.
words.e: The nurse expressed her sympathy for the sick patient.
words.zh: (動) 表達；表現　(形) 快速的；特快的

words.d: A fist is a hand with fingers bent in toward the palm.
words.e: The bully made a fist and threatened to hit the small boy.
words.zh: (名) 拳頭；緊握的手　(動) 握拳；用拳頭打

words.d: If something is flexible, then it can bend easily without breaking.
words.e: The tree branch was so flexible it could be bent into a circle and not break.
words.zh: (形) 柔軟的；易彎曲的　(形) 靈活的；有適應力的

words.d: To flush means the face becomes red due to heat, illness, or emotion.
words.e: After the long race, the runner’s face was flushed.
words.zh: (動) 沖洗；沖水　(形) 臉紅的；充足的

words.d: To injure someone means to damage a part of their body.
words.e: The car crash injured two people.
words.zh: (動) 傷害；受傷　(動) 損害；危害

words.d: A lump is a small piece of something that is solid.
words.e: The artist took a lump of clay and turned it into a beautiful pot.
words.zh: (名) 腫塊；疙瘩　(動) 把...合併；歸為一類　(形) 成塊的；結塊的

words.d: A mixture is something that is made by mixing other things together.
words.e: The walls were built using a mixture of water, rock, and dirt.
words.zh: (名) 混合物；混合　(動) 混合；摻合

words.d: To reconcile means to return to a friendly relationship.
words.e: After arguing, the two friends were reconciled with each other.
words.zh: (動) 調解；使和好

words.d: To ruin something means to harm or damage it greatly.
words.e: Our walk in the park was ruined by the sudden rain.
words.zh: (動) 毀壞；毀滅　(名) 廢墟；毀滅

words.d: To shatter something means to break it suddenly into many tiny pieces.
words.e: When the ball hit the window, the glass shattered.
words.zh: (動) 粉碎；震碎

words.d: Shutters are wooden or metal covers in front of a window.
words.e: Mr. Smith closed the shutters every night to make his bedroom dark.
words.zh: (名) 快門；百葉窗

words.d: To sift something means to remove all the large pieces.
words.e: The baker sifted the flour into a large bowl.
words.zh: (動) 篩；過濾

words.d: If something is slight, then it is small or minor.
words.e: There was only a slight change in the little boy’s height.
words.zh: (形) 輕微的；細小的　(動) 輕視；怠慢

words.d: To sparkle means to shine brightly with quick flashes of light.
words.e: The stars sparkled in the winter night’s sky.
words.zh: (動) 閃爍；閃耀　(名) 閃光；光彩

words.d: To sprinkle means to scatter something all over something else.
words.e: He sprinkled the pasta with salt and black pepper.
words.zh: (動) 灑；撒

words.d: If food is stale, then it is not fresh but dry, hard, and not good to eat.
words.e: The cookies sat on the table so long that they became stale.
words.zh: (形) 不新鮮的；陳腐的

words.d: To utter a word or a sound means to say it.
words.e: The lost boy was so scared that he could barely utter a single word.
words.zh: (動) 說出；發出(聲音)

words.d: You use although to say that one thing is contrasted by another.
words.e: Although she was late, her friends gave her a warm welcome.
words.zh: (連) 雖然；縱然　(副) 儘管；雖說

words.d: To apply something means to put it on.
words.e: She always applies makeup to her face before going outside.
words.zh: (動) 申請；應徵　提出申請　(動) 使用；運用　(動) 涂抹；敷

words.d: To await something means to wait for it.
words.e: The players awaited the judge’s decision.
words.zh: (動) 等待；期待　(動) 留待；等著被

words.d: When something is beloved, it is very special and you like it very much.
words.e: The boy took a nap next to his beloved cat.
words.zh: (形) 心愛的；摯愛的　(名) 心愛的人；摯愛
