words.d: To bury someone or something means to put them in the ground.
words.e: They buried their grandfather under his favorite tree after he died.
words.zh: (動) 埋藏；埋葬　(動) 隱藏；掩蓋

words.d: A climate is the usual weather in a place.
words.e: The climate in the desert is very hot.
words.zh: (名) 氣候；氣象　(名) 風氣；環境

words.d: When you complain, you say that you are unhappy about something.
words.e: The workers complained that they were being treated unfairly.
words.zh: (動) 抱怨；不滿　(名) 抱怨；訴苦

words.d: To confuse someone means to make them feel like they are unsure.
words.e: The sign confused the traveler because it pointed in two directions.
words.zh: (動) 使困惑；使混淆　(動) 弄混；搞混

words.d: When something is due, it is expected to happen or be done at that time.
words.e: The papers were due on the 19th.
words.zh: (形) 應該；預期的　(形) 到期的；應繳納的　(名) 應得的；應付款

words.d: When you talk about an entire thing, you are talking about the whole thing.
words.e: He was so hungry that he ate the entire pizza by himself.
words.zh: (形) 整個的；全部的　(形) 完整的；未損傷的

words.d: To establish something means to create it.
words.e: He wanted to establish a club for people to help the Earth.
words.zh: (動) 建立；設立　(動) 確定；證實

words.d: A furnace is a place where heat is made.
words.e: Mr. Jones came to fix the furnace.
words.zh: (名) 熔爐；火爐　(名) 極熱的地方

words.d: A leash is a rope or chain that is used to lead an animal.
words.e: A lot of dogs must wear a leash to keep them from running away.
words.zh: (名) 皮帶；繩子　(動) 用皮帶拴住；束縛

words.d: To mature means to grow up to become an adult.
words.e: When they matured, they became as tall as their parents.
words.zh: (形) 成熟的；懂事的　(動) 成熟；變成熟

words.d: To measure something means to find out the quality, value, or effect of it.
words.e: The scientists carefully measured the amount of chemicals in the tubes.
words.zh: (名) 措施；方法　(動) 測量；衡量　(形) 適度的；有分寸的

words.d: The midst of something is the middle of it.
words.e: She was in the midst of cleaning when the telephone rang.
words.zh: (介) 在…之中；在…中間　(名) 中間；中央

words.d: Misery is extreme suffering.
words.e: There was a lot of misery after Sam lost his dog.
words.zh: (名) 痛苦；悲慘　(名) 貧困；困境

words.d: When something happened prior to something else, it happened earlier.
words.e: Ron had to wait since he arrived prior to the scheduled meeting time.
words.zh: (形) 先前的；較早的　(副) 在前；居先

words.d: Research is close and careful study to discover new things.
words.e: Scientists did a lot of research on the subject of blood type.
words.zh: (名) 研究；調查　(動) 研究；調查

words.d: A variety of something is a group of many different kinds of it.
words.e: There are a variety of flowers at the shop.
words.zh: (名) 多樣化；種類

words.d: If something happens altogether, it happens completely.
words.e: The company stopped using sugar altogether in its food.
words.zh: (副) 總共；一共　(副) 完全地；根本上

words.d: To bind is to bring people together.
words.e: The victims of the flood were bound by their need to help each other.
words.zh: (動) 綁；束縛　(動) 裝訂；釘成冊

words.d: A bruise is a dark mark caused by being hit by something.
words.e: She got a bruise on her knee from falling down.
words.zh: (名) 瘀傷；淤青　(動) 使受傷；碰傷　(形) 受傷的；有瘀傷的

words.d: A custom is a way of doing things that has been the same for a long time.
words.e: It is a custom that the bride and groom have the first dance.
words.zh: (名) 風俗；習慣　(形) 訂做的；習慣性的

words.d: When someone is disobedient, they do not follow the rules or instructions.
words.e: The disobedient children didn’t listen to their mother and had an accident.
words.zh: 當某人不服從時，他們不遵守規則或指示。

words.d: To foresee something is to know about it before it happens.
words.e: The teacher didn’t foresee any problems with her large class.
words.zh: (動) 預見；預先看到　(動) 預測；預料

words.d: To glimpse something is to see it for a short time.
words.e: She glimpsed outside the window as the plane was about to land.
words.zh: (名) 一瞥；匆匆一看　(動) 瞥見；匆匆看到

words.d: A hoop is a ring that is made of plastic, metal, or wood.
words.e: The boys tried to toss the ball through the basketball hoop.
words.zh: 箍是由塑膠、金屬或木材製成的環。

words.d: Misfortune is bad luck or an unlucky event.
words.e: His family helped him when he encountered misfortune.
words.zh: (名) 不幸；厄運　(名) 災難；困難

words.d: When something is negative, it is unpleasant or sad.
words.e: She doesn’t like to say negative things about her friends.
words.zh: (形) 否定的；消極的　(名) 否定；負面的事物

words.d: Per is used to mean “each” when giving a price, size, or amount.
words.e: It costs $8 per person to watch the baseball game.
words.zh: (介) 每；每一

words.d: To plead is to ask for something you want very badly.
words.e: He pleaded for his parents to let him go to the soccer game.
words.zh: (動) 懇求；辯護

words.d: To rip something means to pull it apart.
words.e: She ripped the paper in half by accident.
words.zh: (動) 撕；撕裂　(名) 裂口；撕裂處

words.d: The sake of something is the reason for doing it.
words.e: The parents worked hard for their children’s sake.
words.zh: (名) 目的；利益

words.d: To scrape something is to rub it very hard with something sharp.
words.e: I accidentally scraped the paint off the side of the car.
words.zh: (動) 刮除；擦掉

words.d: A source of something is the place that it comes from.
words.e: The river was the source of drinking water for the village.
words.zh: (名) 來源；出處

words.d: When somebody is stern, they are very serious.
words.e: The stern teacher didn’t allow the students to speak during class.
words.zh: 當某人嚴厲時，他們非常嚴肅。

words.d: To stitch is to use a needle and thread to join pieces of cloth together.
words.e: My grandmother stitched the pieces together to make a big blanket.
words.zh: (名) 一針；縫線　(動) 縫合；縫補

words.d: A thump is the sound of a heavy object falling.
words.e: They heard the thump when the bowling ball hit the floor.
words.zh: 砰的一聲是重物落地的聲音。

words.d: When somebody is vehement, they are angry and emotional.
words.e: Gina was vehement when she found out that Liz was bad in school.
words.zh: (形) 激烈的；強烈的

words.d: A civilization is an organized group of humans that have culture and government.
words.e: Most early civilizations in Central America didn’t use metal weapons.
words.zh: (名) 文明；文化　(名) 開化；教化

words.d: When something is convenient, it is easy to do and does not take a lot of work.
words.e: The bus is a convenient way to get to school.
words.zh: (形) 方便的；便利的　(形) 近便的；容易到達的

words.d: A den is a living space for some types of animals, such as lions.
words.e: The mother lion left her babies in the den.
words.zh: 巢穴是某些動物（如獅子）的居住空間。

words.d: Dew is the drops of water that form on the ground outside during the night.
words.e: When we woke up, we saw that the grass was covered in dew.
words.zh: (名) 露水；露　(動) 凝結成露

words.d: When something is drastic, it is extreme or major.
words.e: She made a drastic decision to get her long hair cut short.
words.zh: (形) 劇烈的；激進的　(形) 嚴厲的；極端的

words.d: To exit means to leave.
words.e: The students exited through the front door.
words.zh: (名) 出口；出去　(動) 離開；退出

words.d: A flock is a group of animals, such as birds, sheep or goats.
words.e: There was one black sheep in the entire flock.
words.zh: (名) 羊群；一大群　(動) 聚集；成群結隊

words.d: To fold is to bend something like paper or cloth so that it takes up less space.
words.e: I folded the paper and put it in my pocket.
words.zh: (動) 摺疊；對摺　(名) 褶皺；摺痕

words.d: A lid is a top for a box or container that can be removed.
words.e: He lifted the lid of the box and revealed her present.
words.zh: (名) 蓋子；蓋　(動) 用蓋子蓋住；覆蓋

words.d: To loom is to seem very large and often scary.
words.e: The ominous clouds loomed over the school.
words.zh: (動) 逼近；隱約出現　(名) 織布機

words.d: When something is mighty, it is strong and large.
words.e: The mighty wrestler scared all who faced him.
words.zh: (形) 強大的；有力的　(形) 巨大的；龐大的

words.d: A mushroom is a fungus with a round top. Some are used as food.
words.e: The soup had fresh mushrooms in it.
words.zh: (名) 蘑菇；菌類　(動) 迅速增長；蓬勃發展

words.d: When something is native, it is originating in a certain place or area.
words.e: Avocadoes are native fruits of Mexico.
words.zh: (形) 本地的；本國的　(名) 本地人；原住民

words.d: Poison is a dangerous substance that causes illness or death.
words.e: They used poison to get rid of the rats in their home.
words.zh: (名) 毒藥；毒物　(動) 毒害；下毒

words.d: Reeds are tall and skinny plants that grow in groups near water.
words.e: The sun set behind the reeds of the lake’s shore.
words.zh: (名) 蘆葦；簧片

words.d: To shield something is to protect it.
words.e: She shielded her eyes from the sun with sunglasses.
words.zh: (名) 盾牌；保護物　(動) 保護；遮擋

words.d: Stormy describes something affected or characterized by storms.
words.e: The golfers decided to go home because of the stormy weather.
words.zh: (形) 暴風雨的；激烈的

words.d: To sway is to move slowly from side to side.
words.e: She swayed while she listened to the music.
words.zh: (動) 搖晃；影響　(名) 搖擺；影響力

words.d: When something is urban, it is related to the city.
words.e: Subways are an important form of urban transportation.
words.zh: (形) 都市的；城市的

words.d: To wade is to walk in or pass through water.
words.e: The child waded in the water at the beach.
words.zh: (動) 涉水；跋涉

words.d: An accent is a certain way of speaking that shows where a person is from.
words.e: The new teacher’s accent was clearly a German one.
words.zh: (名) [ˈæksɛnt] 口音；腔調　(動) [ækˈsɛnt] 強調；使突出

words.d: A barber is a person whose job is to cut hair.
words.e: My hair is getting much too long. I’d better go to the barber’s shop.
words.zh: (名) 理髮師；男理髮店

words.d: A basement of a house or building is a room that is built underground.
words.e: They turned their basement into a game room.
words.zh: (名) 地下室；地窖

words.d: When something is blank, it does not have anything on it.
words.e: She got a blank paper to draw on.
words.zh: (形) 空白的；無字跡的　(名) 空白；空白處　(動) 忘記；(記憶)一片空白

words.d: To blink means to shut the eyes and quickly open them again.
words.e: I blinked many times so that my eyes could adjust to the bright light.
words.zh: (動) 眨眼；閃爍　(名) 眨眼；一瞬間

words.d: A choir is a group of people who sing together.
words.e: He had choir practice every day after school.
words.zh: (名) 合唱團；唱詩班

words.d: When something is comic, it is funny.
words.e: The comic actor was famous for his jokes.
words.zh: (形) 滑稽的；好笑的　(名) 漫畫；喜劇演員

words.d: To complicate something means to make it harder than necessary.
words.e: The bad weather complicated finishing the job quickly.
words.zh: (動) 使複雜；使難以理解　(形) 複雜的；難懂的

words.d: To decline an offer or invitation means to say no to it.
words.e: She declined his offer to pay for her dinner.
words.zh: (動) 拒絕；婉言謝絕　(動) 下降；衰退　(名) 衰落；下降

words.d: An errand is a trip taken to do a specific activity.
words.e: He couldn’t go to practice because he had several errands to do.
words.zh: (名) 差事；跑腿　(動) 跑腿；辦差

words.d: A glove is a piece of clothing that covers your fingers and hand.
words.e: When it gets cold, I always put on a pair of gloves.
words.zh: (名) 手套　(動) 戴手套；用手套覆蓋

words.d: A hermit is one who lives alone and does not spend time with others.
words.e: The hermit lived a simple life in a small cave in the forest.
words.zh: (名) 隱士；隱居者　(名) 隱居修士

words.d: If something is done justly, then it is fair.
words.e: We justly decided to give the prize to him.
words.zh: 若某事被公正地執行，則代表它是公平的。

words.d: Leather is a material made from animal skin that is used to make clothing.
words.e: He got a new leather jacket for his birthday.
words.zh: (名) 皮革；皮革製品　(形) 皮革的；用皮革製成的

words.d: To ponder something is to think about it carefully.
words.e: She sat in the park and pondered her problem.
words.zh: (動) 沉思；仔細考慮

words.d: To reserve something means to keep it for a certain person or time.
words.e: He reserved a table at the busy restaurant.
words.zh: (動) 預訂；保留　(名) 儲備；保留區

words.d: A script is the words of a film or play.
words.e: He read the script of the play three times.
words.zh: (名) 劇本；手稿　(動) 編寫腳本

words.d: To search for something or someone means to look for them carefully.
words.e: I searched the newspaper for a new job.
words.zh: (動) 搜尋；搜查　(名) 搜索；搜尋

words.d: To slam is to close something hard.
words.e: She slammed the book shut after she finished reading it.
words.zh: (動) 砰地關上；猛擊

words.d: A staircase is a set of stairs found inside a building.
words.e: The staircase leads directly into the kitchen.
words.zh: (名) 樓梯；階梯

words.d: Afflicted means to suffer physically or mentally.
words.e: He was afflicted by pain in his right arm.
words.zh: 受苦指在身體或精神上承受痛苦。

words.d: An aisle is a space between two things that people use to walk.
words.e: They were told to clear the aisle because the plane was about to land.
words.zh: (名) 走廊；通道　(名) 飛機座位之間的通路

words.d: The atmosphere is the air around the Earth where weather conditions form.
words.e: Scientists worry that harmful substances are hurting the atmosphere.
words.zh: (名) 大氣；氛圍　(名) 氣氛；環境

words.d: An author is a person who wrote a certain piece of writing.
words.e: The author was hard at work on his next novel.
words.zh: (名) 作者；作家　(動) 創作；著述

words.d: A breakdown is a failure to work correctly.
words.e: Her car had a breakdown, and she wasn’t sure how to fix it.
words.zh: (名) 故障；崩潰　(動) 分解；發生故障

words.d: Cargo is the items carried by a ship or airplane.
words.e: The cargo of the ship got wet when it started raining.
words.zh: (名) 貨物；貨運　(名) 船貨；運送的物品

words.d: A chapter is a part of a book that usually has a number or a title.
words.e: The first chapter of a book usually introduces the main character of a story.
words.zh: (名) 章；章節　(名) (書籍或歷史的)部分；時期

words.d: To connect two things means to join them together.
words.e: I connected the mouse to my laptop computer.
words.zh: (動) 連接；接上　連結；聯繫

words.d: Etc. is short for “etcetera.” It is used to refer to other unspecified objects.
words.e: She was going to bring treats to the party: cookies, muffins, cake, etc.
words.zh: (縮) 等等；及其他　(片) 諸如此類

words.d: To flip means to press a switch quickly to turn it on or off.
words.e: To turn on the lights, just flip this switch.
words.zh: (動) 翻轉；翻動　(動) 快速切換；按動開關

words.d: If you are idle, you are not doing anything.
words.e: She read a book to keep from being idle.
words.zh: (形) 閒散的；懶惰的　(動) 閒置；虛度

words.d: To notify someone of something is to tell them about it.
words.e: The teacher notified us about a change to our assignment.
words.zh: (動) 通知；告知　(動) 報告；宣布

words.d: Peas are a vegetable that is small, round and green.
words.e: His favorite food was peas.
words.zh: (名) 豌豆

words.d: A raisin is a dried grape.
words.e: Raisins are one of my favorite snacks.
words.zh: (名) 葡萄乾

words.d: To retain something is to keep it.
words.e: Even by the afternoon, the day had retained the morning’s freshness.
words.zh: (動) 保留；記住

words.d: To state something means to say it in a definite way.
words.e: The president stated his opinion about the world’s health concerns.
words.zh: (名) 狀態；州　(動) 陳述；說明

words.d: A tray is a flat plate used to hold food.
words.e: The waiter brought our food on a tray.
words.zh: (名) 托盤；盤子

words.d: If something is unfortunate, it is bad or unlucky.
words.e: It was unfortunate that Dave’s team lost, but he still had fun.
words.zh: (形) 不幸的；令人遺憾的

words.d: When something is vivid, it is bright and colorful.
words.e: The figures in the painting were vivid.
words.zh: (形) 生動的；鮮明的

words.d: To vomit means to have food come up from one’s stomach.
words.e: It is common for women to vomit when they are pregnant.
words.zh: (動) 嘔吐　(名) 嘔吐物

words.d: To betray someone means to treat them in a dishonest way.
words.e: The man betrayed his country when he gave away national secrets.
words.zh: (動) 背叛；洩露　(動) 暴露；顯露

words.d: A blast is a loud noise made by something that explodes.
words.e: There was a loud blast when the police officer fired the gun.
words.zh: (名) 爆炸；氣流　(動) 炸毀；破壞　(片) 太棒了

words.d: A bracelet is a piece of jewelry that you wear around your wrist.
words.e: My father gave me a pretty gold bracelet for my birthday.
words.zh: (名) 手鐲；手環

words.d: To cease means to stop.
words.e: After about an hour, the rain ceased, and a rainbow appeared.
words.zh: (動) 停止；終止　(名) 停止；終結

words.d: To choke means to cough because you have difficulty breathing.
words.e: There was a lot of smoke in the air, and it made me choke.
words.zh: (動) 窒息；哽咽　(動) 使無法呼吸

words.d: When an event is civil, it happens inside a country.
words.e: The country was torn apart by a terrible civil war.
words.zh: (形) 公民的；民間的　(形) 有禮貌的；溫文儒雅的

words.d: To comment means to say something that shows your personal opinion.
words.e: Tom commented on the poor quality of the food.
words.zh: (名) 評論；意見　(動) 評論；發表意見

words.d: To cross means to go from one side to the other side.
words.e: We used his boat to cross to the other side of the lake.
words.zh: (動) 穿過；通過　(形) 生氣的；不悅的　(名) 十字；交叉

words.d: A dent is damage caused by something heavy hitting something else.
words.e: That blue car hit my car, and now there is a small dent in my car door.
words.zh: (名) 凹痕；凹陷　(動) 使凹陷；損害

words.d: To distrust someone means to believe that they are not honest.
words.e: Don’t lend money to someone if you distrust them!
words.zh: (動) 不信任；懷疑　(名) 不信任；懷疑

words.d: A fort is a small building that is specially built to defend an area from attack.
words.e: When the army arrived, they built a big fort on the top of the hill.
words.zh: (名) 堡壘；要塞　(動) 加強；防禦

words.d: To found means to bring something into existence.
words.e: The pastor founded his church in the countryside.
words.zh: (動) 創辦；建立　(動) 發現；找到

words.d: Lining is a piece of cloth that covers the inside of clothes.
words.e: This jacket is very warm because it has a thick lining.
words.zh: (名) 襯裡；內襯　(名) 襯墊物；保護層

words.d: A mass is a large number of things of one type.
words.e: We received a mass of letters this morning.
words.zh: (名) 大量；群眾　(形) 大規模的；廣泛的

words.d: To pray means to talk to God.
words.e: When people go to church, they pray for their families and friends.
words.zh: (動) 祈禱；祈求

words.d: If a place is rife with something bad, it is very common in that place.
words.e: This part of the country is rife with disease.
words.zh: 若某處充斥著某種壞事，表示該事物在該地非常普遍。

words.d: When something is the sole thing, it is the only thing of a particular type.
words.e: His sole purpose in life was to help others.
words.zh: (形) 唯一的；單獨的　(名) 腳底；鞋底

words.d: If you sweep the floor, you clean it with a tool like a broom or a brush.
words.e: There is a lot of dirt on the floor. Can you sweep it please?
words.zh: (動) 打掃；掃除

words.d: Treachery is a behavior in which a person betrays a country or a person.
words.e: The man ran away to escape from the treachery of his wife.
words.zh: 背叛是指一個人出賣國家或他人的行為。

words.d: To tuck something means to put it somewhere so that it is neat or safe.
words.e: He looked sloppy without his shirt being tucked into his pants.
words.zh: (動) 塞進；捲起

words.d: A background is a person’s education, family, and experience.
words.e: The new teacher had a background in science and math.
words.zh: (名) 背景；背景資訊　(名) 教育背景；家庭背景

words.d: Bait is something used to trick a person or thing to do something.
words.e: The best bait for catching fish is a big, fat worm.
words.zh: (名) 餌；誘餌　(動) 用餌引誘；激怒

words.d: To chronicle something means to record an event or speech.
words.e: The daily newspaper chronicles local and world events.
words.zh: (名) 編年史；歷史記錄　(動) 記錄；詳細記述

words.d: Copper is a red-brown metal often used in electric wire and pipes.
words.e: Ancient hunters melted copper to make knives and spears.
words.zh: (名) 銅；銅幣　(形) 銅色的；紅銅色的

words.d: A disease is an illness that causes specific problems.
words.e: He had a disease that caused him to lose his hearing.
words.zh: (名) 疾病；病　(動) 使患病；使衰退

words.d: Folklore is the collection of beliefs and stories of a culture.
words.e: India’s folklore has stories written in long poems about great warriors.
words.zh: (名) 民間故事；民俗傳說　(名) 民間文化；傳統知識

words.d: To infect someone means to give them an illness.
words.e: The common cold infects hundreds of millions of people each year.
words.zh: (動) 使感染；傳染　(名) 感染

words.d: To itch means to rub the skin with your fingernails.
words.e: The rough fabric in his shirt made the back of his neck itch.
words.zh: (名) 癢；搔癢感　(動) 發癢；使發癢　(形) 發癢的；想要做的

words.d: Literature is books, plays, and poetry.
words.e: Early American literature covers the poetry and stories from 1500 to 1800.
words.zh: (名) 文學；文學作品　(名) 文獻；印刷品

words.d: A millennium is one thousand years.
words.e: Stonehenge is believed to have been built about 5 millenniums ago.
words.zh: (名) 千年；千年期

words.d: A myth is a traditional story that explains a culture’s history and beliefs.
words.e: In Greece, there was a myth about a woman who had snakes for hair.
words.zh: (名) 神話；傳說　(名) 虛假的信念；謊言

words.d: To relate to something means to have a connection with it.
words.e: A company’s plan usually relates to how much profit it can make.
words.zh: (動) 與...有關；敘述；建立關係

words.d: A religion is a belief in a god or gods.
words.e: Their religion taught that people should forgive their enemies.
words.zh: (名) 宗教；宗教信仰

words.d: A sum is a specific amount of money.
words.e: He calculated the numbers to see what the sum of his bills would be.
words.zh: (名) 總數；金額　(動) 總結；合計

words.d: A teller is a person who works with a bank’s customers.
words.e: The teller at the bank helped Kelly put money into a savings account.
words.zh: (名) 出納員；講述者

words.d: If someone is trustworthy, they are honest and truthful.
words.e: Mary is one of the most trustworthy people I’ve ever met.
words.zh: (形) 值得信賴的；可靠的

words.d: To update something means to make it more modern.
words.e: We need to update the programs on our computers.
words.zh: (動) [ʌp'det] 更新；使現代化　(名) ['ʌp.det] 更新；最新消息

words.d: A vein is a tube in the body that carries blood toward the heart.
words.e: The blue veins in my hand are just under my skin.
words.zh: (名) 血管；脈；礦脈；葉脈

words.d: Venom is a poisonous substance that comes from animals or plants.
words.e: A snake’s venom can be used to cure the illnesses it creates.
words.zh: (名) 毒液；惡意

words.d: To promote someone means to raise them to a higher position or rank.
words.e: After two years in the company, she was promoted to a manager.
words.zh: (動) 促進；推銷

words.d: Charity is an act of giving help, usually money, to those who need it.
words.e: Thanks to his friends’ charity, he had enough money to pay the rent.
words.zh: (名) 慈善；慈善機構　(名) 仁慈；寬容

words.d: Commerce is the activity of buying and selling things.
words.e: The new shopping mall increased the commerce in that section of town.
words.zh: (名) 商業；貿易　(名) 商務；商品買賣

words.d: To condemn someone means to give them a specific punishment.
words.e: The judge condemned the criminal to five years in prison.
words.zh: (動) 譴責；批評　(動) 判刑；定罪

words.d: If something is cozy, then it is comfortable, warm, and relaxing.
words.e: The thick blanket made the bed very cozy.
words.zh: (形) 舒適的；溫暖舒適的　(形) 親切的；親密的

words.d: To deplete an amount of something means to use up all of it.
words.e: All the driving he was doing was depleting his car’s fuel supply.
words.zh: (動) 耗盡；用完　枯竭；減少

words.d: An economy is the money and businesses of a country or region.
words.e: The factory was good for the economy because it brought jobs to the area.
words.zh: (名) 經濟；節約　(形) 經濟的；省錢的

words.d: An empire is a large group of countries ruled by an emperor or empress.
words.e: The emperor built roads to make travel easier throughout the empire.
words.zh: (名) 帝國；帝制　(名) 權力大的企業或組織

words.d: Goods are anything that can be bought or sold.
words.e: Shoes, hats, dresses and purses were the goods she wanted to buy.
words.zh: (名) 商品；貨物　(名) 物品；財產

words.d: To heed something means to obey or follow it.
words.e: You should heed the advice on the sign and not drive so fast.
words.zh: (動) 注意；理會　(動) 遵守；聽從

words.d: To hitchhike means to travel by asking for rides from passing vehicles.
words.e: She didn’t have a car, so she hitchhiked several miles to her brother’s home.
words.zh: 搭便車是指透過請求過往車輛載送來旅行。

words.d: To mock someone means to tease them in a cruel way.
words.e: The girls mocked Nancy because she was a new student.
words.zh: (動) 嘲笑；模仿　(形) 假的；虛假的

words.d: If someone is neutral, then they do not help either of the two fighting sides.
words.e: The girl’s friend remained neutral while the couple was arguing.
words.zh: (形) 中立的；不偏不倚　(形) 中性的；無色的

words.d: To persecute means to treat someone badly.
words.e: Dan felt persecuted because he was smaller than the other boys at school.
words.zh: (動) 迫害；虐待

words.d: Pity is the feeling of sadness and kindness for those who are suffering.
words.e: Because she had pity for the lost boy, she helped him find his parents.
words.zh: (名) 可惜；遺憾　(動) 同情；憐憫

words.d: To reduce something means to make it less in size or number.
words.e: When the store reduced its prices, people wanted to shop there.
words.zh: (動) 減少；降低

words.d: A scribe was a person whose job was to copy written works.
words.e: In Ancient Egypt, scribes recorded important events.
words.zh: 抄寫員的工作是複製書面作品。

words.d: A temper is someone’s mood or a chance that they might get angry.
words.e: She has a temper. Even the slightest mistakes make her angry.
words.zh: (名) 脾氣；性情　(動) 使回火；調和

words.d: A throne is the special chair in which a ruler sits.
words.e: Nobody except the king and queen sat in the thrones in the great hall.
words.zh: (名) 王位；寶座

words.d: Unity is the state of people working together for a certain purpose.
words.e: The project was finished early, thanks to the unity of the workers.
words.zh: (名) 團結；統一

words.d: A victor is a group or person that wins in a contest.
words.e: At the end of the game, the blue team was the victor.
words.zh: (名) 勝利者；優勝者

words.d: If something is accurate, it is completely correct.
words.e: The story in the newspaper wasn’t very accurate.
words.zh: (形) 準確的；精確的

words.d: To analyze something is to study it.
words.e: The scientist will analyze the blood sample.
words.zh: (動) 分析；解析　(動) 研究；檢查

words.d: An asteroid is a giant rock from outer space.
words.e: In 1908, a giant asteroid hit Siberia.
words.zh: 小行星是來自外太空的巨大岩石。

words.d: A controversy is a dispute about something that affects many people.
words.e: There has been a lot of controversy over the judge’s decision.
words.zh: (名) 爭議；爭論　(形) 有爭議的；引起爭論的

words.d: To evolve is to change over time.
words.e: Many people think that humans evolved from animals.
words.zh: (動) 進化；演變　(動) 發展；逐漸形成

words.d: A factor is something that has an effect on the way another thing happens.
words.e: Smoking is the main factor that causes lung cancer.
words.zh: (名) 因素；要素　(動) 是...的因素；影響

words.d: If something is genetic, it is related to the genes in one’s body.
words.e: The color of one’s eyes is genetic.
words.zh: (形) 遺傳的；基因的　(形) 與生俱來的；天生的

words.d: A genome is the collection of all the genes in a living thing.
words.e: Understanding the human genome may help cure many diseases.
words.zh: 基因組是生物體內所有基因的集合。

words.d: To be identical is to be the same as someone or something else.
words.e: James and John are identical twins.
words.zh: (形) 完全相同的；一模一样的　(形) 同一的；同一个的

words.d: An intellectual is a very smart person.
words.e: We’ve always considered my Uncle Max the intellectual of the family.
words.zh: (形) 聰慧的；智慧的　(名) 知識份子；聰慧的人

words.d: A majority of something is almost all of the people or things in that group.
words.e: A majority of the people voted for Tom Smith in the election.
words.zh: (名) 多數；大多數　(名) 成年；法定年齡

words.d: A mammal is an animal that usually has hair and is not born from an egg.
words.e: Even though it lives in the water, whales are actually mammals.
words.zh: (名) 哺乳動物；有奶腺的動物

words.d: To multiply is to increase in number.
words.e: In the past year, the number of people at work multiplied by ten percent.
words.zh: (動) 乘以；相乘　(動) 增加；繁殖

words.d: Offspring are the children of a person or the babies of an animal.
words.e: The dog’s offspring had the same color of fur as she did.
words.zh: (名) 後代；子孫　(名) 子女；產物

words.d: A pesticide is a substance used to kill insects.
words.e: The farmer sprayed his crops with a pesticide to keep bugs away.
words.zh: (名) 農藥；殺蟲劑

words.d: To regulate something is to control how it happens.
words.e: The bank regulates how much money people can borrow from it.
words.zh: (動) 調節；管理

words.d: To reinforce something is to make it stronger.
words.e: Peter reinforced his opinion with information from a book.
words.zh: (動) 加強；增援

words.d: If one is stricken by a disease or problem, they are badly affected by it.
words.e: Mike was stricken with a horrible illness.
words.zh: 若某人受疾病或問題侵襲，則表示其深受影響。

words.d: If something is vast, it is very large.
words.e: The wealthy man bought a vast amount of land in the countryside.
words.zh: (形) 廣闊的；巨大的

words.d: A vegetarian is someone who does not eat any meat products.
words.e: I became a vegetarian because I don’t like the taste of meat.
words.zh: (名) 素食主義者；(形) 素食的

words.d: To cherish something means to hold it as very important.
words.e: I cherish this trophy I won.
words.zh: (動) 珍惜；重視　(動) 懷念；深深喜愛

words.d: Compassion is a feeling of understanding for someone who is hurt or suffering.
words.e: The veterinarian had compassion for the sick little puppy.
words.zh: (名) 同情心；憐憫　(動) 同情；憐憫

words.d: Consent is permission to do something.
words.e: Their mother gave the children consent to go outside and play.
words.zh: (名) 同意；許可　(動) 同意；贊成

words.d: The core is the center of something.
words.e: The rings of a tree start forming at its core.
words.zh: (名) 核心；中心　(動) 去除果核

words.d: If someone is cunning, they are good at tricking people.
words.e: The cunning child fooled his parents into thinking that he was kind.
words.zh: (形) 狡猾的；精明的　(名) 狡詐；詭計

words.d: If someone is dizzy, then they feel off balance as if they will fall down.
words.e: The pregnant woman was dizzy after standing up too quickly.
words.zh: (形) 頭暈的；眩暈的　(形) 使人困惑的；令人眼花的

words.d: Equilibrium is the balance between different forces.
words.e: The sudden drop in prices upset the equilibrium of the economy.
words.zh: (名) 平衡；均衡　(名) 穩定狀態；平衡狀態

words.d: To foster a feeling or a skill means to help it develop.
words.e: She helped foster a sense of calm in the little boy.
words.zh: (動) 培養；促進　(動) 領養；撫養

words.d: To grind something means to break it into very small pieces or powder.
words.e: She wanted to grind the beans, so she could brew some coffee.
words.zh: (動) 磨；研磨　(動) 辛苦地工作；努力奮鬥

words.d: To growl means to make a deep, angry sound.
words.e: The dog started to growl at the man walking by.
words.zh: (動) 低聲咆哮；發出沙啞聲　(名) 咆哮聲；沙啞聲

words.d: Moderation is a state of being just enough but not too much.
words.e: It is important to eat in moderation so that you can have a healthy body.
words.zh: (名) 適度；溫和　(動) 調節；主持

words.d: A predator is an animal that kills and eats other animals.
words.e: The peregrine falcon is a predator that eats fish.
words.zh: (名) 掠食者；捕食者

words.d: If someone is sane, they can think in a normal way.
words.e: Oliver does not act like a sane person when he is angry.
words.zh: (形) 心智健全的；明智的

words.d: A saucer is a small round dish that you set a cup on.
words.e: He placed the spoon on the saucer.
words.zh: (名) 碟子；茶托

words.d: To snatch something means to take it away with a quick motion.
words.e: He was so hungry that he snatched an apple from a tree.
words.zh: (動) 搶奪；奪取

words.d: To stagger means to move in an unsteady way and almost fall over.
words.e: He staggered around after having too much to drink.
words.zh: (動) 踉蹌；搖晃

words.d: To stumble means to put your foot down wrong so that you almost fall.
words.e: He stumbled as he ran through the puddle.
words.zh: (動) 絆倒；結巴地說；(名) 絆倒

words.d: If someone is tense, they are worried something bad might happen.
words.e: After the phone call, Monica was very tense.
words.zh: (形) 緊張的；拉緊的　(名) 時態

words.d: To tumble means to fall, often in a rolling way.
words.e: He lost his balance while snowboarding and tumbled to the ground.
words.zh: (動) 跌倒；翻滾

words.d: To withhold something is to not give it to someone.
words.e: They withheld all information until she paid her fine.
words.zh: (動) 扣留；保留

words.d: An aircraft is a vehicle that flies in the sky, such as an airplane or helicopter.
words.e: At the museum in the airport, you can see a lot of old aircraft.
words.zh: 飛行器是在天空中飛行的交通工具，例如飛機或直升機。

words.d: A celebrity is someone who is famous.
words.e: It was the highlight of the evening when the celebrities arrived.
words.zh: (名) 名人；名星　(名) 名聲；名望

words.d: Concrete is a substance made from stones.
words.e: The man covered the ground with concrete.
words.zh: (形) 具體的；實際的　(名) 混凝土；水泥

words.d: If someone is decisive, they make decisions quickly.
words.e: Our boss is very decisive, so it did not take long to organize the project.
words.zh: (形) 決定性的；果斷的　(形) 明確的；決定結果的

words.d: If someone is esteemed, many people like or respect them.
words.e: An esteemed scientist is coming to the university to talk about her discoveries.
words.zh: 受尊敬的人會受到許多人的喜愛或尊重。

words.d: If something is ethical, it is the right thing to do.
words.e: Many people believe that it is ethical to help others in need.
words.zh: (形) 倫理的；道德的　(形) 符合道德的；誠實的

words.d: If plants or animals are extinct, there are none left.
words.e: There used to be dinosaurs all over the world, but now they are extinct.
words.zh: (形) 滅絕的；絕種的　(形) 熄滅的；消失的

words.d: If a person or plant is hardy, it is strong and can live though difficult conditions.
words.e: The farmer is a hardy man and doesn’t mind working outside.
words.zh: (形) 強壯的；耐寒的　(形) 勇敢的；堅強的

words.d: An institute is an organization that is interested in research or teaching.
words.e: I am going to a lecture about ancient Rome at the Historical Institute.
words.zh: (名) 機構；研究所　(動) 建立；設立

words.d: Jealousy is a feeling of wanting something that somebody else has.
words.e: She felt a lot of jealousy when she saw Luke with two girls.
words.zh: (名) 妒忌；嫉妒　(形) 妒忌的；猜疑的

words.d: To migrate means to move from one place to another.
words.e: Many birds migrate to warmer countries in the winter.
words.zh: (動) 遷移；遷徙　(動) 移居；移動

words.d: To nurture something means to care for it as it grows or develops.
words.e: Robert nurtured his plants, and that is why they grow so well.
words.zh: (動) 培養；養育　(名) 營養；培育

words.d: If something is overhead, it is located above you.
words.e: As we sat on top of the hill, a plane flew overhead.
words.zh: (形) 頭頂上的；上方的　(副) 在頭頂上；在上方　(名) 經常性開支；管理費用

words.d: A principle is a belief about the correct way to behave.
words.e: To maintain principles, it’s vital to watch, listen, and speak carefully.
words.zh: (名) 原則；原理

words.d: If a place is rural, it is in the countryside instead of the city.
words.e: I want to live in a small house in a rural area.
words.zh: (形) 鄉村的；農村的

words.d: If a place is secluded, it is far away from any other place.
words.e: There was a secluded bench in the park.
words.zh: (形) 隱蔽的；僻靜的

words.d: A species is a type of plant or animal.
words.e: There are 21 different species of butterfly in this forest.
words.zh: (名) 物種；種類

words.d: A swamp is a very wet area of land.
words.e: There are lots of wild animals living in the swamp.
words.zh: (名) 沼澤；濕地　(動) 淹沒；使應接不暇

words.d: To traverse means to move or travel through an area.
words.e: The explorer traversed the desert alone on a camel.
words.zh: (動) 橫越；穿過

words.d: Zoology is a subject in which people study animals.
words.e: Helen wants to study zoology because she has always liked animals.
words.zh: 動物學是一門研究動物的學科。

words.d: An assumption is something that you believe is true but cannot prove.
words.e: I went to the cafeteria on the assumption that everyone would be there.
words.zh: (名) 假設；假定　(名) 假想；認定

words.d: Barley is a grain that is used for animal feed, health food and beer.
words.e: The farmer grew barley to feed his cows in the winter.
words.zh: (名) 大麥；麥類穀物

words.d: A beast is a large, dangerous animal.
words.e: A lion is one of the fiercest beasts on Earth.
words.zh: (名) 野獸；凶惡的人　(形) 野蠻的；殘忍的

words.d: A colonel is a military officer.
words.e: James has been given a promotion in the army. He is now a colonel.
words.zh: (名) 陸軍上校；空軍上校

words.d: If a disease is contagious, it is easily carried from one person to another.
words.e: You must wear protective clothing because the patient’s illness is contagious.
words.zh: (形) 易傳染的；有傳染性的　(形) 易蔓延的；容易傳播的

words.d: A corpse is a dead body of a human.
words.e: After the accident, the corpse was taken to the hospital.
words.zh: (名) 屍體；死屍

words.d: A crisis is a situation that is extremely stressful or dangerous.
words.e: The airport workers’ strike led to a crisis at the airport.
words.zh: (名) 危機；困境　(名) 關鍵時刻；轉折點

words.d: To cure means to cause an illness or injury to end or disappear.
words.e: My dentist cured me of my toothache.
words.zh: (動) 治癒；治療　(名) 療法；治療方法

words.d: If something is deformed, it is not shaped normally and may appear ugly.
words.e: Even though the carrot was deformed, it was still safe to eat.
words.zh: 如果某物變形，則形狀不正常，可能顯得難看。

words.d: To discriminate means to judge people according to their looks.
words.e: They discriminated against her because she was different.
words.zh: (動) 歧視；區別對待　(動) 區分；辨別

words.d: An embassy is where government officials work in a foreign country.
words.e: If you lose your passport, you should contact the embassy.
words.zh: (名) 大使館；使館

words.d: To extinguish a fire means to make it stop.
words.e: Michael extinguished the small fire with the hose.
words.zh: (動) 熄滅；撲滅　(動) 消除；結束

words.d: Flint is a hard stone that people used to make weapons for hunting.
words.e: There are a lot of flint tools in the museum.
words.zh: 燧石是一種堅硬的石頭，人們曾用它製作狩獵武器。

words.d: To harass someone means to annoy or trouble them.
words.e: The children harassed their mother because they wanted her attention.
words.zh: (動) 騷擾；困擾　(動) 持續攻擊；折磨

words.d: To integrate means to join, communicate and socialize.
words.e: Governments want immigrants to integrate with the population.
words.zh: (動) 整合；融合　(動) 使…融入；使…一體化

words.d: If something is miniature, it is very small.
words.e: The boy enjoys playing with his miniature train set in his bedroom.
words.zh: (形) 極小的；縮小的　(名) 微小物；縮小版

words.d: Nutrition is the process by which people use food to stay healthy.
words.e: It is important to pay attention to nutrition if you want to be an athlete.
words.zh: (名) 營養；營養學　(形) 營養的；有益健康的

words.d: If something happens promptly, it happens quickly or on time.
words.e: Arrive promptly, or we won’t have time to discuss everything.
words.zh: (副) 迅速地；立即地；準時地

words.d: A technician is a person who is skilled in electronic or mechanical work.
words.e: I need to call the technician to help me with my computer.
words.zh: (名) 技術人員；技師

words.d: The tropics are the areas of land and sea close to the equator.
words.e: People like to go to the tropics for vacation because it’s warm.
words.zh: 熱帶地區是指靠近赤道的陸地與海域。

words.d: If something is beneficial, it is good for you.
words.e: Drinking milk everyday is beneficial to your bones.
words.zh: (形) 有益的；有利的　(形) 受惠的；受益的

words.d: A birthplace is a place where a person is born or where something started.
words.e: China is the birthplace of chopsticks.
words.zh: 出生地是一個人誕生或某事物起源的地方。

words.d: The capacity of something is the amount of things that can be put in it.
words.e: The parking lot has reached its full capacity.
words.zh: (名) 容量；能力　(名) 職位；身分

words.d: If something is comparative, it is being judged based on something else.
words.e: The money that John has is comparative to most other adults.
words.zh: (形) 比較的；相對的　(名) 比較級

words.d: If something is comprehensive, it has all the details about something else.
words.e: The teacher gave us a comprehensive review for the exam.
words.zh: (形) 全面的；廣泛的　(形) 綜合性的；包含一切的

words.d: To conserve something is to protect it from being ruined or used completely.
words.e: The group worked to conserve the beauty of Europe’s national parks.
words.zh: (動) 保護；保存　節約；節省

words.d: If something is crucial, it is extremely important to another thing.
words.e: Clean air is crucial to the survival of humans, plants, and animals.
words.zh: (形) 關鍵的；決定性的　(形) 極其重要的；至關緊要的

words.d: Cumulative describes an increase by adding one after another.
words.e: The cumulative snowfall in the area is 50 centimeters per year.
words.zh: (形) 累積的；逐漸增加的

words.d: To deposit something is to put it into a place or another thing.
words.e: I deposited the money into my bank account.
words.zh: (名) 保證金；定金　(動) 存放；沉澱　(形) 沉積的

words.d: To distribute something is to give it to a number of people.
words.e: The teacher distributed crayons and markers to his students.
words.zh: (動) 分配；發放　(動) 分散；散佈

words.d: The equator is an imaginary line that splits the Earth into north and south.
words.e: The equator crosses the northern part of South America.
words.zh: (名) 赤道；地球中心的想像圓線

words.d: Exotic describes something unusual because it is from far away.
words.e: Rebecca tried many exotic foods on her trip to Africa.
words.zh: (形) 異國的；來自外國的　奇特的；不尋常的

words.d: If something is federal, it comes from the government of a country.
words.e: Sometimes federal laws are different from state laws.
words.zh: (形) 聯邦的；聯邦政府的

words.d: A formation is the way that something is made.
words.e: The formation of ice happens when water freezes.
words.zh: (名) 形成；構成　(名) 隊伍排列；陣形

words.d: The frequency of something is the number of times that it happens.
words.e: The frequency of rainstorms is very high, especially during the spring.
words.zh: (名) 頻率；出現次數　(名) 頻道；頻率

words.d: An objective is a goal or plan that someone has.
words.e: My objective this week is to finish my homework by 7:30 every night.
words.zh: (形) 客觀的；不偏不倚的　(名) 目標；目的

words.d: Oxygen is a gas that all living things need to breathe.
words.e: My aunt believes that the oxygen in the country is cleaner than in the city.
words.zh: (名) 氧；氧氣

words.d: A rainforest is a forest that is in a place where it rains very often.
words.e: The rainforest is home to many animals.
words.zh: 雨林是位於經常下雨地區的森林。

words.d: A strategy is a plan for how to do something.
words.e: The team came up with a strategy to win the game.
words.zh: (名) 策略；戰略

words.d: If an area is wooded, it is covered with trees.
words.e: Jim and Ben decided to go hiking in the wooded area by the river.
words.zh: 若某地區樹木茂密，則稱其為林地。

words.d: Avail is help. It is often used in the achievement of a goal.
words.e: His studying was to no avail because he failed the test.
words.zh: (動) 有用；幫助　(名) 利益；幫助

words.d: To expand is to become bigger in size.
words.e: A balloon will expand as you blow air into it.
words.zh: (動) 擴大；擴展　(動) 膨脹；展開

words.d: To define means to clearly state, show, or explain what something is.
words.e: People define success in many different ways.
words.zh: (動) 定義；解釋　(動) 確定；說明

words.d: To dread is to be afraid of something that could, or is going to, happen.
words.e: I dread the idea that I will not get into college.
words.zh: (動) 害怕；懼怕　(名) 恐懼；害怕

words.d: If something is fundamental, it is a basic part of something.
words.e: The fundamental rules of basketball are easy.
words.zh: (形) 基本的；根本的　(名) 基本要素；基礎

words.d: If something is horrifying, it is frightening and very unpleasant.
words.e: There was a horrifying car accident today.
words.zh: (形) 令人恐懼的；驚人的　(動) 使恐懼；使害怕

words.d: If someone is incredulous about something, they do not believe that it is true.
words.e: She was incredulous that monkeys could ever drive a car.
words.zh: (形) 不相信的；懷疑的　(形) 難以置信的；令人難以接受的

words.d: To linger is to last for a long time.
words.e: The smell of fresh cookies lingered in the bakery.
words.zh: (動) 逗留；停留　(動) 緩慢消散；拖延

words.d: An organism is a living thing, especially a very small one.
words.e: We studied the organism on the microscope.
words.zh: (名) 生物；有機體

words.d: To paraphrase is to make someone else’s writing or speech shorter.
words.e: The students were asked to paraphrase the story they just heard.
words.zh: 改寫是指將他人的寫作或發言變得更簡短。

words.d: A plague is a serious disease that quickly spreads to many people.
words.e: A plague in Europe killed millions of people.
words.zh: (名) 瘟疫；災害　(動) 折磨；使苦惱

words.d: If something happens presently, it is happening right now.
words.e: Presently, our profits are good, but by next year we can do even better.
words.zh: (副) 目前；不久

words.d: If something is random, it happens without any pattern or reason.
words.e: Young children often ask random questions.
words.zh: (形) 隨機的；任意的

words.d: A riot is a crowd that reacts to bad news by violently breaking laws.
words.e: A riot broke out after the candidate lost the election.
words.zh: (名) 暴動；騷亂

words.d: To scribble is to write something quickly without caring about how it looks.
words.e: I scribbled a rough diagram of our plan and gave it to him.
words.zh: (名) 潦草的筆跡；亂寫　(動) 潦草地寫；亂畫

words.d: A shrine is a religious building built to honor a person, event, or god.
words.e: He prayed at the shrine for an hour.
words.zh: (名) 神社；聖地

words.d: Solitude is the state of being totally alone.
words.e: John lives a life of solitude because he doesn’t get along well with people.
words.zh: (名) 孤獨；獨處

words.d: If a contrast is stark, then the things being compared are utterly different.
words.e: There is a stark contrast between their test scores.
words.zh: (形) 鮮明的；赤裸的

words.d: To summon a person is to ask them to come to you.
words.e: We summoned the doctor as soon as we noticed she was sick.
words.zh: (動) 召喚；召集

words.d: To worsen is to get worse.
words.e: The weather suddenly worsened, and we had to stay inside.
words.zh: (動) 惡化；變壞

words.d: An automobile is a car.
words.e: The first automobiles were very different from the ones that exist today.
words.zh: (名) 汽車；自動車

words.d: A candidate is a person who is competing to win something such as a job.
words.e: Alice is the best candidate for the job.
words.zh: (名) 候選人；應徵者　(名) 考生；申請人

words.d: If something is confidential, it must be kept secret.
words.e: The information from the meeting is confidential.
words.zh: (形) 機密的；保密的　(形) 信任的；親密的

words.d: If something is corporate, it is related to a large business.
words.e: Tom enjoys working in the corporate world.
words.zh: (形) 公司的；企業的　(名) 公司；企業

words.d: To enhance something is to make it better.
words.e: Amy’s blue shirt really enhances the color of her eyes.
words.zh: (動) 提高；增強　(動) 改善；增進

words.d: An era is a period of time that has something special about it.
words.e: During the medieval era, knights wore protective armor.
words.zh: (名) 時代；年代　(名) 時期；紀元

words.d: A guideline is a rule about how to do something.
words.e: Before they began the project, the teacher gave them some guidelines.
words.zh: (名) 指導方針；準則　(名) 指引；標準

words.d: To incorporate is to add something to another thing.
words.e: I decided to incorporate a new ingredient into my cake recipe.
words.zh: (動) 併入；納入　(動) 成立公司；設立法人

words.d: To interact is to talk to or do something with another person.
words.e: The kids began to interact when the adults left the room.
words.zh: (動) 互動；相互作用　(名) 互動；交互作用

words.d: An interval is the time between two things happening.
words.e: Tony rested for brief intervals while he worked in the yard.
words.zh: (名) 間隔；間隙　(名) 休息時間；中場休息

words.d: If something is mobile, it can be moved easily.
words.e: Mobile phones are popular because you can take them anywhere.
words.zh: (形) 可移動的；流動的　(名) 手機；行動電話

words.d: To modify something is to change it a little bit.
words.e: I modified my outfit by adding a belt to it.
words.zh: (動) 修改；改變　(動) 調整；改進

words.d: If two things are parallel, they are the same distance away from each other.
words.e: There are two yellow parallel lines dividing both sides of traffic.
words.zh: (形) 平行的；(動) 與…平行；(名) 平行線

words.d: A phenomenon is something that can be seen as it is happening.
words.e: I was amazed when I saw the phenomenon of shooting stars.
words.zh: (名) 現象

words.d: To pollute means to make air, water, or land dirty, unclean, or foul.
words.e: The careless factory polluted the river with chemicals.
words.zh: (動) 污染

words.d: To ridicule is to make fun of something in a mean way.
words.e: The other students ridicule Peter’s foreign accent.
words.zh: (名) 嘲笑；奚落　(動) 嘲笑；奚落

words.d: If something is solar, it is related to the sun.
words.e: Using solar energy is good for the environment.
words.zh: (形) 太陽的；太陽能的

words.d: A territory is a piece of land that belongs to a country but isn’t a state.
words.e: Gibraltar is a territory of Great Britain.
words.zh: (名) 領土；領域

words.d: A tournament is a competition, usually with many people participating.
words.e: My dad is playing in a golf tournament tomorrow.
words.zh: (名) 錦標賽；聯賽

words.d: Transportation is any type of vehicle that can carry people or things.
words.e: I don’t have a car, so my normal transportation is the train.
words.zh: (名) 交通；運輸

words.d: A bill is a statement of money owed for goods or a service.
words.e: I have so many bills that I do not know how to pay for them all.
words.zh: (名) 帳單；帳款　(名) 鈔票；紙幣　(名) 法案；議案

words.d: A boundary is the line where one area of land stops, and another begins.
words.e: This fence shows the boundary between our yard and yours.
words.zh: (名) 邊界；界線　(名) 界限；範圍

words.d: Chaos is a situation that is confusing and not ordered.
words.e: His presentation was in chaos. I couldn’t understand what he meant.
words.zh: (名) 混亂；混沌　(名) 極度混亂的狀態；無序

words.d: If a person is consistent, they keep the same behavior or attitude.
words.e: Sara comes in every day and is our most consistent worker.
words.zh: (形) 一致的；前後相符的　(形) 持續的；堅持不懈的

words.d: A cyclone is a large storm with heavy rain and winds that spin in a circle.
words.e: Hundreds of homes were damaged by the cyclone.
words.zh: 氣旋是一種伴隨暴雨與旋轉強風的大型風暴。

words.d: If someone or something is doomed, they are going to fail or be destroyed.
words.e: Since I spent all my money, my date with Jane is doomed.
words.zh: (形) 注定失敗的；必然滅亡的

words.d: An heir is a person who receives money or property of someone who dies.
words.e: The princess was the heir to the king and queen’s throne.
words.zh: (名) 繼承人；法定繼承人　(名) 繼承者；承人

words.d: If something is martial, it is related to fighting or war.
words.e: Karate is a martial art that began many years ago in Japan.
words.zh: (形) 軍事的；戰爭的　(形) 好戰的；勇武的

words.d: If food is organic, it is grown without adding chemicals to it.
words.e: The organic carrots are more expensive, but they’re better for you.
words.zh: (形) 有機的；有機體的

words.d: Poultry is a bird, such as a chicken, that is used for meat and eggs.
words.e: He raises poultry and sells their meat for extra money.
words.zh: (名) 家禽；家禽肉

words.d: To scramble is to move somewhere quickly and desperately.
words.e: The hikers scrambled down the side of the hill.
words.zh: (動) 爭搶；攀爬

words.d: A sergeant is a soldier or police officer of middle rank.
words.e: He was promoted to sergeant after a year in the army.
words.zh: (名) 士官；中士

words.d: If you describe something as sheer, it is complete and very strong.
words.e: I was impressed by her sheer dedication to jogging.
words.zh: (形) 純粹的；完全的；陡峭的

words.d: A stance is an attitude about an issue that someone states clearly.
words.e: My stance is that using oil and gas is bad for the environment.
words.zh: (名) 立場；姿勢

words.d: A telegraph is a method of sending electric messages on wires.
words.e: In the 1900s, the telegraph was the fastest way to send a message.
words.zh: (名) [`tɛlə.græf] 電報機；電報　(動) [tə`lɛgrəf] 打電報；電傳

words.d: Textile is cloth that has been woven or knitted.
words.e: The blue textile was going to be used to make blouses.
words.zh: (名) 紡織品；紡織業　(形) 紡織的

words.d: A tornado is a tube-shaped formation of air that spins very quickly.
words.e: During a tornado, the safest place to be is underground.
words.zh: (名) 龍捲風

words.d: A typhoon is a large tropical storm that moves in circles.
words.e: Thousands of people lost electricity after a typhoon hit Australia.
words.zh: (名) 颱風

words.d: To wail is to show sadness by crying loudly.
words.e: The baby wailed because it was hungry.
words.zh: (動) 哭嚎；哀號

words.d: A wardrobe is the collection of all of a person’s clothing.
words.e: She bought some new clothes to expand her wardrobe.
words.zh: (名) 衣櫥；衣櫃

words.d: To allot something means to give it to someone.
words.e: The coach allotted each team five minutes to prepare a strategy.
words.zh: (動) 分配；分派　(動) 撥給；預留

words.d: To appall means to horrify, shock, or disgust someone.
words.e: The boy was appalled when he saw the accident.
words.zh: (動) 使震驚；使驚愕　使感到厭惡

words.d: A cache is a hiding place for valuable things.
words.e: The pirates kept their jewelry in a cache hidden in a cave.
words.zh: 藏匿處是指存放貴重物品的隱蔽地點。

words.d: Convenience is a state of being able to do something with little effort.
words.e: The Internet allows consumers to shop at their own convenience.
words.zh: (名) 便利；方便　(名) 便利設施；便利商店

words.d: A dearth is an amount or supply which is not large enough.
words.e: There is a dearth of money in my bank account. I can’t afford a new car.
words.zh: (名) 缺乏；短缺　(名) 不足量

words.d: If a thing you do is deliberate, you intend to do it.
words.e: She made a deliberate effort to save money each month.
words.zh: (動) 仔細考慮；深思熟慮　(形) 故意的；蓄意的；深思熟慮的

words.d: When something is dire, it is terrible and very serious.
words.e: The tornado created a dire situation for the small town.
words.zh: (形) 極度的；危急的　(形) 嚴重的；絕望的

words.d: To elapse means to pass, as in seconds, minutes, or hours.
words.e: A few seconds must elapse before you can take another picture.
words.zh: (動) 時間流逝；經過　(名) 時間的流逝；間隔

words.d: Empathy is sharing or understanding another person’s feelings.
words.e: The caring nurse had empathy for her patients.
words.zh: (名) 同理心；換位思考　(動) 感同身受；體諒

words.d: When something is fanciful, it is unusual or unrealistic.
words.e: The girl had fanciful ideas about doing well in school without studying.
words.zh: (形) 充滿想像的；異想天開的　(形) 虛構的；不切實際的

words.d: To gripe means to complain constantly.
words.e: Lawrence always gripes when he has to do chores.
words.zh: (動) 抱怨；發牢騷；使不滿　(名) 不滿；怨言

words.d: When something is grueling, it is very hard to do.
words.e: The climber faced the grueling task of reaching the top of the steep mountain.
words.zh: (形) 極其疲勞的；令人筋疲力盡的　(動) 使極度疲勞；使筋疲力盡

words.d: When something is mundane, it is boring, common, or ordinary.
words.e: The man had the mundane chore of raking thousands of leaves into piles.
words.zh: (形) 平凡的；單調的　(形) 日常的；普通的

words.d: To opt is to make a choice, especially when deciding in favor of something.
words.e: My brother likes chocolate ice cream, but I always opt for vanilla.
words.zh: (動) 選擇；決定　(動) 選用；採用

words.d: Outrage is a very strong emotion of anger or shock.
words.e: Tommy was feeling outrage when his parents said he couldn’t go to the dance.
words.zh: (名) [ˈaʊtˌredʒ] 憤怒；暴行　(動) [aʊtˈredʒ] 激怒；使震怒

words.d: When an amount of something is paltry, it is very small.
words.e: The poor man had a paltry sum of money.
words.zh: 當某物的數量是微薄的，表示它非常少。

words.d: To rectify something means to correct it.
words.e: I quickly rectified the spelling mistakes that I had on my essay.
words.zh: (動) 改正；糾正

words.d: When someone is resourceful, they are good at dealing with hard situations.
words.e: After his boat sunk, Matt was resourceful enough to build a raft.
words.zh: 當某人足智多謀時，他們善於應對困難情況。

words.d: Sustenance is food and water needed to keep a person, animal, or plant alive.
words.e: Without the proper sustenance, the man will starve.
words.zh: 維生之物是指維持人、動物或植物生存所需的食物與水。

words.d: When something is tedious, it is long, frustrating, and boring.
words.e: His job involved a lot of tedious typing, filing, and organizing.
words.zh: (形) 乏味的；冗長的

words.d: An abbey is a house or group of houses where monks or nuns live.
words.e: When the monk returned to the abbey, he went immediately to his bedroom.
words.zh: (名) 修道院；大教堂

words.d: If something is abundant, then it is available in large quantities.
words.e: Cakes, cookies, and candy were so abundant that the child was very happy.
words.zh: (形) 豐富的；大量的

words.d: To adjoin something means to be next to or attached to something else.
words.e: She can listen to her brother’s conversations because her room adjoins his.
words.zh: (動) 與...相鄰；毗鄰　(動) 靠近；接近

words.d: If something is ample, then it is enough or more than enough.
words.e: There was an ample supply of oats to feed the horses.
words.zh: (形) 充足的；豐富的　(形) 寬敞的；寬闊的

words.d: If a place is arid, then it is hot and dry and gets very little or no rain.
words.e: Not many plants grow in the arid desert.
words.zh: (形) 乾旱的；缺水的　(形) 枯燥的；無趣的

words.d: A cathedral is an important and often large and beautifully built church.
words.e: The large cathedral is full of people on Sunday mornings.
words.zh: (名) 大教堂；主教座堂　(形) 主要的；中心的

words.d: To deprive someone of something means to not let them have it.
words.e: Because the child was bad, she was deprived of her dessert after dinner.
words.zh: (動) 剝奪；使失去　(動) 使處於貧困

words.d: A drought is a long period of time in which little or no rain falls.
words.e: After three months of drought, the vegetation and trees started dying.
words.zh: (名) 乾旱；缺水　(動) 使乾旱

words.d: If someone is eligible, then they are permitted to do or have something.
words.e: Only people who bought tickets were eligible to win a prize.
words.zh: (形) 有資格的；符合條件的　(形) 合適的；可取的

words.d: To fast means to go without food or drink for a period of time.
words.e: In her religion, they fast for five days and then have a big feast.
words.zh: (形) 快的；迅速的　(副) 快地；迅速地

words.d: To grumble means to complain.
words.e: He grumbled about having to work late on Friday.
words.zh: (動) 抱怨；嘟囔　(名) 抱怨；低沉的聲音

words.d: If someone goes inland, they travel into the center of a country or land.
words.e: The river curved inland near the campground.
words.zh: (形) 內陸的；靠近內陸的　(名) 內陸；內地

words.d: Moisture is small drops of water in the air or on a surface.
words.e: If you breathe on a window, moisture from your breath collects on the glass.
words.zh: (名) 濕度；水分　(動) 使濕潤；增加水分

words.d: If something happens nonetheless, then it occurs despite some other thing.
words.e: She tried to keep the dog out of the mud, but it got dirty nonetheless.
words.zh: (連) 然而；但是　(副) 儘管如此；仍然

words.d: An oath is a formal, often public, promise.
words.e: Judges must take an oath to be fair to everyone in court.
words.zh: (名) 誓言；誓約　(動) 發誓；起誓

words.d: A prairie is a large flat area of grassland.
words.e: The prairie was perfect for a farm because there were hills and trees.
words.zh: (名) 大草原；牧場

words.d: If something is ragged, then it is old, torn, and falling apart.
words.e: They could see his toes through the holes in his ragged shoes.
words.zh: (形) 破爛的；衣衫襤褸的

words.d: If an area of land is rugged, then it is rocky and difficult to travel through.
words.e: Their car couldn’t make it far along the rugged roads.
words.zh: (形) 崎嶇的；堅韌的

words.d: If something is scarce, then it is in a very small amount.
words.e: When gasoline was scarce, we rode our bikes, instead of driving, to school.
words.zh: (形) 缺乏的；稀有的

words.d: To speculate means to guess about something.
words.e: My sister looked at the sky and speculated that it would rain tomorrow.
words.zh: (動) 推測；投機

words.d: If something is analytic, it is related to logic and reasoning.
words.e: The analytic article criticized the new plan and presented one of its own.
words.zh: 若某物是分析性的，則與邏輯和推理相關。

words.d: To assert a fact or belief means to state it with confidence.
words.e: He asserted that his mother’s cooking was better than his best friend’s.
words.zh: (動) 主張；聲稱　(動) 堅持；維護

words.d: A bachelor is an unmarried man.
words.e: Since he was a bachelor, Jason did his shopping by himself.
words.zh: (名) 學士；未婚男子　(名) 單身漢；獨身者

words.d: Calculus is an advanced type of mathematics.
words.e: By using calculus, scientists determined small changes in the stars’ brightness.
words.zh: 微積分是一種高等數學。

words.d: If something is celestial, it is related to the sky or to outer space.
words.e: Comets are celestial objects that are rarely seen.
words.zh: (形) 天空的；天體的　(形) 極其美妙的；夢幻般的

words.d: If something is cognitive, it is related to learning and knowing things.
words.e: After her physical examination, her cognitive strengths were tested.
words.zh: (形) 認知的；涉及思考的　(名) 認知能力

words.d: A collision is the act of two things hitting into each another.
words.e: The collision between the two cars created a loud noise.
words.zh: (名) 碰撞；衝突　(名) 抵觸；矛盾

words.d: If someone is competent, they are able to think or act successfully.
words.e: Competent employees are much better than unknowledgeable ones.
words.zh: (形) 勝任的；能幹的　(形) 足夠的；充分的

words.d: A diploma is a certificate proving that someone has completed their studies.
words.e: After four years of college, Mary finally had a diploma.
words.zh: (名) 文憑；畢業證書　(名) 外交文件；國家證書

words.d: To excel at a subject or activity means to be very good at it.
words.e: Jenny excels at playing the piano.
words.zh: (動) 擅長；傑出　(動) 超越；勝過

words.d: Geology is the study of the Earth’s natural structures and how they change.
words.e: Because he studied geology, he knew how the mountains were formed.
words.zh: (名) 地質學；地質構造

words.d: To harness something means to control and use it, usually to make energy.
words.e: The sails harness the wind in order to move.
words.zh: (名) 馬具；安全帶　(動) 利用；駕馭；套上馬具

words.d: An intellect is a person’s ability to understand things easily.
words.e: She was known for her quick and strong intellect as well as her beauty.
words.zh: (名) 智力；理智　(名) 聰慧的人；知識份子

words.d: If someone is keen, they are intelligent.
words.e: Only the keenest of students could have solved that math problem.
words.zh: (形) 熱情的；積極的　(形) 敏銳的；靈敏的

words.d: Mythology is a group of stories from a particular country or region.
words.e: Egyptian mythology was the basis for a religion.
words.zh: (名) 神話；神話故事　(名) 神話體系；傳說

words.d: Physiology is the study of the various parts of living things.
words.e: His work in physiology helped him understand how the human body works.
words.zh: (名) 生理學

words.d: If something is radioactive, then it lets out, or is related to, radiation.
words.e: Radioactive materials can be very bad for anyone’s health.
words.zh: (形) 放射性的

words.d: Relativity is a set of ideas about time and space developed by Albert Einstein.
words.e: Relativity teaches that light travels at the same speed in the universe.
words.zh: (名) 相對論；相對性

words.d: Sociology is the study of human society, its organizations, and problems.
words.e: Sociology teaches that people’s problems are a result of their society.
words.zh: (名) 社會學

words.d: If something is theoretical, it is based on theory rather than experience.
words.e: His conclusion was only theoretical and not meant to be publicized.
words.zh: (形) 理論上的；假設的

words.d: An administrator is a person who controls a business, company, or organization.
words.e: Everyone in the store did whatever the administrator asked them to do.
words.zh: (名) 管理者；行政人員　(名) 遺產管理人

words.d: If someone is affluent, they are wealthy.
words.e: People in the city are usually more affluent than people in the country.
words.zh: (形) 富裕的；富有的　(形) 充足的；豐富的

words.d: To audit means to inspect financial records from a person or business.
words.e: The government usually audits companies that report lower than usual incomes.
words.zh: (名) 審計；稽核　(動) 審計；旁聽

words.d: To automate a company means to install machines or computers to do the work.
words.e: When the bank automated, it started installing ATM machines.
words.zh: (動) 自動化；使自動運作　(名) 自動化系統

words.d: To bribe someone means to illegally persuade them for a favor with money.
words.e: The judge was bribed so that she would set the suspect free.
words.zh: (名) 賄賂；贿金　(動) 贿赂；用賄賂收買

words.d: If someone is corrupt, they break the law for money or fame.
words.e: The corrupt policemen didn’t arrest the man because he gave them money.
words.zh: (形) 腐敗的；貪汙的　(動) 使腐敗；使墮落

words.d: To dispose of something means to get rid of it.
words.e: He disposed of the can by throwing it into the recycle bin.
words.zh: (動) 處理；丟棄　(動) 使傾向；使有意願

words.d: A headquarters is a building where the bosses of a company work.
words.e: He drove the long route to headquarters because it was a nice day.
words.zh: (名) 總部；司令部　(名) 指揮中心；總指揮處

words.d: An incentive is what makes a person want to do something.
words.e: The chance of winning a prize was incentive to get people to play the game.
words.zh: (名) 獎勵；動力　(形) 有激勵作用的；鼓勵性的

words.d: An infrastructure is a collection of services needed to run a society or business.
words.e: Power lines are important parts of a city’s infrastructure.
words.zh: (名) 基礎設施；基礎建設　(名) 公共設施；下層結構

words.d: To legislate means to make laws.
words.e: Senators have to legislate fairly, so most people will enjoy the benefits.
words.zh: (動) 立法；制定法律　(動) 通過法律

words.d: If something is legitimate, then it is acceptable according to the law.
words.e: She found a legitimate plan to raise extra funds for her vacation.
words.zh: (形) 合法的；正當的　(形) 真實的；正宗的

words.d: To manipulate something means to skillfully or unfairly control or affect it.
words.e: The Dr. manipulated the data to make it look like the cure was working.
words.zh: (動) 操縱；控制　巧妙處理；操作

words.d: Merchandise is goods ready to be purchased or sold.
words.e: The store added more merchandise because there were more shoppers.
words.zh: (名) 商品；貨物　(動) 推銷；販售商品

words.d: Retail is the activity of selling goods to the public, often for personal use.
words.e: Though cheap to make, once a t-shirt reaches retail, it costs ten times as much.
words.zh: (名) [ˋritel] 零售　(動) [riˋtel] 零售

words.d: Revenue is the income made by a company.
words.e: The new products really increased the business’s monthly revenue.
words.zh: (名) 收入；稅收

words.d: Rubbish is trash or waste.
words.e: The floor around the garbage can was covered with all kinds of rubbish.
words.zh: (名) 垃圾；廢物

words.d: A subsidy is money given by the government to companies to assist them.
words.e: The official gave the company a subsidy, so it could open two new factories.
words.zh: (名) 補助金；津貼

words.d: A transaction is an act of buying or selling something.
words.e: Because the clerk was new at the job, the simple transaction took a long time.
words.zh: (名) 交易；處理

words.d: To violate a law, rule, or agreement means to break it.
words.e: I was given a ticket because the policeman said I violated the speed limit.
words.zh: (動) 違反；違背

words.d: To assess something means to judge the structure, purpose, or quality of it.
words.e: She assessed the condition of the toy car before buying it.
words.zh: (動) 評估；評價　(動) 估計；判斷

words.d: To astonish someone means to greatly surprise them.
words.e: The amount of people that came to her party astonished her.
words.zh: (動) 使驚訝；使震驚　(動) 使吃驚；讓人感到意外

words.d: To commence something means to begin it.
words.e: His speech commenced with a “thankyou” to all who had helped him succeed.
words.zh: (動) 開始；著手　(名) 開始；開幕式

words.d: The essence of something is its important qualities or basic characteristics.
words.e: The essence of the argument was that both sides felt they had lost money.
words.zh: (名) 本質；精髓　(名) 精油；香精

words.d: To extract something means to remove it.
words.e: The dentist extracted the woman’s damaged tooth and put in a fake one.
words.zh: (動) [ɪkˈstrækt] 提取；萃取　(名) [ˈɛkstrækt] 精華；萃取物

words.d: If something is fabulous, it is extremely good.
words.e: This strawberry is the best I’ve ever had. It’s fabulous.
words.zh: (形) 極好的；了不起的　(形) 難以置信的；傳說中的

words.d: Haste is speed in movement or action.
words.e: In order to get to the meeting in time, he proceeds with haste.
words.zh: (名) 匆忙；急速　(動) 匆忙進行；加快速度

words.d: An impulse is a sudden thoughtless urge to do something.
words.e: Because of the scary noise, she had an impulse to run somewhere and hide.
words.zh: (名) 衝動；突然的慾望　(名) 推動力；刺激　(動) 推動；激勵

words.d: Latter describes something last in a series or the second choice of two things.
words.e: In the latter minutes of the game, the visitors scored the winning goal.
words.zh: (形) 後者的；後面的　(代) 後者；兩者中的後一個

words.d: A molecule is the smallest basic unit that makes up a physical substance.
words.e: A tiny drop of water is made up of thousands of molecules of water.
words.zh: (名) 分子；分子結構

words.d: If something is ongoing, then it is still happening or still growing.
words.e: The development of plants is ongoing because it takes time for them to mature.
words.zh: (形) 持續的；進行中的　(形) 不斷的；未完成的

words.d: If something is pharmaceutical, then it is related to the development of drugs.
words.e: Pharmaceutical companies discover new cures to illnesses all the time.
words.zh: (形) 製藥的；藥物的

words.d: If someone is precise, then they are exact and careful about their work.
words.e: The builder was very precise about where he placed the nails.
words.zh: (形) 精確的；明確的

words.d: Proximity is closeness in time, space, or relationships.
words.e: All the trees in the proximity of the beach had been cut down.
words.zh: (名) 接近；鄰近

words.d: Publicity is public attention given to someone or something by the media.
words.e: She received a lot of publicity after her performance in the film.
words.zh: (名) 宣傳；公開

words.d: A remedy is a cure for a disease, argument, or problem.
words.e: A good remedy for a headache is an aspirin and a glass of water.
words.zh: (名) 補救方法；治療法

words.d: The significance of something is the quality that makes it important.
words.e: The significance of the snowy weather was that we didn’t have to go to school.
words.zh: (名) 重要性；意義

words.d: If something is subsequent, then it comes after something else in time.
words.e: The flood and the subsequent rescue of those caught in the flood were on TV.
words.zh: (形) 後續的；隨後的

words.d: If something is synthetic, then it is made to be like something natural.
words.e: Clothing made out of synthetic fabrics is very effective at keeping people warm.
words.zh: (形) 合成的；人造的

words.d: If something is terminal, then it causes or results in death.
words.e: Since his condition was not terminal, he felt a great sense of relief.
words.zh: (名) 終點站；航站　(形) 末期的；終點的

words.d: The altitude of a place is its height above sea level.
words.e: The air was thin at such a high altitude on the mountain.
words.zh: (名) 海拔；高度　(名) 高度角；仰角

words.d: A coastline is the outline of a country’s coast.
words.e: He noticed that most of the cities in Australia are on the coastline.
words.zh: (名) 海岸線；海岸邊界

words.d: To deter means to prevent or discourage someone from doing something.
words.e: Icy roads deter people from driving their cars.
words.zh: (動) 阻止；制止　遏阻；勸阻

words.d: To devise something means to have an idea or plan about it in the mind.
words.e: The thieves devised a plan to steal the diamonds.
words.zh: (動) 設計；想出　創造；發明

words.d: Expertise is the knowledge and skills to do something well.
words.e: John has a lot of advertising expertise. He can sell anything!
words.zh: (名) 專業知識；專門技能　(名) 專家的意見；專業判斷

words.d: A fracture is a crack or break in something.
words.e: Don’t stand on that teg because there is a fracture. It might get worse.
words.zh: (名) 骨折；裂縫　(動) 使骨折；破裂

words.d: To impair something means to damage it or make it worse.
words.e: Drinking coffee impairs my ability to go to sleep.
words.zh: (動) 損害；削弱　(動) 妨礙；減少

words.d: To implement something means to ensure that what has been planned is done.
words.e: The school decided to implement a new teaching strategy.
words.zh: (動) [`Impləˌmɛnt] 實施；執行　(名) [`Impləmənt] 工具；器具

words.d: If something is indigenous, it is originally from, or native to, a place.
words.e: Tomatoes are indigenous to the Americas.
words.zh: (形) 土著的；原住民的　(形) 本地的；本土的

words.d: Insight is a deep and accurate understanding of something.
words.e: The physics textbook gave the student new insight about gravity.
words.zh: (名) 洞察；領悟　(名) 深刻的理解

words.d: A limb is a large branch on a tree.
words.e: The monkey sat on the tree limb and enjoyed a piece of fruit.
words.zh: (名) 四肢；手臂或腿　(名) 樹枝；分支

words.d: A migraine is a painful headache that makes one feel sick.
words.e: My sister gets a migraine every time she has a lot of stress.
words.zh: (名) 偏頭痛；嚴重頭痛

words.d: Optimism is the feeling of being hopeful about the future or success of something.
words.e: The mother had optimism about her children’s futures.
words.zh: (名) 樂觀；樂觀主義　(形) 樂觀的

words.d: When something is peculiar, it is strange, sometimes in a bad way.
words.e: That peculiar smell coming from the kitchen reminds me of rotten eggs.
words.zh: (形) 奇怪的；獨特的

words.d: When a person is proficient at something, they can do it well.
words.e: Secretaries are proficient at typing quickly.
words.zh: (形) 熟練的；精通的

words.d: A quest is a long and difficult search for something.
words.e: The treasure hunter went on a quest to find an ancient gold necklace.
words.zh: (名) 追尋；探索

words.d: A ridge is a long, narrow piece of raised land.
words.e: The brown bear walked along the edge of the mountain ridge.
words.zh: (名) 山脊；屋脊

words.d: A spouse is the person to whom someone is married.
words.e: I live in a home with my spouse and our two children.
words.zh: (名) 配偶

words.d: To thrust means to push or move something quickly with a lot of force.
words.e: The boxer thrust his fist into the punching bag.
words.zh: (名) 推；刺　(動) 推；刺

words.d: To tolerate something means to be able to accept it even when it is unpleasant.
words.e: When you are in a hurry, it can be hard to tolerate traffic signals.
words.zh: (動) 容忍；忍受

words.d: If a plant or animal is aquatic, it lives or grows in water.
words.e: The dolphin is an aquatic mammal.
words.zh: (形) 水生的；與水有關的

words.d: The biosphere is the earth’s surface and atmosphere where there are living things.
words.e: Birds, trees, and worms all thrive in the biosphere.
words.zh: 生物圈是地球表面及大氣層中有生物存在的區域。

words.d: When something is bizarre, it is very strange.
words.e: My bizarre dreams make no sense to me when I am awake.
words.zh: (形) 奇怪的；古怪的　(形) 離奇的；荒誕的

words.d: Celsius is a scale for measuring temperature.
words.e: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius.
words.zh: (名) 攝氏溫度；攝氏度數

words.d: If something is coarse, that means it has a rough texture.
words.e: The coarse sweater made my skin itch.
words.zh: (形) 粗糙的；粗纖維的　(形) 粗魯的；不禮貌的

words.d: A companion is a person that someone spends a lot of time with.
words.e: I always walk to school with my companion Frank.
words.zh: (名) 同伴；夥伴　(名) 陪伴者；伴侶

words.d: To digest means to swallow food and pass it through the body.
words.e: Allow some time for food to be digested before going swimming.
words.zh: (動) [dəˋdʒɛst] 消化；吸收　(名) [ˋdaɪdʒɛst] 文摘；摘要

words.d: The duration of an event is the time during which it happens.
words.e: The girls watched television for the duration of the evening.
words.zh: (名) 持續時間；時間長度　(名) 期間；時期

words.d: Ecology is the study of the environment and living things.
words.e: We study ecology to learn how to help improve the Earth.
words.zh: (名) 生態學；生態　(形) 生態的；環保的

words.d: A feat is an impressive or difficult achievement or action.
words.e: The elephant’s standing up on one leg was a feat.
words.zh: (名) 功績；壯舉　(動) 完成；達成

words.d: If something is infinite, it has no limit or end.
words.e: Many scientists believe that the universe is infinite.
words.zh: (形) 無限的；無窮的　(名) 無限；無窮

words.d: The nucleus is the central part of an atom or cell.
words.e: The nucleus is made up of many tiny particles.
words.zh: (名) 細胞核；原子核　(名) 中心；核心

words.d: A parasite is a tiny animal or plant that attaches to another animal to get food.
words.e: The sick dog was covered in parasites.
words.zh: 寄生蟲是附著在另一動物身上獲取食物的微小動植物。

words.d: When something is prominent, it is important and well known.
words.e: Queen Victoria was a prominent person in history.
words.zh: (形) 突出的；顯眼的

words.d: When something is repetitive, it is repeated many times and becomes boring.
words.e: Working on an assembly line making cars every day is a repetitive job.
words.zh: (形) 重複的；反覆的

words.d: If something is reproductive, it has to do with a living thing producing young.
words.e: The reproductive system of a plant is simple.
words.zh: 生殖性與生物產生後代有關。

words.d: When a place is temperate, it never gets too hot or cold.
words.e: In Peru, the weather is temperate and rarely gets too hot or cold.
words.zh: (形) 溫和的；適度的

words.d: Tolerance is the ability to accept something painful or unpleasant.
words.e: Boxers have a high tolerance for pain.
words.zh: (名) 寬容；容忍

words.d: To undergo an action means to have it happen to you.
words.e: The cancer patient undergoes treatments twice a week. gous
words.zh: (動) 經歷；接受

words.d: When someone is vulnerable, they are weak and without protection.
words.e: He felt very vulnerable when he was stranded in the desert.
words.zh: (形) 脆弱的；易受傷的

words.d: If someone is adept at something, they are very good at doing it.
words.e: The carpenter is very adept at building houses.
words.zh: (形) 熟練的；精通的　(動) 使適應；調整

words.d: If land is barren, it has no plants growing on it.
words.e: People cannot farm in barren lands.
words.zh: (形) 貧瘠的；不毛的　(形) 空虛的；無結果的

words.d: If something is ceramic, it is made of baked clay.
words.e: The house’s roof was made of ceramic tiles.
words.zh: (形) 陶瓷的；陶製的　(名) 陶瓷製品；陶器

words.d: If something is culinary, it is related to cooking.
words.e: I gained culinary skills after working in a restaurant for many years.
words.zh: (形) 烹飪的；廚藝的　(形) 烹飪有關的；食物的

words.d: If something is dense, it has a lot of things close together.
words.e: I easily became lost in the dense forest.
words.zh: (形) 密集的；稠密的　(形) 濃厚的；難以理解的

words.d: Dignity is the ability to be calm and worthy of respect.
words.e: When his company went out of business, he faced it with dignity.
words.zh: (名) 尊嚴；尊貴　(名) 莊重；體面

words.d: To dominate someone or something is to control them.
words.e: The loud man dominated the conversation.
words.zh: (動) 控制；主宰　(動) 佔優勢；居於首位

words.d: If something is edible, you can eat it.
words.e: We learn about edible plants when we go camping.
words.zh: (形) 可食用的；可吃的

words.d: If someone is hostile, they are angry and unfriendly.
words.e: We were happy to move away from our hostile neighbor.
words.zh: (形) 充滿敵意的；不友善的　(形) 敵對的；對立的

words.d: Your intake of food is the amount of food you take into your body.
words.e: The doctor said I needed to increase my intake of fruits and vegetables.
words.zh: (名) 攝入量；進氣　(動) 吸入；納入

words.d: If someone does something likewise, they do the same thing as someone else.
words.e: If Joe is staying away from school to go swimming, I want to do likewise.
words.zh: (副) 同樣地；也；同樣　(連) 同樣；並且

words.d: Malnutrition is the condition of not getting enough nutrients.
words.e: After eating only a meager amount of food, she suffered from malnutrition.
words.zh: 營養不良是指無法獲得足夠營養的狀況。

words.d: Medication is medicine or drugs given to people who are sick.
words.e: The doctor gave me medication to treat my illness.
words.zh: (名) 藥物；藥　(名) 醫療；用藥

words.d: A misconception is a wrong idea about something.
words.e: People once believed the misconception that the Earth is flat.
words.zh: (名) 錯誤的想法；誤解

words.d: If something is obscure, it is not well-known.
words.e: The old man travels the world in search of obscure books.
words.zh: (形) [əb`skjʊr] 模糊的；不清楚的　(動) [əbskj`ʊr] 使...模糊；隱瞞

words.d: To oppress someone means to rule over them in a cruel and unfair way.
words.e: Free speech had been oppressed in his country.
words.zh: (動) 壓迫；欺壓　(動) 使沮喪；使苦惱

words.d: To peel fruits and vegetables is to remove their skin.
words.e: We peeled the apple before eating it.
words.zh: (動) 剝皮；削皮　(名) 果皮

words.d: A prescription is permission from a doctor to get medicine.
words.e: The doctor gave me a prescription for my medication.
words.zh: (名) 處方；藥方

words.d: A respirator is a machine that helps weak or sick people breathe.
words.e: The man needed a respirator to breathe.
words.zh: 呼吸器是一種幫助虛弱或生病者呼吸的機器。

words.d: To strive is to struggle to achieve something.
words.e: People who strive to succeed often do.
words.zh: (動) 努力；奮鬥

words.d: If something is archaic, it is very old or outdated.
words.e: To be competitive, we must update our archaic equipment.
words.zh: 若某物是古老的，則表示它非常老舊或過時。

words.d: If someone is benevolent, they are kind and generous.
words.e: My father was a benevolent man and gave lots of money to charity.
words.zh: (形) 慈善的；仁慈的　(形) 友善的；善良的

words.d: Brass is a metal that is used to make musical instruments and ornaments.
words.e: Brass is used to make musical instruments like trumpets.
words.zh: (名) 黃銅；銅製品　(形) 黃銅製的；厚顏無恥的

words.d: Capitalism is an economic system where private companies make goods for profit.
