words.e: Most industries in the world today are based on capitalism.
words.zh: (名) 資本主義；資本制度

words.d: A component is a part of a larger machine.
words.e: Computers have many different components, so they are complicated to build.
words.zh: (名) 零件；組成部分　(形) 組成的；構成的

words.d: Dependence is a situation in which somebody relies on something else.
words.e: Young children have a dependence on their parents.
words.zh: (名) 依賴；依存　(名) 成癮；上癮

words.d: To diminish means to reduce or get smaller.
words.e: As the economy got worse, my savings diminished.
words.zh: (動) 減少；降低　(動) 縮小；衰退

words.d: A drawback is a disadvantage.
words.e: The drawback of having a car is that it is very expensive to maintain.
words.zh: (名) 缺點；不利之處　(名) 退縮；後退

words.d: A fad is something that is popular for a short time.
words.e: The hula hoop was a fad for a few years, but it soon lost its popularity.
words.zh: (名) 一時風尚；短暫流行的事物　(動) 逐漸消退；褪色

words.d: To impose means to interrupt or force your ideas on other people.
words.e: He imposes on his wife every morning by expecting her to make breakfast.
words.zh: (動) 強加；施加　課稅；徵收

words.d: Managerial describes something related to a manager or management.
words.e: Nancy has a managerial position at the bank.
words.zh: (形) 管理的；經理的　(形) 管理階層的；主管的

words.d: If something is medieval, it comes from the period between 650 and 1500 CE.
words.e: We visited a castle that was built during medieval times.
words.zh: (形) 中世紀的；中古的　(形) 古老的；陳舊的

words.d: If something is obsolete, it is not used anymore because something better exists.
words.e: Since computers became inexpensive, typewriters have become obsolete.
words.zh: (形) 過時的；廢棄的　(形) 老舊的；陳舊的

words.d: A peninsula is a large piece of land that is surrounded by the sea on three sides.
words.e: The state of Florida is an example of a peninsula.
words.zh: (名) 半島

words.d: If a person has prestige, people admire or respect them.
words.e: The young actress gained much prestige after she won an award.
words.zh: (名) 聲望；威信

words.d: A proportion is an amount that shows the link between the parts and the whole.
words.e: Only a small proportion of the people in this town actually work here.
words.zh: (名) 比例；部分

words.d: If something is radical, it is very new or different.
words.e: The president is planning to make some radical changes to the law.
words.zh: (形) 根本的；激進的

words.d: To refute something means to prove that it is false or incorrect.
words.e: The bank manager has refuted the claims that he lied to his customers.
words.zh: (動) 反駁；駁斥

words.d: If something is spectacular, it looks or sounds very impressive.
words.e: There was a spectacular fireworks display in the park at New Year.
words.zh: (形) 壯觀的；引人入勝的

words.d: To weave means to make cloth using horizontal and vertical threads.
words.e: We saw a woman weave a blanket on our vacation to South America.
words.zh: (動) 編織；編造

words.d: An accountant is a person whose job is to keep financial accounts.
words.e: The accountant helped me keep track of my money.
words.zh: (名) 會計師；會計人員

words.d: A capitalist is a business person who invests in trade and industry for profit.
words.e: The capitalist invested in a factory that made wheat into cereal.
words.zh: (名) 資本家；從事資本主義的人

words.d: Contempt is the feeling of having no respect for something.
words.e: The judge had contempt for the wicked criminal.
words.zh: (名) 輕蔑；蔑視　(動) 輕蔑；鄙視

words.d: To dedicate oneself to something means to put a lot of time and effort into it.
words.e: The nun dedicated herself to helping people in need.
words.zh: (動) 致力於；奉獻　(動) 獻身；專注

words.d: A ditch is a narrow hole cut into the ground by a road or a field.
words.e: When the car slid off of the road, it fell into the ditch.
words.zh: (名) 溝渠；水溝　(動) 拋棄；放棄

words.d: An enterprise is a company or business.
words.e: My father owns an advertising enterprise.
words.zh: (名) 企業；事業　(動) 從事；承辦

words.d: When something is exquisite, it is very beautiful or pleasant.
words.e: The artist made exquisite watercolor paintings.
words.zh: (形) 精緻的；優美的　(形) 極度的；劇烈的

words.d: To finance someone or something means to provide money for them.
words.e: The government financed the scientist’s experiments with new weapons.
words.zh: (名) 財務；金錢　(動) 提供資金；籌資

words.d: When someone is indifferent toward something, they have a lack of interest in it.
words.e: Lisa is indifferent toward school. She doesn’t care what her final grades are.
words.zh: (形) 冷淡的；不關心的　(形) 普通的；平庸的

words.d: To irrigate means to supply water to land so that crops can grow.
words.e: In dry climates, it is important to irrigate fields of crops.
words.zh: 灌溉是指供水給土地以利作物生長。

words.d: To maximize something is to make it as great in amount, size, or importance.
words.e: You should exercise regularly to maximize a healthy lifestyle.
words.zh: (動) 最大化；使達到最大　(動) 充分利用；發揮到最大

words.d: When something is monetary, it relates to money.
words.e: A strong monetary policy is important for a country to be successful.
words.zh: (形) 貨幣的；金錢的　(形) 與金融制度相關的

words.d: A precaution is an action that is meant to stop something bad from happening.
words.e: Asa precaution, you should put on a heavy coat before going out in cold weather.
words.zh: (名) 預防措施；預防

words.d: Preliminary describes something that happens before a more important event.
words.e: The runners must do well in the preliminary races to qualify for the final race.
words.zh: (形) 初步的；預備的

words.d: To saturate something means to completely soak it with a liquid.
words.e: The sponge was saturated with soapy water and dripped all over the floor.
words.zh: (動) 使濕透；使飽和

words.d: The simplicity of something is the fact that it is easy to do or understand.
words.e: We were able to find the house thanks to the simplicity of the directions.
words.zh: (名) 簡單；簡樸

words.d: To sow seeds means to plant them in the ground.
words.e: He always sows his garden seeds in the springtime.
words.zh: (動) 播種；散播

words.d: Soy is a food made from soybeans, such as flour or butter.
words.e: Soy can be made into tofu, sauce, and also milk.
words.zh: (n.) 醬油

words.d: A spade is a tool used for digging.
words.e: The gardener used her spade to make a hole for the seed.
words.zh: 鏟子是一種用於挖掘的工具。

words.d: When something is upcoming, that means it will happen in the near future.
words.e: The kids were worried about their upcoming exam.
words.zh: (形) 即將到來的；即將發生的

words.d: When a bad thing is acute, it is very severe and intense.
words.e: When she fell out of the tree, the girl felt an acute pain in her arm.
words.zh: (形) 嚴重的；急性的　(形) 敏銳的；尖銳的

words.d: Aggression is behavior that is mean or violent to others.
words.e: The problem was only made worse by Mark’s aggression.
words.zh: (名) 侵略；攻擊　(名) 好鬥；挑釁

words.d: A banquet is a grand formal dinner.
words.e: Both families brought a lot of food for the wedding banquet.
words.zh: (名) 宴會；盛宴　(動) 設宴款待；享受盛宴

words.d: A biography is an account of someone’s life that is written by someone else.
words.e: We read a biography about Charles Darwin in science class.
words.zh: (名) 傳記；自傳　(名) 傳記文學；生平記錄

words.d: To boost something means to increase or improve it.
words.e: Lowering prices boosts customers’ interest in shopping.
words.zh: (動) 促進；增強　(名) 推動；提升

words.d: To clap means to hit one’s hands together to express pleasure or get attention.
words.e: After the speech, everyone in the crowd clapped their hands for the speaker.
words.zh: (動) 拍手；鼓掌　(名) 掌聲；拍擊聲

words.d: To compel someone to do something means to force them to do it.
words.e: Traffic signs compel drivers to drive safely.
words.zh: (動) 強迫；迫使　(動) 促使；驅使

words.d: The dominance of a person is their state of being more powerful than others.
words.e: Large gorillas hit their chests to express their dominance over others.
words.zh: (名) 優勢；統治　(動) 佔優勢；統治

words.d: When something is gorgeous, it is very pleasing and attractive.
words.e: The girl picked out a gorgeous dress to wear to the dance.
words.zh: (形) 非常美麗的；華麗的　(形) 極好的；令人驚嘆的

words.d: When something is inevitable, it is certain to happen or cannot be avoided.
words.e: It is inevitable that the days will get longer in the summer.
words.zh: (形) 不可避免的；必然的　(形) 無法阻止的；確定會發生的

words.d: A legacy is an effect that exists because of a person or thing in the past.
words.e: The legacy of the ancient Egyptians can be seen in their monuments.
words.zh: (名) 遺產；傳承　(名) 遺留物；影響

words.d: A masterpiece is a very good painting, novel, movie, or other work of art.
words.e: The Arc de Triomphe is considered a masterpiece in the world of architecture.
words.zh: (名) 傑作；大師之作　(名) 代表作；最佳作品

words.d: If there are multiple things, there are many of them.
words.e: When the stunt went wrong, the man suffered multiple injuries.
words.zh: (形) 多個的；多重的　(名) 倍數；多重項

words.d: To narrate a story means to write about it or read it aloud.
words.e: This story was written by John, but Aaron is narrating it to the crowd.
words.zh: (動) 敘述；講述　(動) 旁白；配音解說

words.d: When something is notorious, it is well-known because of something bad.
words.e: This area of town is notorious for gang activity.
words.zh: (形) 臭名昭著的；惡名遠播的　(形) 聲名狼藉的；臭名遠揚的

words.d: When something is outdated, it is old and no longer useful in modern time.
words.e: Tape players are becoming outdated because of digital music.
words.zh: (形) 過時的；落伍的

words.d: When a thing is talked about overall, the whole thing is considered.
words.e: Overall, the party was a huge success.
words.zh: (形) 整體的；全面的　(副) 總體上；大體上

words.d: A partiality is a tendency to prefer one thing to another.
words.e: She has a partiality for walking to school instead of driving.
words.zh: 偏愛是指傾向於偏好某事物勝過另一事物。

words.d: When an act is spontaneous, it is not planned. It happens suddenly.
words.e: My wife made a spontaneous decision to buy a new sofa while I was at work.
words.zh: (形) 自發的；自然的

words.d: A virtue is a good quality or way of behaving.
words.e: My best virtue is forgiveness.
words.zh: (名) 美德；優點

words.d: Anthropology is the study of people, society, and culture.
words.e: In anthropology class, I learned about simple tools that ancient cultures used.
words.zh: (名) 人類學；人類文化和社會的研究

words.d: To applaud means to clap in order to show approval.
words.e: Everyone cheered and applauded Manny’s efforts.
words.zh: (動) 鼓掌；喝彩　(動) 讚同；贊成

words.d: To appoint someone to a job means to give the job to them.
words.e: Two students were appointed to help the scientists with their research.
words.zh: (動) 任命；委派　(動) 約定；安排

words.d: When things are compatible, they work well or exist together successfully.
words.e: Jan and Fred are too different. They will never be compatible.
words.zh: (形) 相容的；兼容的　(形) 能並存的；相合的

words.d: Competence is the ability to do something well or effectively.
words.e: The job was easy because the group had enough competence to do it well.
words.zh: (名) 能力；勝任　(名) 專業知識；技能

words.d: To confer with someone means to discuss something with them to make a decision.
words.e: I will have to confer with my wife before I can purchase a new car.
words.zh: (動) 商談；協商　(動) 授予；頒發

words.d: When things are consecutive, they happen one after another without interruption.
words.e: The king ruled for ten consecutive years.
words.zh: (形) 連續的；相鄰的　(名) 連續發生的事物

words.d: When something is crude, it is not exact or detailed, but it can still be useful.
words.e: She drew crude hearts on the ground to show how much she loved him.
words.zh: (形) 粗糙的；未加工的　(形) 粗魯的；無禮的

words.d: A cube is a solid object with six square surfaces that are all the same size.
words.e: Please get me some ice cubes to put in my soda.
words.zh: (名) 立方體；正方形　(動) 切成立方體；立方

words.d: Feedback is comments to a person about how they are doing something.
words.e: I asked my boss for feedback on my work.
words.zh: (名) 反饋；意見　(動) 提供反饋；回應

words.d: Ignorance of something is lack of knowledge about it.
words.e: When he failed the test, his ignorance of math was obvious.
words.zh: (名) 無知；缺乏知識　(名) 不了解；不認識

words.d: When something is masculine, it is a quality or thing related to men.
words.e: American football is usually considered a masculine sport.
words.zh: (形) 男性的；陽剛的　(形) 男人的特徵

words.d: A monument is a structure that is built to remind people of a person or event.
words.e: A large monument was built to honor the brave soldiers.
words.zh: (名) 紀念碑；紀念建築　(名) 歷史遺跡；重要證據

words.d: When someone is muscular, they are very fit and strong.
words.e: He exercised regularly so that his body could become muscular.
words.zh: (形) 肌肉發達的；強健的

words.d: A person’s posture is the position in which they stand or sit.
words.e: Your back will feel better if you improve your posture.
words.zh: (名) 姿勢；姿態

words.d: To situate something means to place or build it in a certain place.
words.e: The road was situated between the forest and the lake.
words.zh: (動) 使位於；使坐落於

words.d: To supervise something means to make sure that it is done correctly.
words.e: Allen supervised the construction workers to ensure everyone’s safety.
words.zh: (動) 監督；監考

words.d: Symmetry is the state of having two halves that are exactly the same.
words.e: The artist made sure to use perfect symmetry when painting the butterfly.
words.zh: (名) 對稱；勻稱

words.d: A tattoo is a design that is drawn permanently on the skin with needles.
words.e: The surfer had tattoos on both his arms.
words.zh: (名) 刺青；紋身　(動) 刺青於；紋身於

words.d: An undergraduate is a student at a college who is studying fora bachelor’s degree.
words.e: She was excited to finish high school and enroll as an undergraduate in the fall.
words.zh: (名) 大學生

words.d: A brook is a small stream.
words.e: Water flows down several brooks on the mountain.
words.zh: (名) 小溪；溪流　(動) 容忍；忍受

words.d: To cater to someone means to provide them with all the things needed or wanted.
words.e: Bill was too sick to get out of bed, so his nurse catered to his needs.
words.zh: (動) 提供飲食；滿足需求

words.d: When someone is considerate, they pay attention to the needs of others.
words.e: The considerate boy gave a present to his girlfriend on Valentine’s Day.
words.zh: (形) 體貼的；周到的　(動) 考慮；顧及

words.d: The consumption of food or drink is the act of eating or drinking it.
words.e: These apples are too rotten for consumption.
words.zh: (名) 消費；消耗　(名) 肺結核

words.d: Criteria are factors on which a person judges or decides something.
words.e: Before she got the job, she had to meet all the necessary criteria.
words.zh: (名) 標準；準則

words.d: Crust is the tough outer part of a loaf of bread.
words.e: The little boy never ate the crust of his pizza.
words.zh: (名) 地殼；硬皮　(名) 麵包皮；外層

words.d: To degrade someone means to cause people to have shame.
words.e: The teacher degraded Bob when she announced his poor performance to the class
words.zh: (動) 貶低；降低　(動) 使變壞；惡化

words.d: To entitle someone means to give them the rights to have or do something.
words.e: His golden ticket entitled him to sit in the front row at the concert.
words.zh: (動) 給予權利；使有資格　使享有

words.d: To escort people means to safely accompany them to a place.
words.e: Her bodyguards escorted her to the movie theater.
words.zh: (動) [ɪˈskɔrt] 護送；陪同　(名) [ˈɛskɔrt] 護衛者；同伴

words.d: When something is external, it is connected to an outer part.
words.e: It is warm inside my house, but the external temperature is freezing.
words.zh: (形) 外部的；外面的　(形) 外在的；表面的

words.d: A facility is a building that exists for a particular purpose.
words.e: There are many educational facilities in big cities.
words.zh: (名) 設施；便利　(名) 才能；技能

words.d: A faculty is a mental or physical ability.
words.e: The boy’s mental faculties impressed all of his teachers.
words.zh: (名) 能力；才能　(名) 教職員；系所

words.d: A heap of things is a large pile of them.
words.e: After the building was torn down, all that was left was a heap of bricks.
words.zh: (名) 堆；大量　(動) 堆積；積累

words.d: A hemisphere is one half of the earth.
words.e: In the northern hemisphere, the weather is usually warmest in July and August.
words.zh: (名) 半球；地球的一半

words.d: A hound is a type of dog that is often used for racing or hunting.
words.e: The men took their hounds with them when they went on the hunting trip.
words.zh: (名) 獵犬；狗　(動) 窮追；逼迫

words.d: If something is impersonal, it is not friendly and makes people feel unimportant.
words.e: The boy felt scared on his first day at the big, impersonal high school.
words.zh: (形) 不個人的；冷淡的　(形) 客觀的；沒有個人感情的

words.d: An ornament is an attractive object that people display in their homes.
words.e: The woman kept some colorful ornaments on the shelves.
words.zh: (名) 裝飾品；飾物

words.d: A pedestrian is a person who is walking on a street.
words.e: Cars should be careful when pedestrians are walking around.
words.zh: (名) 行人；步行者　(形) 行人的；徒步的

words.d: A sanctuary is a place where people in danger can go to be safe.
words.e: The church was made into a sanctuary for homeless people in the winter.
words.zh: (名) 庇護所；聖所；保護區

words.d: A spectator is someone who watches something, especially a sports event.
words.e: There were thousands of spectators at the big game.
words.zh: (名) 觀眾；旁觀者

words.d: An asset is a skill or quality that is useful or valuable.
words.e: The coach realized the boy’s speed was an asset to the team.
words.zh: (名) 資產；有利條件　(名) 優勢；長處

words.d: An aspect is one part or feature of something.
words.e: I thought about the different aspects of owning two dogs.
words.zh: (名) 方面；層面　(名) 外觀；樣子

words.d: Braille is a system of raised patterns on paper that allows the blind to read.
words.e: The boy enjoyed reading his favorite books written in Braille.
words.zh: 點字是一種紙上凸起的圖案系統，讓盲人可以閱讀。

words.d: A bud is a part of a plant that turns into a flower or a leaf.
words.e: Two weeks after planting the seed, a small bud appeared.
words.zh: (名) 花蕾；芽　(動) 發芽；萌發

words.d: To coordinate things is to make different parts work together.
words.e: Each skating team had to coordinate their movements for the show.
words.zh: (動) 協調；調配　(名) 坐標；座標

words.d: To disprove something means to show that it is not true.
words.e: The scientist disproved the theory that the sun moved around the Earth.
words.zh: (動) 證明...是錯誤的；駁斥

words.d: If something is humanitarian, it is connected to helping people’s lives.
words.e: After the flood, several humanitarian organizations offered help.
words.zh: (形) 人道主義的；人文的　(名) 人道主義者；慈善家

words.d: A hypothesis is an idea for something that has not been proved yet.
words.e: The teacher did an experiment to prove whether his hypothesis was right.
words.zh: (名) 假說；假設　(名) 推測；猜想

words.d: An imprint is an effect or lesson from an experience that is hard to forget.
words.e: The experience ofwarleftan imprint on his mind that troubled him.
words.zh: (名) [ˈɪmˌprɪnt] 印記；痕跡　(動) [ɪmˈprɪnt] 留下印象；印刷

words.d: When something is informative, it provides a lot of information.
words.e: The travel guide had a lot of informative facts about the region.
words.zh: (形) 提供資訊的；有教育意義的；富有啟發性的

words.d: When something is optic, it relates to the eyes or light.
words.e: Her blindness was caused by a problem with her optic nerve.
words.zh: (形) 眼睛的；視覺的　(名) 光學

words.d: A premise is an idea on which something is based.
words.e: The premise of the movie that Bobbi and I watched was unrealistic.
words.zh: (名) 前提；假定

words.d: A rack is an object with shelves that holds things.
words.e: He stored his tools on a rack.
words.zh: (動) 折磨；使痛苦；(名) 架子；擱架

words.d: The Renaissance was a period between the 14th and 17th centuries.
words.e: Leonardo Da Vinci was a popular artist of the Renaissance.
words.zh: (名) 文藝復興

words.d: To revere something is to admire it greatly.
words.e: The students revere their teacher, who has taught them a lot.
words.zh: (動) 尊敬；崇敬

words.d: When something is simultaneous, it occurs at the same time as something else.
words.e: The movement of the gears inside the watch was simultaneous.
words.zh: (形) 同時的；同步的

words.d: A skeptic is a person who does not believe something.
words.e: The scientist showed the skeptic that dinosaurs did exist by providing evidence.
words.zh: 懷疑論者是指不相信某事物的人。

words.d: When something is spatial, it relates to the position and size of things.
words.e: He was asked where the books were located to test his spatial ability.
words.zh: (形) 空間的

words.d: To specify is to describe something clearly.
words.e: The poster didn’t specify where the concert was taking place.
words.zh: (動) 指定；具體說明

words.d: Wax is a substance that is slightly shiny and melts when heated.
words.e: The candles are made of wax.
words.zh: (名) 蠟　(動) 打蠟；上蠟

words.d: An accessory is a thing that is added to another thing to make it look better.
words.e: The store sold colorful accessories like bags, sunglasses, and makeup.
words.zh: (名) 配件；附屬品　(形) 附加的；輔助的

words.d: An acquisition is something that a person buys or gets in some way.
words.e: Marty was happy with his new acquisition: a very fast bicycle.
words.zh: (名) 獲得；習得　(名) 所得物；收購品

words.d: When something is adequate, it is good enough for something else.
words.e: Without adequate notice of the road block, they will have to turn around.
words.zh: (形) 足夠的；充分的　(形) 令人滿意的；適當的

words.d: Cardboard is a material made out of stiff paper. It is often used to make boxes.
words.e: We packed our things into cardboard boxes and moved to our new home.
words.zh: (名) 硬紙板；紙板　(形) 硬紙板製的；平板的

words.d: A dilemma is a difficult situation in which a choice has to be made.
words.e: Choosing either the tastier or healthier drink proved to be quite a dilemma.
words.zh: (名) 進退兩難的局面；困境　(名) 兩難的選擇；難題

words.d: When something is elaborate, it contains a lot of details.
words.e: She gave the teacher an elaborate explanation of her project.
words.zh: (形) 詳細的；精心製作的　(動) 詳細說明；精心設計

words.d: To facilitate something is to make it easier.
words.e: To facilitate the meeting, Melissa used a simple computer program.
words.zh: (動) 促進；便利　(動) 協助進行；提供便利條件

words.d: A fleet is a group of ships.
words.e: The fleet of ships spent a few days at the dock.
words.zh: (名) 艦隊；船隊　(形) 快速的；敏捷的　(動) 快速移動；掠過

words.d: A grid is a pattern of squares with numbers and letters to find places on a map.
words.e: We located our town using the grid.
words.zh: 網格是由數字與字母組成的方格圖案，用於在地圖上尋找位置。

words.d: To import means to bring in a product from another country.
words.e: Foods that have been imported are usually more expensive.
words.zh: (動) [ɪm`pɔrt] 進口；輸入　(名) [`ɪmpɔrt] 進口商品；重要性

words.d: To infer something is to decide it is true based on other information one has.
words.e: By the position of the sun in the sky, she inferred that it was noon.
words.zh: (動) 推論；推斷　(動) 暗示；意味著

words.d: To inflate something means to fill it up with air.
words.e: I helped him inflate the balloons.
words.zh: (動) 充氣；膨脹　(動) 誇大；抬高

words.d: When something is innate, it is something that one is born with, it is not learned.
words.e: He had the innate desire to please his teachers.
words.zh: 當某事物是天生的，代表它與生俱來，並非後天學習。

words.d: Marble is a type of rock that feels cold and is smooth when cut.
words.e: The large house had floors made of marble.
words.zh: (名) 大理石；彈珠　(形) 大理石的；冷漠的

words.d: A mast is a long pole on a ship that holds the sail.
words.e: The mast held both sails of the ship upright.
words.zh: 桅杆是船上用來支撐船帆的長桿。

words.d: Nausea is the feeling of being sick to your stomach.
words.e: The doctor said the medicine would help get rid of her nausea.
words.zh: (名) 噁心；想吐　(動) 使噁心；使厭煩

words.d: When something is naval, it relates to a country’s navy or military ships.
words.e: The country sent all of its naval forces to protect them.
words.zh: (形) 海軍的；軍艦的

words.d: A pouch is a small, flexible bag that is usually made of cloth.
words.e: I keep my money in a small pouch.
words.zh: (名) 小袋；錢包　(動) 把…放入袋中

words.d: If something is saturated, it is completely wet.
words.e: Leigh’s hair became saturated in the rain storm.
words.zh: (形) 飽和的；濕透的

words.d: An update is an act of making something more modern.
words.e: I’m doing an update on my personal phone directory.
words.zh: (動) [ʌp'det] 更新；使現代化　(名) ['ʌp.det] 更新；最新消息

words.d: An addict is a person who cannot stop doing or having something.
words.e: She was a coffee addict. She had more than three cups each day.
words.zh: (名) [ˈædɪkt] 成癮者；上癮的人　(動) [əˈdɪkt] 使成癮；沉溺於　(形) 成癮的；上癮的

words.d: When something is archeological, it relates to archeology.
words.e: They found archeological evidence that proved an ancient species of man.
words.zh: 當某事物與考古學相關，即指與考古學有關。

words.d: Archeology is the study of ancient people through their artifacts.
words.e: He studied archeology to learn more about ancient Egyptian culture.
words.zh: (名) 考古學；考古

words.d: To brainstorm is to have a lot of ideas about a certain topic.
words.e: The students met after school to brainstorm ideas for their assignment.
words.zh: (名) 腦力激盪；集體討論　(動) 進行腦力激盪；集思廣益

words.d: A budget is the amount of money available to spend on something.
words.e: His budget for food was very tight.
words.zh: (名) 預算；預算案　(動) 編列預算；規劃開支

words.d: When something is chaotic, it is crazy, confused, and hectic.
words.e: The first day of school can be chaotic for a new student.
words.zh: 當某事物混亂時，代表它瘋狂、困惑且忙亂。

words.d: To cite something is to mention it as an example or as proof of something.
words.e: She cited six reasons that the school needed to build new classrooms.
words.zh: (動) 引用；列舉　(動) 提及；引證

words.d: To correspond is to match or to be similar to something.
words.e: The boy’s story didn’t correspond with his mother’s version.
words.zh: (動) 符合；相應　(動) 通信；交流

words.d: A courtyard is an outdoor area that is surrounded by the walls of a building.
words.e: During the summer, the courtyard is a nice place to have lunch.
words.zh: (名) 院子；庭院　(名) 校園內的空地

words.d: An estate is a large area of land owned by a family or organization.
words.e: He lived on his father’s estate in the country.
words.zh: (名) 莊園；房產　(名) 遺產；財產

words.d: Fraud is the crime of gaining money by lying or by tricking people.
words.e: Experts say that credit card fraud increases around the holidays.
words.zh: (名) 詐騙；欺詐　(動) 詐騙；騙取

words.d: Hydrogen is a gas that has no taste, color, or smell.
words.e: Balloons filled with hydrogen can easily float away if you aren’t careful.
words.zh: (名) 氫；氫氣

words.d: Integrity is honesty and good morals.
words.e: The principal had a lot of integrity.
words.zh: (名) 正直；誠實　(名) 完整性；整體

words.d: To knit is to make fabric by connecting strings together.
words.e: My grandmother knitted me a sweater.
words.zh: (動) 編織；針織　(名) 針織品；毛衣

words.d: An outlook is a person’s opinion or way of thinking about something.
words.e: He changed his outlook about rats after he read a book about them.
words.zh: (名) [ˈaʊtˌlʊk] 觀點；展望　(動) [aʊtˈlʊk] 比...好看

words.d: A parachute is a device that helps people and things fall to the ground safely.
words.e: They used parachutes dropped from airplanes to send supplies to the civilians.
words.zh: (名) ['pɛrəˌʃut] 降落傘　(動) [ˌpɛrə'ʃut] 跳傘；空投

words.d: When something is prehistoric, it is from a time when there was no written history.
words.e: The scientists found prehistoric pots used by the people in the area.
words.zh: (形) 史前的

words.d: A proponent is a person who supports an idea or a plan.
words.e: He was a proponent of using environmentally friendly products.
words.zh: (名) 支持者；提倡者

words.d: To refine something is to make it better by making changes.
words.e: The principal refined his method of controlling students over the years.
words.zh: (動) 精煉；改進

words.d: To restrict is to limit something and prevent it from getting bigger.
words.e: The club restricted the amount of members.
words.zh: (動) 限制；約束

words.d: An attorney is one who gives others advice about the law.
words.e: The attorney appeared in front of the judge for me.
words.zh: (名) 律師；檢察官

words.d: When something is chronic, it happens over and over again over time.
words.e: He had chronic pain in his chest and needed to see a doctor.
words.zh: (形) 慢性的；長期的　(形) 持續的；根深蒂固的

words.d: Discipline is training that helps people follow the rules.
words.e: One of the teacher’s jobs is to teach her students discipline.
words.zh: (名) 紀律；訓練　(動) 懲罰；訓練

words.d: A donor is somebody who gives something to an organization.
words.e: He was proud to be a blood donor.
words.zh: (名) 捐贈者；施予者　(名) 器官捐贈者

words.d: A fellow is someone who shares a job or quality with someone else.
words.e: All of my fellow patients at the hospital have also complained about the food.
words.zh: (名) 同伴；夥伴　(形) 同類的；相同的

words.d: Gossip is information that might be untrue but is still discussed anyway.
words.e: The friends exchanged gossip about the people they knew in school.
words.zh: (名) 閒言碎語；八卦　(動) 說閒話；八卦

words.d: To graduate from a school means to complete and pass all courses of study there.
words.e: At the end of the spring, my friends and I will graduate from high school.
words.zh: (動) [ˈgrædʒuˌet] 畢業；完成學業　(名) [ˈgrædʒuɪt] 畢業生；大學畢業生

words.d: Graffiti is words or drawings in public places.
words.e: The wall was covered with colorful graffiti.
words.zh: (名) 塗鴉；壁畫

words.d: A guardian is someone who protects somebody or something.
words.e: The librarians are the guardians of the books.
words.zh: (名) 監護人；保護者　(名) 守護神；保衛者

words.d: To implicate someone is to show that they have done a crime or something bad.
words.e: The man was implicated in the theft at the store.
words.zh: 牽連某人是指顯示他們犯了罪或做了壞事。

words.d: Kin is a person’s family and relatives.
words.e: His kin were all farmers.
words.zh: (名) 親戚；親屬　(名) 同類的人或物

words.d: A referee is a person who makes sure that the rules are followed in sports.
words.e: The soccer player didn’t agree with the referee.
words.zh: (名) 裁判；(動) 擔任裁判

words.d: To sever something is to cut through it completely.
words.e: He severed the string using scissors.
words.zh: (動) 切斷；中斷

words.d: A shaft is a handle of a tool or weapon.
words.e: The golf club had a long wooden shaft that he held in his hands.
words.zh: (名) 軸；柄

words.d: To stab means to cut someone or something with a sharp object like a knife.
words.e: He stabbed the fork into the potato and passed it to his daughter.
words.zh: 刺傷是指用刀等尖銳物體切割某人或某物。

words.d: A stimulus is something that causes growth or activity.
words.e: Having a lot of money is a stimulus for people to buy more things.
words.zh: (名) 刺激；刺激物

words.d: A suspicion is a feeling that something is possible or true in a crime.
words.e: The police had a suspicion that the driver had stolen the purse.
words.zh: (名) 懷疑；嫌疑

words.d: To terminate something means to stop or end it.
words.e: The trip was terminated after the car broke down.
words.zh: 終止某事即停止或結束它。

words.d: A theme is the main subject of a book, movie, or painting.
words.e: The students discussed the book’s theme in class.
words.zh: (名) 主題；題目

words.d: Tuition is the amount of money paid to go to a school.
words.e: University tuitions have increased by fifty percent in the last five years.
words.zh: (名) 學費

words.d: If someone is aggressive, then they constantly want to fight.
words.e: Nobody liked to play games with him because he was always too aggressive.
words.zh: (形) 好鬥的；富有進攻性的　(形) 積極進取的；主動的

words.d: Amnesty is a pardon given to prisoners of war.
words.e: She was denied amnesty for her involvement in the war.
words.zh: (名) 赦免；特赦　(動) 赦免；特赦

words.d: An arena is a building where people can watch sports and concerts.
words.e: The new arena was all set to hold the championship match.
words.zh: (名) 競技場；舞台　(名) 領域；範疇

words.d: An auditorium is a large building used for public events.
words.e: People have gathered at the school auditorium to watch the play.
words.zh: (名) 禮堂；演講廳　(名) 觀眾席；講堂

words.d: A captive is a prisoner.
words.e: The guards told the captive that there was no way he could escape the prison.
words.zh: (形) 被囚禁的；被俘虜的　(名) 俘虜；囚犯

words.d: Combat is fighting between two people or groups.
words.e: The two warriors were locked in combat.
words.zh: (動) [kəmˈbæt] 對抗；戰鬥　(名) [ˈkɑmbæt] 戰鬥；格鬥

words.d: If something is commonplace, then it is ordinary.
words.e: There is nothing commonplace about the way Morris dresses.
words.zh: (形) 普通的；平凡的　(名) 平凡的事；老生常談

words.d: A compound is an enclosed area such as a prison or factory.
words.e: The workers waited outside the compound for the gates to the factory to open.
words.zh: (名) [ˈkɑmpaʊnd] 化合物；混合物　(形) [ˈkɑmpaʊnd] 複合的；混合的　(動) [kəmˈpaʊnd] 加重；惡化

words.d: A corps is a division of a military force.
words.e: The army had a corps of archers who trained apart from the regular soldiers.
words.zh: (名) 軍團；兵團　(名) 團體；集團

words.d: To distract someone means to stop them from concentrating on something.
words.e: The phone call distracted him so much that he forgot all about his homework.
words.zh: (動) 分散注意力；打擾　(動) 使心煩意亂；使困擾

words.d: If someone is dumb, they are unable to speak.
words.e: She did not share the secret. She remained silent as if she were dumb.
words.zh: (形) 啞的；不能說話　(形) 愚蠢的；傻的

words.d: A foe is an enemy or opponent.
words.e: It was hard to believe that anyone could be his foe.
words.zh: (名) 敵人；對手　(名) 反對者；仇敵

words.d: To hack something means to cut it into uneven pieces.
words.e: My uncle used the ax to hack the tree into many logs.
words.zh: (動) 砍；劈　(名) 黑客；駭客

words.d: To meditate means to focus or think deeply in silence.
words.e: She liked to meditate for several hours of each day.
words.zh: (動) 冥想；沉思　(動) 深思熟慮；思考

words.d: To nick someone means to cut them slightly with a sharp object.
words.e: While cutting the carrots, the cook nicked his finger with the edge of his knife.
words.zh: (名) 小傷口；刻痕　(動) 割傷；刻下

words.d: To provoke someone means to annoy them on purpose to cause violence.
words.e: The older boy provoked Paul by calling him mean names.
words.zh: (動) 激怒；引起

words.d: A realm is any area of activity or interest.
words.e: He was not very active in the realm of business.
words.zh: 領域是指任何活動或興趣的範圍。

words.d: A reign is the period of time in which a ruler rules.
words.e: The emperor’s reign lasted for only two years.
words.zh: (名) 統治時期；王朝

words.d: Rust is a red and brown coating on iron objects caused by water and air.
words.e: The old metal gate would not swing because the hinges were covered in rust.
words.zh: (動) 生鏽　(名) 鏽；鐵鏽

words.d: If something is sacred, then it is worshipped and respected.
words.e: One religion in India will not harm cows because it believes that they are sacred.
words.zh: (形) 神聖的；莊嚴的

words.d: If someone acts accordingly, they act in a way that is suitable.
words.e: He feels like he did a good job, and his boss should pay him accordingly.
words.zh: (副) 因此；照著辦

words.d: An anchor is a heavy object dropped from a boat to make it stay in one place.
words.e: When the ship reached its destination, the crew dropped the anchor.
words.zh: (名) 錨；支柱　(動) 拋錨；固定　(形) 主要的；關鍵的

words.d: A buoy is a floating sign that warns boats of dangerous areas.
words.e: Don’t steer the boat near those buoys. There are rocks underneath the water.
words.zh: (名) 浮標；浮筒　(動) 使浮起；支撑

words.d: A catastrophe is an unexpected event that causes great suffering or damage.
words.e: It was a catastrophe for my family when my dad lost his job.
words.zh: (名) 大災難；災難　(名) 慘敗；失敗

words.d: Context is the situations that form the background of an event.
words.e: They studied the context of the battle before giving their presentation.
words.zh: (名) 上下文；背景　(名) 環境；情況

words.d: To designate someone or something means to give them a particular description.
words.e: The famous lighthouse was designated a historical monument.
words.zh: (動) 指定；任命　(動) 標明；表示

words.d: To distort something means to lie about it.
words.e: His lawyer distorted the facts so that he would be set free.
words.zh: (動) 扭曲；歪曲　(動) 曲解；篡改

words.d: A dock is an enclosed area where ships go to be loaded, unloaded, and repaired.
words.e: The huge ship pulled into the dock, and the crew unloaded the cargo.
words.zh: (名) 碼頭；船塢　(動) 靠岸；停靠　(動) 扣除；減少

words.d: The fore of something is the front part of it.
words.e: The teacher’s desk is at the fore of the classroom.
words.zh: 某物的前部，即其前端部分。

words.d: If something is frequent, then it happens or is done often.
words.e: While Dad was sick, the doctor made frequent visits to his house.
words.zh: (形) [ˈfrikwənt] 頻繁的；經常的　(動) [friˈkwɛnt] 經常造訪；常去

words.d: When something is genuine, it is true or real.
words.e: After the painting was determined to be genuine, it sold for a million dollars.
words.zh: (形) 真正的；真實的　(形) 誠實的；坦誠的

words.d: Grease is an oily substance put on moving parts, so they work smoothly.
words.e: When I was done working on the car, I had grease all over my hands.
words.zh: (名) [gris] 油脂；潤滑油　(動) [griz] 塗油；潤滑　(形) 油膩的；骯髒的

words.d: When something is intricate, it has many small parts or details.
words.e: The intricate painting on the quilt was very lovely.
words.zh: (形) 複雜的；精細的　(形) 難以理解的；曲折的

words.d: To offset means to use one thing to cancel out the effect of another thing.
words.e: Increased wages are offset by higher prices for goods.
words.zh: (名) [ˋɔfˏsɛt] 抵消；補償　(動) [ˋɔfˋsɛt] 抵消；彌補

words.d: To overlap something means to cover a piece of it.
words.e: The gift on top overlaps the other gift on the bottom.
words.zh: (動) [ˌovɚ'læp] 重疊；部分重複　(名) ['ovɚˌlæp] 重疊部分

words.d: To precipitate an event means to cause it to happen sooner than normal.
words.e: The violent attack precipitated an all-out war.
words.zh: (動) [prɪ'sɪpəˌtet] 促成；使突然發生　(名) [prɪ'sɪpətɪt] 沉澱物

words.d: When something is secondhand, it has been owned by someone else.
words.e: Her secondhand jeans were a bit faded in the front.
words.zh: (形) 二手的；間接的　(副) 二手地；間接地

words.d: A slot is a narrow opening in a machine or container.
words.e: To operate the machine, put your coins into the slot.
words.zh: (名) 時段；位置

words.d: To submerge something means to put it below the surface of a liquid.
words.e: The whale submerged its huge body into the ocean.
words.zh: (動) 淹沒；浸沒

words.d: A tactic is a careful plan to achieve something.
words.e: Sam thought of a good tactic in order to attract more business.
words.zh: (名) 策略；戰術

words.d: When a number is aggregate, it is made up of smaller amounts added together.
words.e: The company totaled its aggregate sales for the entire year.
words.zh: (名) [ˈægrəgɪt] 總和；合計　(動) [ˈægrəˌget] 合併；聚集　(形) 合成的；集合的

words.d: An antibiotic is a medical drug used to kill bacteria and treat infections.
words.e: The doctor gave me a shot of an antibiotic when I got the flu.
words.zh: (名) 抗生素　(形) 抗生素的；殺菌的

words.d: A circuit is a piece of an electronic device that allows electricity to flow.
words.e: Be very careful not to shock yourself when fixing an electrical circuit.
words.zh: (名) 電路；迴路　(名) 巡迴；路線

words.d: To complement something or someone is to make them better.
words.e: The wool scarf complemented her lovely eyes.
words.zh: (名) 補足物；搭配物　(動) 補充；襯托

words.d: To compress something means to press or squeeze it so that it takes up less space.
words.e: I compressed my clothes to fit into a single suitcase.
words.zh: (動) [kəmˋprɛs] 壓縮；壓扁　(名) [ˋkɑmprɛs] 冷敷布；藥布

words.d: A database is a collection of data that is stored in a computer.
words.e: The company has a database of all the names and accounts of their customers.
words.zh: (名) 資料庫；數據庫

words.d: An equivalent is an amount or value that is the same as another amount or value.
words.e: I worked the equivalent of sixty hours this week.
words.zh: (形) 相等的；等量的　(名) 相等物；等價物

words.d: When someone is immune to a disease, they cannot be affected by it.
words.e: Children usually get shots to make them immune to certain diseases.
words.zh: (形) 免疫的；不受影響的　(動) 使免除；豁免

words.d: Input is information that is put into a computer.
words.e: Type the input into the computer program.
words.zh: (名) 輸入；投入　(動) 輸入；輸進

words.d: When a relationship is intimate, the two things are very closely connected.
words.e: I only tell my secrets to my most intimate friends.
words.zh: (形) [ˋɪntəmɪt] 親密的；密切的　(動) [ˋɪntəmet] 暗示；透露

words.d: A magnet is a piece of iron or other material which attracts iron toward it.
words.e: I used a magnet to pick up the nails that were scattered on the floor.
words.zh: (名) 磁鐵；磁石　(名) 吸引力強的人或物

words.d: A person’s metabolism is the way chemical processes in their body use energy.
words.e: If you exercise every day, your metabolism speeds up.
words.zh: (名) 新陳代謝；代謝作用

words.d: A microchip is a small device inside a computer that holds information.
words.e: I can put more data on my computer if I buy a more powerful microchip.
words.zh: (名) 微晶片；晶片

words.d: A phase is a stage in a process or the gradual development of something.
words.e: The first phase in the recycling project involves finding volunteers to help out.
words.zh: (名) 階段；時期　(動) 逐步實行

words.d: To pinch means to take a piece of skin between one’s fingers and squeeze.
words.e: I pinched my nose, so I couldn’t smell the odor from the garbage.
words.zh: (名) 捏；掐　(動) 捏；掐

words.d: When something is prevalent, it is common.
words.e: Growing a beard is more prevalent behavior in men than women.
words.zh: (形) 普遍的；流行的

words.d: When something is quantum, it relates to the behavior of atomic particles.
words.e: The physics student studied quantum mechanics.
words.zh: (名) 量子；(形) 量子的

words.d: A ratio is a relationship between two things expressed in numbers or amounts.
words.e: The boy to girl ratio is one to three.
words.zh: (名) 比率；比例

words.d: A spiral is a shape which winds round and round in a larger and larger circle.
words.e: A strand of DNA looks like two interlocking spirals.
words.zh: (動) 盤旋；螺旋式上升/下降　(名) 螺旋；螺旋形

words.d: When something is viral, it is a disease or infection that is caused by a virus.
words.e: The girl was in bed fora week when she had a viral infection.
words.zh: (形) 病毒式的；廣泛傳播的；(名) 病毒式內容

words.d: If you are astounded, you are very surprised.
words.e: I was astounded that Monica won the art competition.
words.zh: 如果你感到震驚，表示你非常驚訝。

words.d: An attribute is a characteristic of a person or thing.
words.e: He isn’t very clever, but he does have some other positive attributes.
words.zh: (名) ['ætrəˌbjut] 特徵；屬性　(動) [ə'trɪbˌjut] 把...歸因於；認為...是由於

words.d: If someone is bilingual, then they can speak two languages.
words.e: Since you already know English, after learning French you’ll be bilingual.
words.zh: 如果某人會雙語，則他們能說兩種語言。

words.d: A clone is an identical copy of a living creature.
words.e: Scientists recently made a clone of a sheep.
words.zh: (名) 克隆；複製品　(動) 複製；克隆

words.d: Colloquial describes informal words that are more suitable for speech than writing.
words.e: I find it difficult to understand people if they use colloquial language.
words.zh: (形) 日常會話的；非正式的　(形) 口語的；通俗的

words.d: Cosmetics are substances that make the face and skin more beautiful.
words.e: Girls often look nicer when they don’t use so many cosmetics.
words.zh: (名) 化妝品

words.d: To dash means to run or move quickly.
words.e: Helen dashed up the stairs, so she wouldn’t be late for her appointment.
words.zh: (動) 衝刺；急跑　(名) 短跑；衝刺

words.d: Disgust is a feeling of distaste and anger caused by something rude or unpleasant.
words.e: He felt disgust toward his date because she had such terrible eating habits.
words.zh: (名) 厭惡；反感　(動) 使厭惡；使反感　(形) 感到厭惡的

words.d: If something is fluorescent, it is such a bright color that it seems to give off light.
words.e: She highlighted the key words in the document with a fluorescent yellow pen.
words.zh: (形) 螢光的；發螢光的　(形) 明亮的；容易引人注目的

words.d: If you are furious, you are extremely angry.
words.e: My father was furious when he read my bad school report.
words.zh: (形) 極其憤怒的；狂怒的　(形) 猛烈的；激烈的

words.d: A gulf is a gap between people who do not understand each other.
words.e: There has been a gulf between James and Tony since their parents died.
words.zh: (名) 海灣；灣　(名) 深淵；鴻溝

words.d: Humanities are subjects which analyze human ideas, such as history and literature.
words.e: Jennifer has always been more interested in humanities than science.
words.zh: 人文學科是分析人類思想的學科，如歷史與文學。

words.d: A knot is made when you tie the ends of rope or cord together.
words.e: He tied a knot in his shoelaces, so they wouldn’t come off during the race.
words.zh: (名) 結；打結　(動) 打結；系住　(形) 複雜的；難解的

words.d: A linguist is someone who studies languages.
words.e: Tony is a good linguist and speaks four different languages.
words.zh: (名) 語言學家；精通多種語言的人

words.d: A participant is someone who joins in a social event or competition.
words.e: There were thousands of participants in this year’s marathon.
words.zh: (名) 參與者；參加者

words.d: If something is plausible, it is reasonable or possible.
words.e: It is plausible that Jack isn’t here today because he is sick.
words.zh: (形) 看似真實的；合理的

words.d: A ritual is a formal custom that people do regularly.
words.e: Ken was very interested to learn about the religious rituals of the natives.
words.zh: (名) 儀式；例行公事　(形) 儀式的；例行公事的

words.d: A sibling is a brother or sister.
words.e: Jane has two siblings, an older brother and a younger sister.
words.zh: (名) 兄弟姊妹；手足

words.d: If someone is skinny, they are extremely thin.
words.e: Polly is very skinny. I think she needs to eat more.
words.zh: (形) 極瘦的；皮包骨的

words.d: If something is vague, it is not clear, and it gives very few details.
words.e: I asked him about his mother’s health, but he was very vague about it.
words.zh: (形) 模糊的；不明確的

words.d: An acid is a chemical that can burn or dissolve other substances.
words.e: In chemistry class, we mixed two acids together and watched the reaction.
words.zh: (名) 酸；酸性物質　(形) 酸的；尖刻的

words.d: An administration is the group of people who manage a company or organization.
words.e: She hoped she could be promoted to a job in the administration.
words.zh: (名) 管理；行政　(名) 政府部門；內閣

words.d: Administrative describes anything related to managing a company or organization.
words.e: I work as an administrative assistant to the owner of the company.
words.zh: (形) 行政的；管理的　(形) 與政府部門有關的

words.d: Biotechnology is the use of living parts, such as cells, in industry and technology.
words.e: Researchers at the biotechnology company use bacteria to make medicine.
words.zh: 生物科技是將細胞等活體部分應用於工業與技術的領域。

words.d: Cholesterol is a substance in fat, tissues, and blood of all animals.
words.e: When people have too much cholesterol, they are at a high risk for heart problems.
words.zh: (名) 膽固醇；一種脂肪物質

words.d: A coalition is a group of people or organizations working for a common purpose.
words.e: The companies formed a coalition to make trade less expensive.
words.zh: (名) 聯盟；聯合　(名) 結合；聯合體

words.d: When something is deceptive, it encourages one to believe something that is false.
words.e: The scary-looking man’s appearance is deceptive, but he is actually very nice.
words.zh: (形) 欺騙的；具有迷惑性的　(形) 外表騙人的；容易誤導的

words.d: Diabetes is a medical condition where a person has too much sugar in their blood.
words.e: Overweight people are more likely to suffer from diabetes than slimmer ones.
words.zh: (名) 糖尿病；糖尿病患者

words.d: To eliminate something that is unwanted means to completely remove it.
words.e: Wearing a seatbelt eliminates some of the dangers of driving a car.
words.zh: (動) 消除；排除　(動) 淘汰；刪除

words.d: Erosion is the destruction of rock or soil due to flowing water or weather.
words.e: Canyons are formed because rivers of fast-moving water caused erosion.
words.zh: (名) 侵蝕；腐蝕　(動) 侵蝕；逐漸破壞

words.d: Ethics are moral beliefs or rules about right or wrong.
words.e: The act of stealing certainly doesn’t go against some people’s ethics.
words.zh: (名) 倫理；道德　(名) 倫理學；道德標準

words.d: If something is explicit, it is very clear, open, and truthful.
words.e: The man gave a very explicit account of the car accident.
words.zh: (形) 明確的；清楚的　(形) 露骨的；直率的

words.d: A framework is a set of rules or ideas that people use to solve problems.
words.e: His ideas fit into the framework of a successful business plan.
words.zh: (名) 框架；結構　(名) 體系；架構

words.d: To manufacture something means to make it in a factory.
words.e: My father’s company manufactures steel building materials.
words.zh: (動) 製造；生產　(名) 製造商；廠商

words.d: A mechanism is a part of a machine that performs a certain function.
words.e: I can’t open my car door because the locking mechanism is broke.
words.zh: (名) 機制；機構　(名) 方法；途徑

words.d: To minimize means to reduce something to the lowest possible level.
words.e: I checked my homework twice to minimize errors I might have made.
words.zh: (動) 使最小化；減少　(動) 縮小；貶低

words.d: Nectar is a sweet liquid produced by flowers that bees and other insects collect.
words.e: Bees use nectar to make their honey.
words.zh: (名) 花蜜；甘露　(名) 神酒；美酒

words.d: A notion is an idea or belief about something.
words.e: I have a notion that this route would get us to the beach.
words.zh: (名) 觀念；想法　(名) 衝動；傾向

words.d: When things are prone to some bad thing, they are likely affected by it.
words.e: Some people are more prone to catching colds than others.
words.zh: (形) 易於……的；有……傾向的

words.d: When something is straightforward, it is good because it is easy to understand.
words.e: The teacher’s grading system was straightforward and fair.
words.zh: (形) 直接的；簡單的

words.d: If something is astronomical, then it is extremely large.
words.e: It’s an astronomical distance between the Milky Way galaxy and the nearest galaxy.
words.zh: (形) 天文的；極其巨大的　(形) 極高的；天文數字的

words.d: An atom is the smallest unit of a substance.
words.e: A molecule consists of a combination of two or more atoms.
words.zh: (名) 原子；微粒

words.d: Breadth is the distance from one side to the other side of something.
words.e: The breadth of the northern wall of the house is twenty meters.
words.zh: (名) 寬度；範圍　(名) 廣度；涵蓋面

words.d: A circumference is the distance completely around a circular object.
words.e: The circumference of the Earth is obviously much larger than a baseball’s.
words.zh: (名) 圓周；周長　(名) 周圍；邊界

words.d: A comet is an object in space made of ice and rock with a tail of glowing dust.
words.e: Comets take many decades to complete an orbit around a star.
words.zh: (名) 彗星；彗虹

words.d: A crater is a large hole in a planet’s or moon’s surface.
words.e: They could clearly see the big crater on the moon through the telescope.
words.zh: (名) 隕石坑；火山口　(名) 凹陷；坑洞

words.d: A crescent is the curved shape lit on the moon’s face during its early and late stages.
words.e: Ten days ago the entire moon was bright, but now only a small crescent is shining.
words.zh: (名) 新月；月牙　(形) 彎曲的；逐漸增加的

words.d: Debris is the small pieces scattered from something wrecked or destroyed.
words.e: The debris from the cube scattered on the floor.
words.zh: (名) 碎片；殘骸　(名) 廢墟；瓦礫

words.d: Despair is the complete loss of hope.
words.e: After the other company won the account, our salespeople were filled with despair.
words.zh: (名) 絕望；失望　(動) 失去希望；感到絕望

words.d: To embed something means to place it firmly within a surrounding thing.
words.e: The logger embedded the ax into the wood after chopping several logs.
words.zh: (動) 嵌入；埋入　(動) 使深深植入

words.d: A fragment is a small part of something.
words.e: After the light broke, there were fragments of glass to clean up.
words.zh: (名) [ˈfrægmənt] 碎片；片段　(動) [frægˈmɛnt] 分裂；破碎

words.d: A galaxy is any extremely large collection of star systems.
words.e: Our solar system is located in the outer area of our galaxy.
words.zh: (名) 銀河；星系

words.d: If something is gigantic, then it is extremely large.
words.e: Some dinosaurs were so gigantic that they were the size of buildings.
words.zh: (形) 巨大的；龐大的　(形) 非常大的；極其巨大的

words.d: Gloom is a state of being almost completely dark.
words.e: In the gloom of the morning, it was difficult to see the boat on the lake.
words.zh: (名) 黑暗；陰沉　(形) 陰沉的；沮喪的

words.d: To radiate means to send out energy or heat.
words.e: The heat from the fireplace radiated throughout the room.
words.zh: (動) 輻射；散發

words.d: To roam means to move around without a plan or purpose.
words.e: All day the cows roamed around the field eating grass.
words.zh: 漫遊是指沒有計劃或目的地四處走動。

words.d: If something is solitary, then it is lonely or the only one.
words.e: The only thing in the room was a solitary chair.
words.zh: (形) 孤獨的；獨自的

words.d: The spectrum is the full range of color ranging from red to violet.
words.e: You can see the entire spectrum in a rainbow.
words.zh: (名) 光譜；範圍；系列

words.d: A sphere is a three-dimensional round shape, like a ball.
words.e: The balloons were inflated into a variety of colorful spheres.
words.zh: (名) 範圍；領域；球體

words.d: Status is the position of something or someone in relation to others.
words.e: She had achieved the status of being the smartest girl in the class.
words.zh: (名) 地位；狀況

words.d: If someone is bankrupt, then they are unable to pay their debts.
words.e: The store had few customers and soon went bankrupt.
words.zh: (形) 破產的；無法償還債務的　(動) 使破產；導致經濟失敗

words.d: To conform to rules or laws is to obey them.
words.e: The new student had to conform to the school’s dress code.
words.zh: (動) 遵守；順應　符合；一致

words.d: To employ someone means to give work to them.
words.e: The bookstore employed two full-time clerks.
words.zh: (動) 僱用；雇用　(動) 利用；運用

words.d: To expel someone means to force them to leave a place.
words.e: Since he would not follow the rules, the principal had to expel the student.
words.zh: (動) 開除；驅逐　(動) 趕出；排出

words.d: An extension is a part added to something to give it more time or space.
words.e: My parents decided to add an extension to our house for the new baby.
words.zh: (名) 延伸；延長　(名) 延期；展期

words.d: If something is forthcoming, then it is about to happen in the future.
words.e: Some economists predicted that the forthcoming world economy would be severe.
words.zh: (形) 即將到來的；即將發生的　(形) 樂於提供的；坦誠的

words.d: To furnish means to put furniture in a house or room.
words.e: Most homes are furnished with tables, chairs, and beds.
words.zh: (動) 供應；提供　(動) 裝置；配置

words.d: Hygiene is the conditions or methods needed for health and cleanliness.
words.e: People who brush their teeth at least twice a day are practicing good hygiene.
words.zh: (名) 衛生；衛生習慣　(形) 衛生的；清潔的

words.d: If something is hygienic, then it is clean and unlikely to cause disease.
words.e: My sister works very hard to keep her entire home as hygienic as possible.
words.zh: (形) 衛生的；清潔的　(形) 健康的；符合衛生標準的

words.d: A landlord is a man who rents property to a person.
words.e: The landlord collected everyone’s rent money on the first day of every month.
words.zh: (名) 房東；地主　(名) 旅館老闆；酒館老闆

words.d: To lease means to rent property, usually an apartment or land.
words.e: When the family first leased the apartment, the rent was very low.
words.zh: (名) 租約；租賃　(動) 出租；租賃

words.d: If something is mandatory, then it is required by law.
words.e: It’s mandatory that everyone be at least sixteen to drive a car in the US.
words.zh: (形) 強制的；必須的　(形) 法定的；規定的

words.d: To mend something means to fix it when it is broken or damaged.
words.e: Mother mended the rip in my pants with a piece of cloth.
words.zh: (動) 修理；修補　(動) 改善；改正

words.d: A mortgage is a loan for property, especially homes and businesses.
words.e: When they bought their new home, the married couple had to sign a mortgage.
words.zh: (名) 抵押貸款；房貸　(動) 抵押；以房屋作為貸款擔保

words.d: Personnel are employees in a business.
words.e: When business increased, we had to hire more personnel.
words.zh: (名) 人員；人事部門

words.d: Plumbing is the system of pipes used in a home to supply water.
words.e: When the plumbing stopped working, no one was allowed to use the toilets.
words.zh: (名) 水管系統；管道工程

words.d: A tenant is a person who rents property from a landlord.
words.e: The new tenants moved into the house across the street.
words.zh: (名) 租戶；房客

words.d: If something is trendy, then it is very popular and new.
words.e: Carlo bought a trendy new car.
words.zh: (形) 時髦的；流行的

words.d: A utility is a business that supplies services such as water or electricity.
words.e: If you don’t pay the utilities, you may have your electricity turned off.
words.zh: (名) 實用性；公用事業

words.d: Whereby means by which or through which.
words.e: The mayor had a new bridge built whereby the citizens could cross the river.
words.zh: (副) 藉此；憑藉

words.d: If something is aesthetic, then it is concerned with a love of beauty.
words.e: The dresses were noteworthy for their aesthetic design.
words.zh: (形) 美學的；審美的　(名) 美感；審美觀點

words.d: If someone is arrogant, they think that they are more important than others.
words.e: He is very arrogant. Even though he’s not the boss, he tells everyone what to do.
words.zh: (形) 傲慢的；自大的　(形) 驕傲自滿的；目中無人的

words.d: A bias is a person’s likelihood to like one thing more than another thing.
words.e: The mothers had a natural bias for their own child’s picture.
words.zh: (名) 偏見；偏差　(動) 使有偏見；影響　(形) 偏頗的；有偏差的

words.d: A canyon is a narrow valley with steep walls through which a river often flows.
words.e: The canyon was so deep that the ground inside was covered in shadow.
words.zh: (名) 峽谷；險峽

words.d: A creek is a stream or small river.
words.e: Only small fish lived in the shallow waters of the creek.
words.zh: (名) 小溪；溪流

words.d: A drill is a tool with a point that spins in order to make a hole.
words.e: The carpenter used the drill to make several holes in the wood.
words.zh: (名) 鑽孔機；操練　(動) 鑽孔；進行操練　(動) 反覆練習；反覆教授

words.d: An executive is the top manager of a business.
words.e: After twenty years at the company, he finally became the executive.
words.zh: (名) 行政主管；執行官　(形) 行政的；執行的

words.d: Fatigue is a feeling of extreme tiredness.
words.e: After three days with little sleep, she was feeling a lot of fatigue.
words.zh: (名) 疲勞；疲倦　(動) 使疲倦；使厭倦　(形) 疲倦的；疲勞的

words.d: An incline is a sharp rise in something, especially a hill or mountain.
words.e: This mountain has one of the steepest inclines in the world.
words.zh: (動) [ɪnˈklaɪn] 傾斜；彎曲　(名) [ˈɪnklaɪn] 斜坡；坡度

words.d: If something is nasty, then it is not nice or pleasant.
words.e: The rotten apple left a nasty taste inside her mouth.
words.zh: (形) 令人厭惡的；討厭的　(形) 惡劣的；不愉快的

words.d: To perceive something means to be aware of it.
words.e: He was talking loudly, so he did not perceive that the music had stopped.
words.zh: (動) 察覺；理解；看待

words.d: A primate is a type of mammal that includes monkeys, apes, and humans.
words.e: Primates use their hands for such tasks as swinging from branches.
words.zh: 靈長類是包含猴子、猿類和人類在內的一類哺乳動物。

words.d: If something is primitive, then it is simple, basic, and not very developed.
words.e: The computers of the 1980s are primitive compared to those of todays.
words.zh: (形) 原始的；早期的

words.d: A stereotype is a general but often incorrect idea about a person or thing.
words.e: There’s a stereotype that pigs are dirty animals. But they are rather clean.
words.zh: (名) 刻板印象　(動) 對…形成刻板印象

words.d: If something is sticky, then it is covered with a substance that things stick to.
words.e: Place the sticky part of the tape against the paper, so it will cling to the wall.
words.zh: (形) 黏的；棘手的

words.d: A termite is an insect that lives in groups and feeds on wood.
words.e: The wood we found was full of termites.
words.zh: (名) 白蟻

words.d: If something happens thereby an action, then it is the result of that action.
words.e: He didn’t score a goal, thereby ending his chance at setting a record.
words.zh: (副) 因此；從而

words.d: A trail is a path through a wild area.
words.e: A narrow trail cut through the field and over the hills.
words.zh: (名) 痕跡；小徑　(動) 跟蹤；落後

words.d: A twig is a short and thin branch from a tree or bush.
words.e: They started the fire with a handful of dry twigs.
words.zh: (名) 小樹枝；細枝

words.d: Welfare is the health and happiness of a person or group.
words.e: Having plenty of clean water is necessary for the welfare of people.
words.zh: (名) 福利；福祉

words.d: If something is done on one’s behalf, it is done for that person by another.
words.e: The original speaker was sick, so his son gave the speech on his behalf.
words.zh: (介) 代表；為了　(名) 利益；好處

words.d: To flap means to move quickly up and down or from side to side.
words.e: The tiny bird flapped its wings and ate from the flowers.
words.zh: (名) 拍打；襟翼　(動) 拍動；揮動　(形) 鬆垂的；搖晃的

words.d: A glacier is a large piece of ice that moves very slowly.
words.e: The North Pole is covered by a huge glacier.
words.zh: (名) 冰川；冰河

words.d: The globe refers to the Earth.
words.e: Water covers most of the globe.
words.zh: (名) 地球；世界　(名) 地球儀；圓球

words.d: When something is horizontal, it is flat and level with the ground.
words.e: The Russian flag has three horizontal stripes of white, blue, and red.
words.zh: (形) 水平的；橫向的　(副) 水平地；橫向地

words.d: To hum means to make a low, continuous noise.
words.e: The man hummed his favorite song.
words.zh: (動) 哼唱；發出嗯聲　(名) 嗡嗡聲；低鳴聲

words.d: An inventory is a supply of something.
words.e: Gwen was checking the inventory to make sure we had what we needed.
words.zh: (名) 庫存；存貨　(名) 詳細清單；盤點

words.d: If a thought or feeling is inward, it is not expressed or shown to others.
words.e: She had an inward feeling of guilt when she lied to her mother.
words.zh: (形) 向內的；內部的　(副) 向內；朝裡面

words.d: A loaf of bread is bread shaped and baked in one piece.
words.e: Could you please buy a loaf of bread for sandwiches?
words.zh: (動) 懶散地閒蕩；虛度時光　(名) 一條麵包

words.d: An oracle is person who speaks with gods and gives advice about the future.
words.e: The king went to the oracle to ask if going to war was a good idea.
words.zh: (名) 神諭；預言　(名) 神殿；智者

words.d: To orbit something means to move around it in a continuous, curving path.
words.e: The moon orbits the Earth.
words.zh: (名) 軌道；軌跡　(動) 繞…運行；環繞

words.d: An overview is a general description of a situation.
words.e: My brother gave me an overview of the important parts of the book.
words.zh: (名) 概述；概觀

words.d: A preview is an opportunity to see something before it is available to the public.
words.e: The band played us a preview of their new song.
words.zh: (動) 預習；預覽

words.d: If something is previous, then it happened earlier in time or order.
words.e: He turned back to the previous page to read the paragraph again.
words.zh: (形) 先前的；以往的

words.d: To provide something means to supply it.
words.e: Each student was provided with a test and three sharp pencils.
words.zh: (動) 提供；供應

words.d: To recur means to happen more than once.
words.e: Burglaries seem to recur over and over in our neighborhood.
words.zh: (動) 再次發生；重現

words.d: When something is relevant, it is important to a certain person or situation.
words.e: The thirty-year-old book about politics is still relevant to our society today.
words.zh: (形) 相關的；切題的

words.d: A rite is a traditional ceremony carried out by a particular group or society.
words.e: Special masks are worn during the rite when a new baby is born.
words.zh: (名) 儀式；典禮

words.d: To stall means to stop a process and continue it at a later time.
words.e: If you give the car a push, it won’t stall.
words.zh: (動) 熄火；停滯　(名) 攤位；停頓

words.d: If something is supernatural, it is not real or explainable by natural law.
words.e: The dragon had supernatural powers such as flying and breathing fire.
words.zh: (形) 超自然的；神奇的

words.d: To adapt means to change in order to deal with a new situation or addition.
words.e: When he went to the new town, he had to adapt to all the weather changes.
words.zh: (動) 適應；改編　(動) 調整；改造

words.d: Biological describes the process of life and living things.
words.e: In science, we learned about the biological process of bacterial growth.
words.zh: (形) 生物的；生物學的　(形) 生理的；有機的

words.d: When something is cellular, it relates to the cells of animals or plants.
words.e: She used a microscope to see the activity at a cellular level.
words.zh: (形) 細胞的；蜂窩式網路的；行動通訊的

words.d: When people are dynamic, they are lively and have creative ideas.
words.e: The new, dynamic employee came up with a good way to juggle his work load.
words.zh: (形) 充滿活力的；有力的　(名) 動力；驅動力

words.d: A fantasy is a pleasant situation that people think about but is unlikely to happen.
words.e: Becoming an astronaut is a fantasy shared by many children.
words.zh: (名) 幻想；奇想　(形) 幻想的；想像的

words.d: Heredity is the process of passing on features from parents to children.
words.e: The boy’s face is similar to his father’s because of heredity.
words.zh: (名) 遺傳；遺傳性　(名) 遺傳特徵；遺傳因素

words.d: When something is internal, it exists or happens inside a person, object, or place.
words.e: We removed the outer case to reveal the computer’s internal wires.
words.zh: (形) 內部的；內在的　(形) 體內的；內臟的

words.d: When something is minimal, it is very small.
words.e: My lazy husband does a minimal amount of work around the house.
words.zh: (形) 最小的；極少的　(形) 最低的；基本的

words.d: A pioneer is a person who is the first to discover or be involved in something.
words.e: He was a pioneer of computer programming.
words.zh: (動) 開拓；倡導　(名) 先驅；開拓者

words.d: To prescribe medicine means to tell someone to take it.
words.e: When I was sick, the doctor prescribed me flu medicine.
words.zh: (動) 開（藥方）；規定

words.d: When things are respective, they relate separately to each person just mentioned.
words.e: The boxers were told to return to their respective corners.
words.zh: (形) 各自的；分別的

words.d: To revive someone or something means to restore health or life to them.
words.e: She revived the feeling of warmth in her leg by rubbing it softly.
words.zh: (動) 復甦；重新振作

words.d: When rules or systems are rigid, they are severe because they cannot be changed.
words.e: Societies often have rigid rules about the way that people are supposed to act.
words.zh: (形) 嚴格的；僵硬的

words.d: A sequence is a number of events or things that come one after another.
words.e: The dominos fell in a sequence of one after another.
words.zh: (名) 順序；連續　(動) 按順序排列

words.d: To substitute something or someone means to have them take the place of another.
words.e: When I ran out of juice, I had to substitute water to drink in the morning.
words.zh: (名) 代用品；替代者　(動) 替代；取代

words.d: A surgeon is a doctor who is trained to do surgery.
words.e: The surgeon operated on the old man’s heart.
words.zh: (名) 外科醫生

words.d: Therapy is treatment for a particular physical or mental illness or condition.
words.e: After she broke her legs, she used physical therapy to learn how to walk again.
words.zh: (名) 治療；療法

words.d: To transfer something means to move it from one place to another.
words.e: The family transferred the groceries from the shopping cart to the car.
words.zh: (動) 轉移；調動

words.d: A transition is a process where there is a change from one form to another.
words.e: The weather gets colder during the transition from summer to autumn.
words.zh: (名) 過渡；轉變

words.d: A transplant is an operation in which a damaged part of one’s body is replaced.
words.e: The sick child needed a heart transplant to live.
words.zh: (動) [træns'plænt] 移植；遷移　(名) ['trænsplænt] 移植；移植器官

words.d: An aquarium is a building where fish and underwater animals are kept.
words.e: We took a trip to the aquarium and saw a scary shark.
words.zh: (名) 水族館；魚缸

words.d: If something is arbitrary, it is not based on any plan or system, so it seems random.
words.e: The classroom had many arbitrary rules that made me confused.
words.zh: (形) 武斷的；任意的　(形) 專斷的；隨意的

words.d: An autobiography is a true story of a person’s life written by that person.
words.e: I read an autobiography about my favorite entertainer.
words.zh: (名) 自傳；自述

words.d: A convention is behavior that is considered to be common or polite.
words.e: In the US, a popular convention is to shake hands when you meet someone.
words.zh: (名) 習俗；慣例　(名) 會議；大會

words.d: If someone is gracious, then they are kind and helpful to those who need it.
words.e: The operator was gracious enough to help me find his number.
words.zh: (形) 親切的；有禮貌的　(形) 優雅的；高貴的

words.d: To improve something means to make it better.
words.e: He studied hard to improve his test scores from the previous year.
words.zh: (動) 改善；改進　(動) 進步；提高

words.d: To insulate something means to protect it from heat, cold, or noise.
words.e: People can conserve energy by insulating their houses.
words.zh: (動) 隔離；使孤立　(動) 絕緣；隔熱

words.d: To intrigue means to cause an interest in something or someone.
words.e: Her mysterious past intrigued her new friend.
words.zh: (動) [In`trig] 引起興趣；吸引　激起好奇心　(名) [`Intrig] 陰謀；詭計

words.d: Longevity is the ability to live for a long time.
words.e: Sea turtles have an amazing longevity.
words.zh: (名) 長壽；長命　(名) 壽命；生命期限

words.d: To misplace something means to lose it.
words.e: I misplaced my wallet, and I didn’t find it until a week later.
words.zh: (動) 放錯地方；遺失　(動) 弄丟；找不到

words.d: When children are naughty, they behave badly or do not do what they are told.
words.e: The boy had to go to his room because he was being naughty.
words.zh: (形) 調皮的；淘氣的　(形) 不聽話的；頑皮的

words.d: A norm is a way of behaving that is considered normal in a particular society.
words.e: Wearing a heavy coat all summer is not considered a norm in the desert.
words.zh: (名) 準則；規範　(名) 常態；標準

words.d: An orangutan is a large ape with red and brown hair and long arms.
words.e: Orangutans use their long arms to swing from trees.
words.zh: 紅毛猩猩是一種大型猿類，擁有紅褐色毛髮和長臂。

words.d: To overload something means to put more things into it than it is meant to hold.
words.e: If you overload the truck, it might crash.
words.zh: (名) 超載；負荷過重　(動) 使超載；使負荷過重

words.d: Philanthropy is the act of helping others, without wanting anything in return.
words.e: The wealthy business owner is well known for his acts of philanthropy.
words.zh: (名) 慈善；慈善事業

words.d: To probe into something means to ask questions to discover facts about it.
words.e: The bank probed into his financial history to see if he qualified for a loan.
words.zh: (動) 探究；探測　(名) 探針；調查

words.d: A recipient of something is the person who receives it.
words.e: I was the recipient of four phone calls today.
words.zh: (名) 接受者；得主

words.d: A reptile is a cold-blooded animal that lays eggs and has skin covered with scales.
words.e: Lizards are my favorite type of reptile.
words.zh: (名) 爬蟲類動物

words.d: To thrive means to do well and be successful, healthy, or strong.
words.e: He may be an old man, but he continues to thrive.
words.zh: (動) 茁壯成長；蓬勃發展

words.d: When something is ultimate, it is the final result or aim of a long series of events.
words.e: By trying hard in school, I will reach my ultimate goal of becoming a doctor.
words.zh: (形) 最終的；極致的

words.d: If something is antique, it is very old and rare, and therefore valuable.
words.e: My grandmother’s antique rocking chair is worth a lot of money.
words.zh: (形) 古老的；古董的　(名) 古董；古物

words.d: An applicant is someone who writes a request to be considered for a job or prize.
words.e: Lots of applicants came into the store when the job position became available.
words.zh: (名) 申請人；求職者　(名) 應試者；考生

words.d: An artifact is an old object made by humans that is historically interesting.
words.e: We studied artifacts from an ancient Chinese settlement.
words.zh: (名) 人造物品；手工製品　(名) 考古遺物；古器物

words.d: When something is authentic, it is not false or a copy of the original.
words.e: We ate authentic Italian food on our vacation to Rome.
words.zh: (形) 真正的；道地的　(形) 可信的；可靠的

words.d: The chronology of a series of past events is when they happened.
words.e: We learned the chronology of World War II in history class.
words.zh: (名) 年代順序；時間順序　(名) 按時間順序排列的歷史事件記錄

words.d: A diplomat is a representative of a country who works with another country.
words.e: The Spanish diplomat discussed trade issues with officials in Peru.
words.zh: (名) 外交官；外交家　(形) 圓滑的；策略性的

words.d: An epic is a long book, poem, or movie about a period of time or a great event.
words.e: The poet wrote an epic about the great discoveries of the past thousand years.
words.zh: (形) 史詩般的；壯大的　(名) 史詩；偉大的事蹟

words.d: An excerpt is a short piece of writing or music taken from a larger piece.
words.e: I didn’t listen to the entire symphony online, but I did play an excerpt.
words.zh: (名) [ˈɛksɝpt] 摘錄；節錄　(動) [ɪkˈsɝpt] 摘取；引用

words.d: A fossil is the hard remains of a prehistoric animal or plant.
words.e: The expert arranged the fossils to build the skeleton of the dinosaur.
words.zh: (名) 化石；遺跡　(形) 陳舊的；過時的

words.d: To humiliate someone means to make them feel ashamed and embarrassed.
words.e: I was humiliated when I tripped and fell down in front of the whole school.
words.zh: (動) 使蒙羞；羞辱　(動) 貶低；使感到羞愧

words.d: When a poem is considered lyric, it is written in a simple and direct style.
words.e: I enjoy reading and creating my own lyric poetry.
words.zh: (名) 歌詞；抒情詩　(形) 抒情的；富有感情的

words.d: Majesty is supreme greatness or authority.
words.e: You should address the king and queen as your majesty.
words.zh: (名) 威嚴；莊嚴　(名) 陛下；皇帝

words.d: The monarch of a country is the king, queen, emperor, or empress.
words.e: The monarch lived in a beautiful palace with a grand gate.
words.zh: (名) 君主；統治者　(名) 帝王蝶

words.d: To precede something means to come before it.
words.e: The hurricane was preceded by a moment of still wind and clear sky.
words.zh: (動) 在...之前發生；領先

words.d: When someone is punctual, they do something or arrive at the right time.
words.e: My mother hates being late. She is the most punctual person I know.
words.zh: (形) 準時的；守時的

words.d: To recruit people means to select them to join or work for an organization.
words.e: We successfully recruited someone to be the new manager.
words.zh: (動) 招募；徵募

words.d: A refund is money given back to a person when an item is returned to a store.
words.e: I asked for a refund because the shoes I bought were too tight.
words.zh: (名) ['riˌfʌnd] 退款　(動) [rɪ'fʌnd] 退還；退款

words.d: A register is an official list or record of people or things.
words.e: At a wedding there is register for all of the guests to sign.
words.zh: (動) 登記；註冊

words.d: Renown is the quality of being well known due to having done good things.
words.e: Michael is a singer of great renown in New Zealand.
words.zh: 聲望因做好事而廣為人知的品質。

words.d: A tusk is a long, curved, pointed tooth of an elephant, boar, or walrus.
words.e: Sadly, some people hunt elephants and remove their tusks to sell them.
words.zh: 長牙是大象、野豬或海象的長而彎曲的尖牙。

words.d: A burden is a serious or difficult responsibility.
words.e: Children who do not behave are a burden to their parents.
words.zh: (名) 負擔；重擔　(動) 使負擔；使承受

words.d: To compromise is to agree to something that is not exactly what you want.
words.e: We both compromised about the game we decided to play.
words.zh: (動) 妥協；折衷　(名) 妥協；讓步

words.d: To craft something is to make it using skill.
words.e: She crafted the bookcase out of solid pine wood and then painted it.
words.zh: (名) 工藝；手工藝　(動) 精心製作；手工製造

words.d: A crook is someone who is not honest or who commits crimes.
words.e: The manager was arrested by the police for being a crook.
words.zh: (名) 騙子；壞人　(名) 彎曲的東西；彎柄　(動) 彎曲；彎起

words.d: Currency is the form of money used in a certain place.
words.e: Elizabeth had to exchange her dollars for foreign currency.
words.zh: (名) 貨幣；通貨　(名) 流通；現行

words.d: An enigma is someone or something that is mysterious or hard to understand.
words.e: The theft of the paintings is an enigma to the investigators.
words.zh: (名) 謎；神秘的人或事

words.d: When people or things are fragile, they are not strong and can be damaged easily.
words.e: The fragile glassware was carefully packed into boxes.
words.zh: (形) 易碎的；脆弱的　(形) 虛弱的；柔弱的

words.d: A hybrid is a mixture of different things or styles.
words.e: In Greek mythology, a centaur is a hybrid of a man and a horse.
words.zh: (名) 混合物；混種動物　(形) 混合的；雜交的

words.d: Innocence is a lack of experience of difficult or complex things in life.
words.e: Everyone who met her found her innocence to be charming.
words.zh: (名) 天真；無辜　(形) 純潔的；無罪的

words.d: To merge two things is to combine them into one whole thing.
words.e: The storm clouds merged into one large menacing cloud that filled the sky.
words.zh: (動) 合併；融合　(名) 合併；融合

words.d: When something is moderate, it is not too big or too small in size or amount.
words.e: It takes a moderate amount of patience to be around small children all day.
words.zh: (形) [ˋmɑdərɪt] 適度的；溫和的　(動) [ˋmɑdəˌret] 減輕；調和

words.d: To overwhelm is to exist in such a large amount that someone cannot deal with it.
words.e: The amount of homework her teacher assigned has overwhelmed her.
words.zh: (動) [ˌovɚˈwɛlm] 壓垮；淹沒　(名) ['ovɚˌwɛlm] 壓倒；征服

words.d: A perception of a situation is a way of thinking about it or understanding it.
words.e: Since he couldn’t see, his perception of life was much different than mine.
words.zh: (名) 知覺；看法

words.d: A reunion is the meeting of people or things that have been separated.
words.e: Every summer we have a family reunion at the lake.
words.zh: (名) 團聚；同學會

words.d: To rig something means to dishonestly arrange it.
words.e: The bad politician rigged the election so that he would win.
words.zh: (動) 操縱；裝配

words.d: A shiver is a shaking movement the body makes when someone is cold or scared.
words.e: I got shivers on my way home because it was so cold.
words.zh: 顫抖是身體因寒冷或恐懼而做出的抖動動作。

words.d: When someone is sociable, they are friendly.
words.e: Many of my good friends are sociable, but I am shy.
words.zh: (形) 好交際的；友善的

words.d: When someone is talkative, they talk a lot.
words.e: My aunt is very talkative whenever she is on the phone.
words.zh: (形) 多話的；愛說話的

words.d: To tow something is to pull it.
words.e: The truck was towing a trailer behind it.
words.zh: (動) 拖；拉；牽引

words.d: To tramp is to put your feet down in a loud, heavy way as you walk.
words.e: The baby tramped across the floor as he was learning to walk.
words.zh: (名) 流浪漢；長途步行　(動) 跋涉；踩踏

words.d: To alleviate pain or suffering means to make it less intense or severe.
words.e: She needed something to alleviate the pain in her back.
words.zh: (動) 減輕；緩解　(動) 舒緩；改善

words.d: Astrology is the study of the stars in the belief that they influence people’s lives.
words.e: Jack, who studies astrology, believes that the stars can predict the future.
words.zh: 占星學是研究星辰，並相信它們影響人們生活的學問。

words.d: To differentiate things or people is to show the difference between them.
words.e: It was hard to differentiate between the identical twins.
words.zh: (動) 區分；辨別　(動) 使不同；區別對待

words.d: To disrupt something or someone is to prevent them from working.
words.e: The loud crash disrupted the class lecture.
words.zh: (動) 破壞；中斷　(動) 擾亂；打擾

words.d: An equation is a math operation to determine the value of something.
words.e: I used the Pythagorean theorem to solve the equation.
words.zh: (名) 方程式；等式　(名) 問題；情況

words.d: To err means to make a mistake.
words.e: The pilot erred in his estimate of the time it would take to make the trip.
words.zh: 犯錯意指犯了錯誤。

words.d: When something is erroneous, it is incorrect or only partly correct.
words.e: The child held the erroneous belief that time machines were real.
words.zh: (形) 錯誤的；不正確的　(形) 謬誤的；荒謬的

words.d: If people or things are frantic, they behave in a wild way because they are frightened.
words.e: The cat became frantic when I tried to give it a bath.
words.zh: (形) 狂亂的；惶恐的　(形) 急促的；匆忙的

words.d: The hull of a boat or tank is the main body of it.
words.e: Afterthe wreckatsea, the ship’s hull was the last part to sink.
words.zh: (名) 船殼；外殼　(動) 去除外殼；剝去

words.d: When an action is inadvertent, it is done without realizing what you are doing.
words.e: She made an inadvertent error when she knocked over the nail polish.
words.zh: 當行為是無意的，意指在未察覺自身行動的情況下完成。

words.d: To improvise something is to do it with whatever is available or without planning.
words.e: There was no meat for the pizza, so we improvised with what was in the fridge.
words.zh: 即興創作某事是指利用現有資源或未經規劃來完成。

words.d: The latitude of a place is its distance from the equator.
words.e: The device was able to tell the traveler his exact latitude.
words.zh: (名) 緯度；自由度；活動範圍

words.d: A mariner is a sailor.
words.e: The old mariner used his telescope to find the shore.
words.zh: (名) 水手；海員　(名) 航海者；船員

words.d: A multitude of things or people is a very large number of them.
words.e: A multitude of people were waiting at the airport.
words.zh: (名) 大量；眾多　(名) 群眾；大批人群

words.d: A nuisance is a person or thing that is annoying or causes a lot of problems.
words.e: The teenager considered her noisy little brothers to be quite a nuisance.
words.zh: (名) 討厭的人或事；麻煩

words.d: The permanence of something is its ability to last forever.
words.e: Poor results will threaten the permanence of the new teaching system.
words.zh: 某事物的永久性是指其能持續存在的能力。

words.d: To revolve around something is to keep it as the main feature or focus.
words.e: My life revolves around sports.
words.zh: (動) 旋轉；繞行；以…為中心

words.d: To soothe means to calm someone who is angry or upset.
