words.e: Chris liked to use many scraps of paper when thinking up ideas.
words.zh: (動) 丟棄；廢棄　(名) 碎片；廢料

words.d: A sector is a part of a country’s economy in a specific type of industry.
words.e: We learned about just a few of the factories within the manufacturing sector.
words.zh: (名) 部門；領域

words.d: To subscribe to something is to agree or to concur with it.
words.e: She subscribed to the view that musical education should be kept in schools.
words.zh: (動) 訂閱；贊同

words.d: To subsist means to have the food, water, and money needed to stay alive.
words.e: Some people are forced to subsist on only a few dollars a month.
words.zh: (動) 勉強維持生計；生存

words.d: To suspend something means to delay or stop it from happening for a while.
words.e: The oil company suspended production until it was sure the factory was safe.
words.zh: (動) 暫停；懸掛

words.d: A synthesis is a combination of different ideas or styles.
words.e: The band’s music was a synthesis of many different musical genres.
words.zh: (名) 合成；綜合

words.d: An acrobat is a person who entertains people by doing amazing physical things.
words.e: There were acrobats at the circus that did impressive and complicated jumps.
words.zh: (名) 特技表演者；雜技演員

words.d: Advocacy is the act of supporting or recommending something.
words.e: Janine’s strong advocacy for the event made people want to attend.
words.zh: 倡導是指支持或推薦某事的行為。

words.d: If something is communal, it involves a group of people.
words.e: On the weekends, Theo and his son picked up trash in the communal park.
words.zh: (形) 共有的；公共的　(形) 集體的；社區的

words.d: If something is fluid, it is smooth and moves gracefully.
words.e: Boris was a famous ballet dancer because people admired his fluid movements.
words.zh: (形) 流動的；流暢的　(名) 流體；液體

words.d: To harmonize means to make different things go well together.
words.e: The sweet and sour flavors of the dish harmonized well and tasted great.
words.zh: 和諧化意指讓不同事物良好地協調共存。

words.d: If someone is industrious, they work hard.
words.e: Dennis was very industrious, so he never had problems finding a job.
words.zh: (形) 勤勞的；勤奮的　(形) 刻苦的；認真的

words.d: When someone is inventive, they are good at creating new things.
words.e: The inventive student built a robot to help her with her chores.
words.zh: (形) 有創意的；富於發明的　(形) 新穎的；獨特的

words.d: If something is judicial, it is related to judges or courts of law.
words.e: The laws changed the way the judicial system was structured.
words.zh: (形) 司法的；法官的　法庭的；審判的

words.d: Mandarin is one of the two main Chinese languages.
words.e: Although John’s parents both spoke Mandarin, he could only speak English.
words.zh: (名) 普通話；國語　(形) 橙黃色的；普通話的

words.d: If something is metropolitan, it relates to a large city.
words.e: All of the metropolitan excitement of New York City made Rufus nervous.
words.zh: (形) 大都市的；首都的　(名) 大都市居民；大主教

words.d: To mimic something means to copy the way it sounds or moves.
words.e: The hunter mimicked a duck’s call and shot the two ducks that called back.
words.zh: (動) 模仿；學...的樣子　(名) 模仿者；學舌者

words.d: When something is misguided, it is based on bad judgment or wrong beliefs.
words.e: The snake bit Molly during her misguided attempt to grab it.
words.zh: (形) 被誤導的；方向錯誤的

words.d: To rehearse means to practice and prepare for a performance in front of people.
words.e: Vick’s band rehearsed all week before their first show.
words.zh: (動) 排練；預演

words.d: To scorn someone means to behave without respect toward them.
words.e: The criminal was scorned by everyone in the community.
words.zh: (名) 輕蔑；鄙視　(動) 輕視；不屑

words.d: If something is sensory, it is related to the senses.
words.e: Iris and Eric preferred the sensory thrill of watching movies in the theater.
words.zh: (形) 感官的；感覺的

words.d: If something is staple, it is standard or basic.
words.e: Hamburgers, steaks and hotdogs are staple foods to cook on a grill.
words.zh: (名) 主食；主要產品　(動) 用釘書機釘住

words.d: A statute is a law that is official and has been written down.
words.e: The new statute made it illegal to walk dogs in the park.
words.zh: (名) 法規；章程

words.d: A veteran is someone who has a lot of experience doing something.
words.e: Hank was the team veteran and had played in more games than anyone else.
words.zh: (名) 老兵；老手；(形) 經驗豐富的

words.d: A villain is an evil person who breaks the law or hurts others.
words.e: The villain escaped with half of my family’s valuables.
words.zh: (名) 反派；惡棍

words.d: A vine is a plant that has long, twisting stems and climbs upward.
words.e: The branches of the trees were covered with vines.
words.zh: (名) 藤蔓；葡萄藤

words.d: To constrain something means to limit its development.
words.e: Jim cannot join us because he is constrained by previous plans.
words.zh: (動) 限制；約束　(動) 強制；迫使

words.d: A depot is a bus or train station.
words.e: He waited for his mother to arrive at the depot.
words.zh: (名) 倉庫；儲存站　(名) 公車站；集散地

words.d: To emulate people means to imitate them because they are greatly admired.
words.e: As a small boy, he always tried to emulate his big brother.
words.zh: (動) 模仿；效法　(動) 競爭；比較

words.d: The forefinger is the finger between one’s thumb and middle finger.
words.e: He shouted, “ There it is!” and pointed with his forefinger.
words.zh: 食指是位於拇指與中指之間的手指。

words.d: The guts are all the organs inside a person or animal.
words.e: The doctor can tell you every process that happens in one’s guts.
words.zh: (名) 勇氣；膽量　(名) 腸子；內臟

words.d: When something is inherent, it is a natural part of something else.
words.e: Sweating is an inherent bodily function when exercising.
words.zh: (形) 內在的；固有的　(形) 天生的；與生俱來的

words.d: To intimidate means to frighten others.
words.e: My dad intimidates my friends whenever they visit.
words.zh: (動) 威脅；恫嚇　(動) 使害怕；使畏縮

words.d: A janitor is a person who makes repairs and takes care of a building.
words.e: The school janitor cleaned up the messy cafeteria.
words.zh: (名) 清潔工；門房

words.d: When something is moist, it is slightly wet.
words.e: The ground is still moist from the rain last night.
words.zh: (形) 潮濕的；濕潤的　(動) 使潮濕；弄濕

words.d: Nope is an informal way of saying “ no.”
words.e: He asked if I had any money, and I had to say, “Nope. ”
words.zh: (副) 不；不是

words.d: To prod means to push someone or something with a finger or pointed object.
words.e: The bully prodded me in the chest with his finger.
words.zh: 戳是指用手指或尖物推某人或某物。

words.d: A ransom is a sum of money paid to a kidnapper to set the person free.
words.e: He kidnapped the prince and demanded $1 million as ransom.
words.zh: (名) 贖金

words.d: To restrain someone or something means to use physical strength to stop them.
words.e: Mike restrained Allen from reaching the door.
words.zh: (動) 克制；限制

words.d: Saliva is the watery liquid in people’s mouths that helps in digestion.
words.e: The baby could not keep the saliva from dripping out of its mouth.
words.zh: (名) 唾液；口水

words.d: To spit means to force liquid from one’s mouth.
words.e: He emerged from the pool and spit water from his mouth.
words.zh: (動) 吐；吐出

words.d: To sprint means to run very fast over a short distance.
words.e: The kids didn’t want to be late to class, so they sprinted to the bus stop.
words.zh: (動) 衝刺；短跑　(名) 衝刺；短跑

words.d: A stunt is something that is done in order to get attention or publicity.
words.e: The man jumped over the cars as a promotional stunt.
words.zh: (名) 特技；噱頭　(動) 阻礙；妨礙

words.d: When people are tolerant, they are approved of because they accept others.
words.e: The tolerant leader thought that everyone was equal regardless of race.
words.zh: (形) 寬容的；容忍的

words.d: A vampire is a fictional monster that sleeps in a coffin and sucks people’s blood.
words.e: The vampire snuck up on the woman and bit her in the neck.
words.zh: 吸血鬼是虛構的怪物，睡在棺材裡並吸食人血。

words.d: To yawn means to open one’s mouth wide and breathe in air.
words.e: The child yawned and stretched her arms before bed for the night.
words.zh: (名) 哈欠　(動) 打哈欠

words.d: An analogy is a connection made between things to show that they are similar.
words.e: The doctor made an analogy between the human heart and a water pump.
words.zh: (名) 類比；比喻　(名) 類似之處；相似點

words.d: A person's ancestry is the series of people from whom a person descended.
words.e: We studied our ancestry and found out that we are related to royalty.
words.zh: (名) 祖先；家族來源　(名) 血統；家世背景

words.d: An archer is a person who uses a bow and arrows.
words.e: The expert archer hit the target every time.
words.zh: (名) 弓箭手；射手　(名) 射手座

words.d: A conspiracy is a secret plan made by a group of people to do something illegal.
words.e: Some people think that there was a conspiracy to kill American president John Kennedy.
words.zh: (名) 陰謀；密謀　(名) 共謀者的集合

words.d: A deputy is the second-highest-ranking person in a business or government.
words.e: While the sheriff was ill, the deputy took over his duties.
words.zh: (名) 副手；代理人　(形) 代理的；副的

words.d: An earl is a nobleman from Britain.
words.e: The Earl of Canterbury lived in a huge mansion.
words.zh: 伯爵是來自英國的貴族。

words.d: When something is fragrant, it has a pleasant smell.
words.e: The girl picked delicious berries and fragrant flowers.
words.zh: (形) 芬芳的；香的　(形) 香氣怡人的；馨香的

words.d: A funnel is an object with a wide top and a narrow bottom for pouring liquids.
words.e: The worker used a funnel when he poured the oil into the engine.
words.zh: 漏斗是一種頂部寬、底部窄的器具，用於傾倒液體。

words.d: If something is hereditary, it is a trait passed onto children from their parents.
words.e: The hereditary disease affected three generations of children.
words.zh: 若某事物為遺傳，則指其為父母傳遞給子女的特質。

words.d: A hymn is a religious song that often praises a god.
words.e: The book was filled with hymns that the worshippers sang.
words.zh: (名) 讚美詩；聖歌

words.d: To invert something means to turn it upside down.
words.e: He inverted the bicycle to make repairs on the wheels.
words.zh: (名) ['ɪnvɝt] 倒置物　(動) [ɪn'vɝt] 翻轉；顛倒；反轉

words.d: Prey is the animals that are hunted and eaten by another creature.
words.e: The lion ran as fast as he could to catch his prey.
words.zh: (名) 獵物；犧牲者　(動) 捕食；折磨

words.d: A procession is a group of people who walk or drive in a line during a public event.
words.e: The procession all celebrated when they reached the end.
words.zh: (名) 隊伍；行列

words.d: A prophet is a person chosen by a god to give the god’s message to people.
words.e: Harold believed that he was one of the prophets of his god.
words.zh: (名) 先知；預言者

words.d: When something is sarcastic, it is the opposite of what is actually meant.
words.e: She hurt her mother’s feelings with her constant sarcastic remarks.
words.zh: (形) 諷刺的；挖苦的

words.d: Seasoning is a mix of salt, herbs, and spices that improves the taste of food.
words.e: This steak tastes bland. Maybe I’ll add some seasoning to it.
words.zh: (名) 調味料；佐料

words.d: Sodium is an element that can be used to make substances such as salt.
words.e: Sodium and chloride combine to make table salt.
words.zh: (名) 鈉

words.d: A tyranny is a cruel and unfair government in which one person has power.
words.e: His tyranny was impossible to live under.
words.zh: (名) 暴政；專制

words.d: A tyrant is someone who treats people in a cruel, harsh, and unfair way.
words.e: Our boss is a tyrant. He makes us work long hours for little pay.
words.zh: (名) 暴君；專制統治者

words.d: Vinegar is a strong liquid made from sour wine that is used in cooking.
words.e: Be careful because too much vinegar will ruin the recipe.
words.zh: (名) 醋

words.d: If something is adjoining, it is next to or joined with a building, or room.
words.e: I couldn’t sleep because the people in the adjoining room were loud.
words.zh: (形) 相鄰的；毗鄰的　(形) 接著的；相連的

words.d: To allege something is to say that it is true without offering proof.
words.e: The little girl had alleged that her older brother hid her favorite doll.
words.zh: (動) 聲稱；宣稱　(動) 指控；控告

words.d: An arch is a curved opening formed under a structure such as a bridge or doorway.
words.e: The arch of the bridge was not high enough for the tall boat to pass underneath.
words.zh: (名) 拱；拱形結構　(動) 弓起；呈拱形　(形) 主要的；狡猾的

words.d: To assemble means to get together in one place.
words.e: The parents assembled to discuss ways to improve their children’s education.
words.zh: (動) 組裝；集合　(動) 聚集；聚合

words.d: A casualty is a person killed or injured in a war or an accident.
words.e: The only casualty in the car accident was a woman who broke her arm.
words.zh: (名) 傷亡者；傷亡　(名) 損失；受害者

words.d: To erect something means to build it.
words.e: The king erected two towers on the north and south sides of his castle.
words.zh: (動) 豎立；建造　(形) 直立的；挺直的

words.d: If something is foul, then it is not pleasant or enjoyable.
words.e: He wouldn’t let his dog drink from the water because it had a foul smell.
words.zh: (形) 骯髒的；污穢的　(形) 違規的；不正當的　(動) 犯規；弄髒

words.d: A hectare is a unit of measure equal to 10,000 square meters.
words.e: His family farm covered many hectares.
words.zh: (名) 公頃（面積單位）

words.d: To heighten an emotion means to increase the intensity of it.
words.e: The pleasant music heightened their enjoyment of the wonderful dinner.
words.zh: (動) 提高；加強　(動) 增強；突出

words.d: Hospitality is friendly behavior and entertainment given to guests or strangers.
words.e: The travelers were amazed at the hospitality given them by the hotel’s staff.
words.zh: (名) 好客；熱情款待　殷勤招待

words.d: A mansion is a large and expensive home.
words.e: The mansion had thirty bedrooms, two kitchens, and a pool.
words.zh: (名) 豪宅；大宅邸

words.d: To outnumber a group means to have a greater number than it.
words.e: The girls outnumbered the boys at the school by four to one.
words.zh: (動) 在數量上超過

words.d: If someone is overjoyed, then they are extremely happy.
words.e: He was overjoyed by the news of his promotion.
words.zh: (形) 非常高興的；狂喜的

words.d: A pasture is an area of land covered with grass for animals to use as food.
words.e: The sheep were taken to a pasture where there was more grass.
words.zh: (名) 牧場；草地　(動) 放牧

words.d: A petition is a written request asking an authority to do something.
words.e: The citizens all signed a petition asking the mayor to repair the sidewalks.
words.zh: (名) 請願書；請願

words.d: To renovate a building means to repair it or build new structures on it.
words.e: The old fire station was renovated into an apartment building.
words.zh: (動) 翻新；修復

words.d: To revise something means to change or update it to make it better.
words.e: When the editor discovered certain facts were wrong, he revised the book.
words.zh: (動) 修訂；修改

words.d: A slab is a large, thick, flat piece of stone, concrete, metal, or wood.
words.e: I looked at various slabs of stone to decorate my house.
words.zh: (名) 厚板；平板

words.d: A terrace is an open area that is connected to a house or an apartment.
words.e: In the afternoons, she liked to sit on the terrace and check her email.
words.zh: (名) 露臺；陽臺

words.d: Turf is a section of grass and the dirt in which it grows.
words.e: After the game, the turf looked ragged.
words.zh: (名) 草皮；地盤　(動) 覆蓋草皮

words.d: If something is analogous to another thing, then it is like it in certain ways.
words.e: The relationship with his teacher was analogous to that of a son and mother.
words.zh: (形) 類似的；相似的　(形) 對應的；平行的

words.d: Binoculars are a device used for seeing things that are far away.
words.e: He could see the ship on the horizon only if he used his binoculars.
words.zh: (名) 雙筒望遠鏡；雙眼鏡

words.d: The bulk of something is its great size.
words.e: The large elephant moved its bulk with legs as strong as tree trunks.
words.zh: (名) 大量；主體　(形) 大宗的；散裝的

words.d: If something comprises something else, it consists of or is made up of it.
words.e: Our school’s football team is mostly comprised with seniors.
words.zh: (動) 組成；構成　包含；包括

words.d: To depict something means to show or portray it, often using art.
words.e: The statue’s face depicted the general’s determination and courage.
words.zh: (動) 描繪；表現　(動) 刻畫；描述

words.d: If something is dual, then it is made up of two parts.
words.e: The room had a dual function. It was a living room, but at night it was a bedroom.
words.zh: (形) 雙重的；二元的　(名) 雙打；雙人賽

words.d: Fahrenheit temperature is a scale where water freezes at 32° and boils at 212°.
words.e: When the temperature dropped to 32° Fahrenheit, it started snowing.
words.zh: (名) 華氏溫標；華氏度數

words.d: To fulfill something means to achieve or finish it.
words.e: The professor did not fulfill his promise not to miss a single class.
words.zh: (動) 完成；滿足　(動) 實現；達成

words.d: A grove is a small group of trees.
words.e: All the trees in this grove are apple trees.
words.zh: (名) 小樹林；果園

words.d: Ore is the raw form of rock or material from which a valuable metal is taken.
words.e: The factory melted the ore and used it to make iron products.
words.zh: (名) 礦石；礦　(名) 礦物資源

words.d: The outback is the wild inland region of Australia where very few people live.
words.e: Many Australian farmers use the outback to raise cattle.
words.zh: 內陸地區是指澳洲人煙稀少的荒野內陸地帶。

words.d: To outweigh something means to exceed it in value, amount, or importance.
words.e: Finding a warm place to sleep outweighed the need to find something to eat.
words.zh: (動) 超過；重於

words.d: A paradox is a true statement or real event that seems illogical.
words.e: The paradox of her work was that the less she worked, the more she got done.
words.zh: (名) 矛盾；悖論

words.d: A pier is a structure that extends into a body of water.
words.e: If you want to catch bigger fish, then go to the far end of the pier.
words.zh: (名) 碼頭；橋墩

words.d: A shortcut is a route that is shorter than the main route.
words.e: We got to the house first because we took a shortcut through the forest.
words.zh: (名) 捷徑；近路

words.d: A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports.
words.e: Our tariff on imported wool makes foreign wool more expensive.
words.zh: (名) 關稅

words.d: A thermometer is a device that measures temperature.
words.e: The thermometer outside the window indicated that it was a hot day today.
words.zh: (名) 體溫計；溫度計

words.d: To tilt something means to tip it into a sloping position.
words.e: She tilted her glass and almost spilled some of the wine inside.
words.zh: (動) 傾斜；使傾斜

words.d: If a statement is vice versa, then its two main ideas are switched with one another.
words.e: Students learn from their teachers, and vice versa.
words.zh: 如果一個陳述是「反之亦然」，則其兩個主要觀點互相對調。

words.d: The whereabouts of someone or something is the place where they are.
words.e: The police looked for the lost dog, but its whereabouts were still unknown.
words.zh: (名) 下落；行蹤

words.d: Amber is a hard, yellowish material that comes from trees.
words.e: The necklace was made from pieces of amber.
words.zh: 琥珀是一種來自樹木的堅硬黃色物質。

words.d: Charcoal is a black material that is used as fuel for fire.
words.e: There was burnt charcoal left on the ground from an old fire.
words.zh: (名) 木炭；炭　(形) 炭的；深灰色的

words.d: A columnist is a writer who creates articles about a particular subject.
words.e: The columnist won praise for his articles about life on the farm.
words.zh: (名) 專欄作家；評論員

words.d: When someone is courteous, they are polite and respectful.
words.e: The mother asked her family to be courteous to their guests.
words.zh: (形) 有禮貌的；恭敬的　(形) 周到的；殷勤的

words.d: Credentials are the proof of someone’s experience or ability to do something.
words.e: The mechanic hung his credentials on his office wall.
words.zh: 資格證明是一個人經驗或能力的證明。

words.d: A cricket is an insect that makes loud noises, usually at night.
words.e: In the country, we could see the stars and hear the crickets at night.
words.zh: (名) 蟋蟀；板球

words.d: A delta is flat area where a river splits into smaller rivers that flow into the sea.
words.e: The archeologists found many ancient materials in the delta of the Nile River.
words.zh: 三角洲是一條河流分流成多條較小河川流入海洋的平坦區域。

words.d: Detergent is soap that is used to clean clothes or dishes.
words.e: My brother added too much detergent to the wash, and bubbles got all over the floor.
words.zh: (名) 洗滌劑；清潔劑　(形) 去污的；清潔的

words.d: A euphemism is a term that is used in place of a mean or unpleasant word.
words.e: “Window maintenance officer” is a euphemism for a window washer.
words.zh: 委婉語是用來替代刻薄或令人不悅詞語的用詞。

words.d: To expire is to no longer be effective because its use has come to an end.
words.e: The man’s driver’s license was going to expire in two days.
words.zh: (動) 期滿；終止　失效；到期　(動) 呼氣；斷氣

words.d: Granite is a very hard type of rock that is often black or pink.
words.e: The kitchen counter was made of granite.
words.zh: 花崗岩是一種非常堅硬的岩石，通常呈黑色或粉紅色。

words.d: Gravel is a combination of small stones mixed with sand.
words.e: The ground around the swing set was covered with gravel.
words.zh: 礫石是混合了沙子的細小石子。

words.d: To haunt is to cause problems or negative thoughts over a long period of time.
words.e: His thoughts about his scary dream haunted him for weeks.
words.zh: (動) 縈繞；困擾　(動) 經常出沒於

words.d: When someone is liberal, they accept different ideas and people.
words.e: My grandparents aren’t as liberal as my parents.
words.zh: (形) 自由的；開放的　(形) 寬宏的；慷慨的

words.d: A maze is a system of paths that is complicated and easy to get lost in.
words.e: We got lost in the maze in the garden.
words.zh: 迷宮是一套複雜且容易讓人迷路的路徑系統。

words.d: Moss is a small green or yellow plant that grows on wet dirt, rocks, or tree trunks.
words.e: Be careful not to slip on the moss that’s covering those rocks.
words.zh: (名) 苔蘚；青苔

words.d: A pebble is a small, round stone.
words.e: We threw pebbles into the lake to make the water splash.
words.zh: (名) 小圓石；鵝卵石

words.d: When a bird pecks, it bites or hits something with its beak.
words.e: The birds pecked at the seeds on the ground.
words.zh: (動) 啄；啄食

words.d: A reservoir is a place for storing water for a town to use.
words.e: If it doesn’t rain soon, the reservoir is going to dry up completely.
words.zh: (名) 水庫；儲藏所

words.d: A streak is a long, thin mark that is easy to see.
words.e: Her hair was brown except for a streak which she dyed blonde.
words.zh: (名) 條紋；一陣子；連勝　(動) 飛奔；加上條紋
